  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Advertising - Version 1'
}

Table campaign.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising campaign. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser or client who owns this campaign.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Every advertising campaign executes a brand strategy and must comply with brand guidelines, tone, safety requirements, and use approved assets. Campaign planning, creative development, and trafficking']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Campaigns are executed for specific client brands—fundamental relationship for brand-level performance reporting, budget tracking, share-of-voice analysis, and brand portfolio management. Domain exper']
  positioning_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_positioning. Business justification: Campaigns execute specific brand positioning strategies with defined target audiences, messaging frameworks, and competitive differentiation. Campaign briefs reference positioning statements, and succ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Campaigns often contract directly with suppliers (data providers, verification vendors, measurement platforms) for campaign-level services. Essential for vendor cost allocation, performance tracking, ']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the campaign in the review workflow: pending, approved, rejected, or revision requested.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the campaign for activation.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign was officially approved for execution.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model used to credit conversions for the campaign: last click, first click, linear, time decay, position-based, or data-driven.']
  brand_safety_level varchar [note: 'Brand safety filtering level applied to the campaign: standard, moderate, strict, or custom. Determines content exclusions.']
  brief_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document link to the original campaign brief or statement of work (SOW).']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the campaign. Overall spending ceiling across all channels and placements.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code for the campaign, often used in trafficking systems and insertion orders.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the advertising campaign used for identification and reporting.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign: draft (planning), approved (ready), active (running), paused (temporarily stopped), completed (finished), or cancelled (terminated).']
  campaign_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance or priority tier of the campaign within the advertisers portfolio.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign based on its strategic objective: brand awareness, direct response, performance, retention, seasonal, or always-on.']
  conversion_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after an ad interaction during which a conversion can be attributed to the campaign.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system. Audit field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the campaign budget and financial metrics.']
  device_targeting varchar [note: 'Device types targeted by the campaign. Comma-separated list such as desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV, OTT.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign is scheduled to end. Flight end date. Nullable for always-on campaigns.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times an ad should be shown to the same user within the frequency cap window.']
  frequency_cap_window varchar [note: 'Time window for the frequency cap: per hour, day, week, month, or entire campaign duration.']
  geo_targeting varchar [note: 'Geographic targeting parameters for the campaign. Comma-separated list of countries, regions, cities, or postal codes.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign went live and started serving ads. May differ from the planned start date.']
  iab_category varchar [note: 'IAB content taxonomy category for the campaign, used for brand safety and contextual targeting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the campaign for internal reference and collaboration.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective or goal of the campaign, such as increase brand awareness, drive conversions, generate leads, or boost engagement.']
  pacing_strategy varchar [note: 'Budget pacing strategy for the campaign: even (spread evenly), ASAP (spend as fast as possible), custom (user-defined), or dayparting (time-of-day based).']
  primary_kpi varchar [note: 'The main metric used to measure campaign success, such as CTR, CPA, ROAS, impressions, conversions, or reach.']
  secondary_kpis varchar [note: 'Additional metrics tracked for campaign performance evaluation. Comma-separated list of KPIs.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign is scheduled to begin running. Flight start date.']
  target_audience_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the target audience for the campaign, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioral characteristics.']
  target_cpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per acquisition or conversion for the campaign. Maximum acceptable cost to acquire one customer or conversion.']
  target_cpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per thousand impressions for the campaign. Used for brand awareness and reach campaigns.']
  target_ctr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target click-through rate percentage for the campaign. Represents the expected ratio of clicks to impressions.']
  target_roas decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target return on ad spend ratio for the campaign. Represents revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising.']
  viewability_standard varchar [note: 'Viewability measurement standard applied to the campaign: MRC (Media Rating Council), GroupM, or custom threshold.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of who created the campaign record. Audit field.']

  Note: 'Master record for an advertising campaign — the authoritative SSOT for campaign identity, objectives, KPIs, flight dates, status, and high-level strategy. Captures campaign name, advertiser/client reference, campaign type (brand awareness, direct response, performance, retention, seasonal, always-on), campaign status (draft, approved, active, paused, completed, cancelled), start and end dates, campaign objective, primary KPI, secondary KPIs, target ROAS, target CPA, target CPM, target CTR, overall budget ceiling, currency, campaign tier, IAB category, campaign brief reference, approval status, approved by, approved date, go-live date, and audit timestamps. Serves as the root entity for all downstream campaign execution, trafficking, and performance data.'
}

Table campaign.flight {
  flight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign flight. Primary key.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Flights are tactical execution windows within campaigns that deliver brand messaging. Creative rotation, audience targeting, and channel mix decisions require brand guideline compliance and brand safe']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign under which this flight executes. Links this flight to its overarching campaign strategy.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Flights may contract flight-specific suppliers for verification, brand safety, or measurement services. Required for flight-level vendor cost allocation, reconciliation, and performance evaluation in ']
  audience_segments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or structured description of audience segments targeted in this flight (e.g., demographic, behavioral, contextual, or custom segments from DMP/CDP).']
  budget_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budget allocated to this flight, representing the financial envelope for all media buys, creative production, and execution costs within this burst period.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the flight budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or structured description of the media channels included in this flight (e.g., \'Display, Video, Social, OOH\'). Defines the multi-channel execution strategy for this burst.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this flight record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  creative_rotation varchar [note: 'The strategy for rotating multiple creatives within this flight (even = equal distribution, weighted = custom weights, optimized = algorithm-driven, sequential = fixed order).']
  daypart_restrictions varchar [note: 'Time-of-day or day-of-week restrictions for ad delivery within this flight (e.g., \'Weekdays 6AM-10PM\', \'Prime Time Only\'). Enables dayparting strategies to optimize audience reach.']
  device_targeting varchar [note: 'Device types targeted for ad delivery in this flight (e.g., \'Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, CTV\'). Enables cross-device or device-specific strategies.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this flight concludes execution. Marks the end of the time-bounded burst window.']
  flight_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for this flight, used in trafficking systems, insertion orders, and billing reconciliation.']
  flight_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this flight, often indicating the burst period, theme, or seasonal focus (e.g., \'Holiday Wave 1\', \'Back-to-School Burst\').']
  flight_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flight. Indicates whether the flight is in planning, actively running, paused, or concluded.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single user should see an ad from this flight within a defined time window. Prevents ad fatigue and optimizes budget efficiency.']
  frequency_cap_period varchar [note: 'The time window over which the frequency cap is enforced (e.g., per day, per week, for the entire flight duration).']
  geo_targeting varchar [note: 'Geographic targeting parameters for this flight, specifying countries, regions, DMAs, postal codes, or custom geo-fences where ads should be delivered.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this flight record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this flight record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this flight, capturing special instructions, strategic rationale, or operational considerations.']
  objective varchar [note: 'The primary marketing objective for this flight. May override or refine the parent campaign objective for this specific burst period.']
  pacing_goal varchar [note: 'The metric by which pacing is measured and controlled (e.g., impressions delivered, clicks generated, conversions achieved, unique reach, or spend amount).']
  pacing_type varchar [note: 'The budget pacing strategy for this flight. Determines how spend is distributed across the flight duration (even = uniform daily spend, front_loaded = higher spend at start, back_loaded = higher spend at end, custom = user-defined schedule).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this flight relative to other flights in the same campaign or portfolio. Higher values indicate higher priority for budget allocation and inventory access.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when this flight begins execution. Marks the start of the time-bounded burst window.']
  target_frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned average number of times each unique user should be exposed to the ad during this flight. Used to balance reach and repetition.']
  target_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned Gross Rating Points for this flight, representing the sum of all rating points delivered. Standard metric for traditional media planning.']
  target_impressions varchar [note: 'The planned number of ad impressions to be delivered during this flight. Used for pacing and performance tracking.']
  target_reach varchar [note: 'The planned number of unique individuals or households to be reached during this flight. Key metric for awareness campaigns.']
  target_trp decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned Target Rating Points for this flight, representing GRP adjusted for the specific target audience. Used for audience-specific media planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this flight record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Represents a discrete time-bounded execution window within a campaign — a campaign flight (also called a campaign burst or wave). Captures flight name, parent campaign reference, flight start date, flight end date, flight status, flight budget allocation, flight objective override, pacing type (even, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom), pacing goal, daypart restrictions, flight priority, channel mix for this flight, and notes. Enables multi-flight campaign structures where a single campaign runs in sequential or overlapping bursts with distinct budgets and targeting parameters. Aligns with Mediaocean Prisma flight-level planning constructs.'
}

Table campaign.line_item {
  line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the line item. Primary key for the line item execution unit within a campaign flight.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Line items are media placements that deliver brand creative. Trafficking teams verify brand safety tier compliance, viewability standards, and creative approval status against brand guidelines before ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign under which this line item executes. Enables rollup reporting and budget tracking at campaign level.']
  contract_insertion_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insertion order that authorizes this line item buy. Links media execution to contractual commitment.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign flight that contains this line item. Establishes the hierarchical relationship between flight and line item.']
  programmatic_deal_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for a programmatic deal (PMP, preferred deal, or programmatic guaranteed) associated with this line item. Nullable for open exchange buys.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Line items frequently use suppliers beyond publishers (ad verification, measurement partners, data providers). Critical for line-item-level vendor reconciliation, cost attribution, and compliance trac']
  trafficking_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Line items are trafficked to ad servers via trafficking orders. Currently no FK exists to track which trafficking order activated a specific line item. This is essential for operational tracking and a']
  vendor_publisher_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media vendor or publisher delivering this line item inventory. Links to vendor master for billing and performance tracking.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'The creative format specification for this line item (e.g., 300x250 banner, 15-second pre-roll, sponsored post). Defines the deliverable unit.']
  agency_commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used to calculate net from gross cost.']
  booked_clicks varchar [note: 'The contracted number of clicks to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for click-based buys.']
  booked_conversions varchar [note: 'The contracted number of conversions or acquisitions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for performance-based buys.']
  booked_impressions varchar [note: 'The contracted number of impressions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for impression-based buys.']
  booked_views varchar [note: 'The contracted number of video views or viewable impressions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for video and viewability-based buys.']
  brand_safety_tier varchar [note: 'The level of brand safety filtering applied to this line item. Determines which content categories and contexts are excluded.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The media channel through which this line item will be executed. Determines platform, format options, and measurement methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|search|social|ooh|dooh|ctv|ott|audio|native|email|affiliate — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this line item record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  creative_assignment_status varchar [note: 'The status of creative asset assignment and approval for this line item. Indicates whether creatives are ready for serving.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values on this line item. Enables multi-currency campaign management.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'The current delivery performance status of the line item. Indicates whether the line item is meeting, exceeding, or falling short of delivery goals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|delivering|under_delivering|over_delivering|completed|paused|stopped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this line item stops serving. Defines the end of the delivery window. Nullable for evergreen line items.']
  frequency_cap_impressions varchar [note: 'The maximum number of impressions a unique user can receive for this line item within the frequency cap period. Used to control ad exposure.']
  frequency_cap_period varchar [note: 'The time period over which the frequency cap is applied. Defines the window for impression counting.']
  gross_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross media cost for this line item before agency commission. Represents the total amount billed to the client.']
  is_programmatic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is executed through programmatic buying platforms (DSP) or through direct/manual insertion orders.']
  line_item_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or SKU for the line item. Used for trafficking, billing reconciliation, and cross-system reference.']
  line_item_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the line item. Typically includes targeting, format, and channel descriptors for operational clarity.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this line item within the parent flight. Used for display ordering and operational sequencing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this line item record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  net_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net media cost for this line item after agency commission and discounts. Used for internal P&L and margin analysis.']
  pacing_status varchar [note: 'The current pacing health of the line item relative to its delivery goal and timeline. Used for proactive delivery management.']
  pacing_strategy varchar [note: 'The delivery pacing strategy for this line item. Determines how impressions are distributed across the flight period.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The buying model or inventory type for this line item. Indicates whether inventory is reserved, auction-based, or programmatic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guaranteed|non-guaranteed|programmatic_guaranteed|preferred_deal|private_auction|open_exchange|direct — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'The delivery priority of this line item relative to other line items competing for the same inventory. Higher values indicate higher priority.']
  rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit rate for this line item based on the rate type. For CPM, this is cost per thousand impressions; for CPC, cost per click; for flat, total cost.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'The pricing model for this line item. Determines how cost is calculated based on delivery metrics (Cost Per Mille, Cost Per Click, Cost Per Acquisition, Cost Per View, flat fee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|cpv|cpe|flat|cpcv|vcpm — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when this line item begins serving. Defines the start of the delivery window.']
  targeting_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of audience targeting parameters applied to this line item (demographics, geography, behavior, context). Detailed targeting stored in related tables.']
  trafficking_status varchar [note: 'The operational status of line item setup and creative trafficking. Indicates readiness for delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|ready_to_serve|live|paused|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  viewability_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target percentage of impressions that must meet viewability standards (MRC definition: 50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).']

  Note: 'Granular execution unit within a campaign flight representing a single buyable unit of media — the atomic record of what is being bought, at what price, on which channel, for which audience. Captures line item name, parent flight reference, campaign reference, channel type (display, video, search, social, OOH, CTV, audio), ad format, placement type, targeting summary, start date, end date, booked impressions, booked clicks, booked views, rate type (CPM, CPC, CPA, CPV, flat), rate amount, net cost, gross cost, currency, IO reference, vendor/publisher reference, trafficking status, delivery status, pacing status, and creative assignment status. The primary unit of media planning and buying execution. Aligns with Google Campaign Manager 360 placement and The Trade Desk ad group constructs.'
}

Table campaign.ad {
  ad_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ad unit. Primary key.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Ads are executable brand creative units. QA teams verify brand guideline compliance (logo usage, color palette, tone), brand safety requirements, and regulatory compliance flags (GDPR, CCPA) against b']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign to which this ad belongs.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset (image, video, HTML5, audio) used in this ad execution.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign line item under which this ad is trafficked.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Individual ads may use specific suppliers for dynamic creative optimization, ad serving, or verification services. Required for ad-level vendor cost tracking, performance attribution, and troubleshoot']
  trafficking_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Ads are trafficked to ad servers via trafficking orders. Currently no FK exists to track which trafficking order activated a specific ad. This is essential for operational tracking (when was this ad t']
  ad_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to this ad unit for identification and reporting purposes.']
  ad_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad unit. Governs whether the ad is eligible for serving. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|rejected|live|paused|archived|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ad_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ad unit by format type. Determines rendering and trafficking requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: banner|video|native|rich_media|audio|dooh|ctv|display|interstitial — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the ad was approved for trafficking and serving.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the ad for serving.']
  brand_safety_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad creative has passed brand safety verification checks.']
  ccpa_opt_out_honored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ad honors CCPA Do Not Sell requests.']
  click_through_url varchar [note: 'Destination URL where users are directed when they click on the ad. May include tracking macros.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the ad stops serving. Null indicates no end date.']
  format varchar [note: 'Dimensions of the ad creative in width x height format (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, 1920x1080). Null for non-display formats.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this ad can be shown to a unique user within the frequency cap period.']
  frequency_cap_period varchar [note: 'Time window over which the frequency cap is enforced.']
  gdpr_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether user consent is required under GDPR before serving this ad.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the ad becomes eligible to start serving.']
  impression_tracker_url varchar [note: 'Third-party tracking pixel or beacon URL fired when the ad impression is served.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'Final destination landing page URL after click-through, excluding tracking parameters.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified the ad record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the ad unit for internal reference and collaboration.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Serving priority of this ad relative to other ads in the same line item. Higher values indicate higher priority.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the ad was rejected during review. Null if not rejected.']
  server_ad_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the ad server platform (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360) for this ad unit.']
  ssl_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad creative and all tracking tags are served over HTTPS for secure delivery.']
  third_party_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether third-party tracking pixels and beacons are enabled for this ad.']
  trafficking_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating whether the ad has been trafficked to the ad server and its current serving state.']
  vast_tag_url varchar [note: 'VAST or VPAID tag URL for video ad serving. Null for non-video ad types.']
  view_tracker_url varchar [note: 'Third-party tracking URL fired when the ad is viewable according to MRC viewability standards.']
  vpaid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad uses VPAID for interactive video ad execution.']
  weight varchar [note: 'Relative weight for rotation when multiple ads are eligible to serve. Used for A/B testing and creative rotation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the ad record.']

  Note: 'Individual ad unit record representing a specific creative execution trafficked and served within a campaign line item. Captures ad name, parent line item reference, campaign reference, ad type (banner, video, native, rich media, audio, DOOH), ad format dimensions (width x height), VAST/VPAID tag reference, click-through URL, impression tracker URL, view tracker URL, creative asset reference, ad status (draft, pending review, approved, rejected, live, paused, archived), trafficking status, ad server ad ID (CM360), go-live date, end date, frequency cap, frequency cap period, and audit timestamps. Bridges the campaign execution layer to the creative asset layer. Aligns with Google Campaign Manager 360 ad trafficking entities.'
}

Table campaign.targeting_rule {
  targeting_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the targeting rule. Primary key.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audience segment when targeting dimension is audience_segment, retargeting, or lookalike.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the parent entity (campaign, flight, or line_item) to which this targeting rule applies.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: targeting_rule has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to campaign, flight, or line_item. Currently only has campaign_id FK. Adding flight_id (nullable) to support flight-level target']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: targeting_rule has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to campaign, flight, or line_item. Currently only has campaign_id FK. Adding line_item_id (nullable) to support line_item-level ']
  positioning_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_positioning. Business justification: Targeting rules implement audience definitions from brand positioning strategy. Target audience segments, geographic scope, and messaging framework from positioning documents drive targeting configura']
  brand_safety_tier varchar [note: 'The brand safety standard applied: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) certified, Global Alliance for Responsible Media (GARM) standard, or custom.']
  browser_type varchar [note: 'The web browser targeted (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this targeting rule respects CCPA opt-out requests (Do Not Sell My Personal Information).']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'The type of network connection targeted: WiFi, cellular, ethernet, or unknown.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this targeting rule record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The origin of the targeting data: first-party (owned), second-party (partner), third-party (purchased), Data Management Platform (DMP), or Customer Data Platform (CDP).']
  daypart_schedule varchar [note: 'The time-of-day or day-of-week schedule for ad delivery, specified in a structured format (e.g., JSON or cron-like expression).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device targeted: desktop, mobile, tablet, Connected TV (CTV), Over-the-Top (OTT), or wearable.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this targeting rule expires and is no longer applied. Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this targeting rule becomes active and begins to be applied.']
  fraud_prevention_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum fraud detection score (0-100) required for ad delivery, used to filter out suspected invalid traffic.']
  frequency_cap_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times an ad can be shown to a user within the specified frequency cap window.']
  frequency_cap_unit varchar [note: 'The time unit for the frequency cap window: hour, day, week, month, or lifetime.']
  frequency_cap_window varchar [note: 'The time window (in the unit specified by frequency_cap_unit) over which the frequency cap limit applies.']
  gdpr_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR user consent is required for this targeting rule to be applied.']
  geo_level varchar [note: 'The granularity of geographic targeting: country, region/state, Designated Market Area (DMA), city, or postal code.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this targeting rule is currently active and being applied to ad delivery.']
  language varchar [note: 'The language targeted, specified as ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, fr).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier that last modified this targeting rule.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this targeting rule record was last modified.']
  os_type varchar [note: 'The operating system targeted (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, tvOS).']
  parent_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of entity to which this targeting rule is applied: campaign, flight, or line_item.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority or precedence of this targeting rule when multiple rules apply to the same entity. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  recency_window varchar [note: 'The time window (in days) for behavioral or retargeting data recency, specifying how recent user activity must be to qualify for targeting.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name for the targeting rule, used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  targeting_dimension varchar [note: 'The dimension or category of targeting applied by this rule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: geography|demographic|behavioral|contextual|keyword|device|daypart|audience_segment|retargeting|lookalike|brand_safety_content_exclusion|brand_safety_keyword_block|brand_safety_domain_block|brand_safety_category_exclusion|frequency_cap|viewability|fraud_prevention — promote to reference product]']
  targeting_operator varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the targeting rule includes or excludes the specified targeting value.']
  targeting_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific value or identifier for the targeting dimension (e.g., country code, age range, device type, audience segment ID).']
  targeting_value_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the format of the targeting value: single value, range, comma-separated list, or pattern/regex.']
  viewability_standard varchar [note: 'The viewability measurement standard applied: Media Rating Council (MRC), Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB), or custom.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier that created this targeting rule.']

  Note: 'Defines the audience, contextual, and brand safety targeting parameters applied to a campaign, flight, or line item. Captures targeting rule name, parent entity type (campaign/flight/line_item), parent entity reference, targeting dimension (geography, demographic, behavioral, contextual, keyword, device, daypart, audience segment, retargeting, lookalike, brand_safety_content_exclusion, brand_safety_keyword_block, brand_safety_domain_block, brand_safety_category_exclusion), targeting operator (include/exclude), targeting value, targeting value type, data source (first-party, second-party, third-party, DMP, CDP), audience segment reference, geo level (country, region, DMA, city, postal code), device type, OS type, browser type, connection type, language, daypart schedule, frequency cap limit, frequency cap window, frequency cap unit, brand safety tier (TAG certified, GARM standard), viewability standard, fraud prevention threshold, GDPR consent required flag, CCPA opt-out flag, recency window, and is_active flag. Enables precise audience targeting governance and brand safety enforcement across programmatic and direct channels.'
}

Table campaign.budget_allocation {
  budget_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget allocation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  budget_optimization_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.budget_optimization_scenario. Business justification: Planning-to-execution traceability: budget_allocations implement recommendations from budget_optimization_scenarios (MMM-driven channel mix, attribution-informed spend allocation). Linking allocation ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign for which budget is being allocated.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent flight within the campaign for which budget is allocated.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item receiving this budget allocation.']
  pacing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.pacing_rule. Business justification: budget_allocation currently has pacing_rule_name (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Should normalize to FK relationship with pacing_rule.pacing_rule_id. This enables joining to get full pacin']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount spent to date against this budget allocation, based on delivered impressions and clicks.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to distribute budget across periods or channels (e.g., percentage-based, fixed amount, dynamic optimization).']
  allocation_reference varchar [note: 'Business-facing reference number or code for this budget allocation, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this budget allocation (e.g., draft, active, paused, completed, cancelled).']
  auto_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated optimization algorithms are enabled to adjust bids and budgets dynamically based on performance.']
  budget_period_type varchar [note: 'The time period granularity for which this budget allocation applies (e.g., monthly, weekly, flight-level).']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'The origin or authority for this budget allocation (e.g., client-approved, agency-estimated, internal forecast).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The advertising channel or media type for which this budget is allocated. CTV (Connected TV), OTT (Over-the-Top), DOOH (Digital Out-of-Home), OOH (Out-of-Home). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|social|search|audio|native|ctv|ott|dooh|ooh|print|radio|tv — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  committed_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of budget that has been committed through insertion orders or programmatic deals but not yet spent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this allocation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daily_impression_goal varchar [note: 'The target number of impressions to be delivered per day under the pacing rule.']
  daily_spend_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount that can be spent per day for this allocation, enforced by the pacing rule.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation expires and delivery must stop. Null for open-ended allocations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation becomes active and delivery can begin.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget allocation record was last updated or modified.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when this budget allocation was last reconciled against actual spend and delivery data from ad servers and billing systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this budget allocation, including rationale for revisions, special instructions, or reconciliation findings.']
  optimization_algorithm_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific optimization algorithm or strategy applied to this allocation (e.g., CPA optimization, ROAS maximization).']
  over_delivery_action varchar [note: 'The action to take when delivery exceeds the pacing goal or budget cap (e.g., pause campaign, throttle bids, send alert).']
  over_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage over-delivery before corrective action is taken (e.g., 5.00 means 5% over-delivery is tolerated).']
  pacing_alert_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage deviation from the pacing goal that triggers an alert to campaign managers (e.g., 10.00 means alert at 10% deviation).']
  pacing_check_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently the pacing engine evaluates delivery performance and adjusts bid strategies (e.g., real-time, hourly, daily).']
  pacing_type varchar [note: 'The pacing strategy governing how budget and impressions are delivered over time (e.g., even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve).']
  planned_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original planned budget amount allocated for this period and channel, as approved in the initial media plan.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the budget reconciliation process for this allocation (e.g., pending, reconciled, discrepancy found, approved).']
  remaining_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unspent budget available for this allocation, calculated as revised (or planned) budget minus actual spend and committed spend.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The revised budget amount after any adjustments, reallocations, or change orders. Null if no revision has occurred.']
  under_delivery_action varchar [note: 'The action to take when delivery falls short of the pacing goal (e.g., boost bids, increase budget allocation, send alert).']
  under_delivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage under-delivery before corrective action is taken (e.g., 5.00 means 5% under-delivery is tolerated).']
  weekly_impression_goal varchar [note: 'The target number of impressions to be delivered per week under the pacing rule.']
  weekly_spend_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount that can be spent per week for this allocation, enforced by the pacing rule.']

  Note: 'Unified financial and delivery control record governing how campaign budget is distributed AND how delivery is paced across flights, line items, channels, and time periods. Captures allocation reference, parent campaign reference, parent flight reference, line item reference, channel type, budget period (monthly, weekly, flight), planned budget amount, revised budget amount, committed spend, actual spend to date, remaining budget, currency, budget source (client-approved, agency-estimated), allocation method (percentage, fixed, dynamic), pacing rule name, pacing type (even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve), daily impression goal, daily spend cap, weekly impression goal, weekly spend cap, pacing check frequency (hourly, daily), over-delivery action (pause, throttle, alert), under-delivery action (boost, alert), pacing alert threshold percentage, over-delivery tolerance percentage, under-delivery tolerance percentage, auto-optimization enabled flag, optimization algorithm reference, effective date range, last reconciliation date, and reconciliation status. Serves as the single SSOT for budget allocation decisions, delivery pacing strategy, and pacing guardrails — practitioners manage budget and pacing as a unified workflow because pacing cannot be set independently of budget constraints. Subsumes all pacing rule definitions previously modeled separately.'
}

Table campaign.pacing_rule {
  pacing_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pacing rule record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign entity to which this pacing rule applies.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent flight entity to which this pacing rule applies, if applicable.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent line item entity to which this pacing rule applies, if applicable.']
  optimization_algorithm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the optimization algorithm or model used for auto-pacing adjustments, if applicable.']
  primary_pacing_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this pacing rule record.']
  auto_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated optimization algorithms are enabled to adjust pacing dynamically based on performance data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pacing rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this pacing rule (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  custom_curve_definition varchar [note: 'JSON or structured definition of a custom pacing curve when pacing_type is set to custom_curve. Defines delivery distribution over time.']
  daily_impression_goal varchar [note: 'Target number of impressions to be delivered per day under this pacing rule.']
  daily_spend_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum spend allowed per day under this pacing rule, expressed in the campaign currency.']
  dayparting_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dayparting (time-of-day targeting) is applied as part of the pacing strategy.']
  dayparting_schedule varchar [note: 'Structured definition of the dayparting schedule (e.g., JSON or cron-like format) specifying hours and days when delivery is allowed or prioritized.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this pacing rule ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended rule.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this pacing rule becomes active and begins governing delivery.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pacing rule record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this pacing rule, used for documentation and operational context.']
  over_delivery_action varchar [note: 'The action to be taken when delivery exceeds the pacing goal or cap (e.g., pause delivery, throttle impressions, send alert).']
  pacing_alert_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage deviation from the pacing goal that triggers an alert to campaign managers (e.g., 10.00 means alert if delivery is 10% off target).']
  pacing_check_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the pacing engine evaluates delivery progress against goals and applies throttling or boosting actions.']
  pacing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pacing rule indicating whether it is actively governing delivery.']
  pacing_type varchar [note: 'The delivery pacing strategy applied to the campaign or line item. Defines how impressions or spend are distributed over the flight period.']
  parent_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of parent entity to which this pacing rule is attached (e.g., campaign, flight, line item, insertion order).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority level for this pacing rule when multiple rules apply to the same entity. Higher values indicate higher priority.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the pacing rule, used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for interpreting pacing schedules and dayparting rules.']
  under_delivery_action varchar [note: 'The action to be taken when delivery falls short of the pacing goal (e.g., boost bid, send alert, reallocate budget).']
  weekly_impression_goal varchar [note: 'Target number of impressions to be delivered per week under this pacing rule.']
  weekly_spend_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum spend allowed per week under this pacing rule, expressed in the campaign currency.']

  Note: 'Defines the delivery pacing strategy and guardrails for a campaign, flight, or line item. Captures pacing rule name, parent entity type, parent entity reference, pacing type (even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve), daily impression goal, daily spend cap, weekly impression goal, weekly spend cap, pacing check frequency (hourly, daily), over-delivery action (pause, throttle, alert), under-delivery action (boost, alert), pacing alert threshold percentage, auto-optimization enabled flag, optimization algorithm reference, and effective date range. Governs how campaign delivery is distributed over time to meet KPIs and avoid over/under-spend.'
}

Table campaign.trafficking_order {
  trafficking_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trafficking order. Primary key for the trafficking order entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign for which this trafficking order is issued.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent flight or media schedule within the campaign that this trafficking order activates.']
  parent_trafficking_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original trafficking order if this is a revision or amendment, enabling tracking of the full order history and change lineage.']
  primary_trafficking_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or ad operations specialist who submitted the trafficking order to the ad server platform.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Trafficking orders involve third-party ad servers or trafficking platforms (suppliers). Essential for tracking which ad server/platform was used, critical for troubleshooting, billing, and vendor perf']
  actual_live_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the trafficking order went live and ads began serving, as confirmed by the ad server platform.']
  ad_server_order_code varchar [note: 'External order or campaign identifier assigned by the ad server platform (e.g., CM360 order ID, Trade Desk campaign ID) for cross-system reconciliation.']
  ad_server_platform varchar [note: 'The ad serving or demand-side platform (DSP) to which this trafficking order is submitted for campaign activation and delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Google Campaign Manager 360|The Trade Desk|DV360|Amazon DSP|Xandr|MediaMath|Adobe Advertising Cloud|Sizmek|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ads_trafficked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual ad creatives that were trafficked and uploaded to the ad server as part of this trafficking order.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trafficking order was marked as completed, indicating that all line items and ads are live and the operational handoff is finalized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trafficking order record was first created in the system, marking the initiation of the trafficking workflow.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the trafficked campaign line items and ads are expected to begin serving live impressions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trafficking order record was most recently updated, tracking the latest change to any field in the order.']
  line_items_trafficked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of campaign line items that were trafficked and configured in the ad server as part of this trafficking order.']
  pixel_firing_validation_status varchar [note: 'Validation status of tracking pixels and conversion tags, confirming that impression, click, and conversion pixels are firing correctly in test environments.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to this trafficking order, indicating urgency and sequencing for ad operations team workload management.']
  qa_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality assurance review was completed and the trafficking order was approved or rejected for go-live.']
  qa_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality assurance review process for this trafficking order, indicating whether pre-launch validation checks have been completed and passed.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided when a trafficking order is rejected during QA or by the ad server platform, including specific issues that must be resolved before resubmission.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking revisions and resubmissions of the trafficking order due to changes, corrections, or rejections.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the trafficking order was completed within the defined SLA target hours, used for operational performance tracking.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours within which the trafficking order should be completed from submission to go-live, as defined by internal SLA policies.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trafficking order was submitted to the ad server or DSP for processing and activation.']
  trafficking_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and instructions provided by the trafficking team regarding special setup requirements, targeting parameters, creative specifications, or platform-specific configurations.']
  trafficking_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique trafficking order number used for operational reference and communication with ad ops teams and external platforms.']
  trafficking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trafficking order indicating its progress through the ad operations workflow from submission to go-live. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|submitted|in_progress|live|completed|rejected|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trafficking_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trafficking order based on the campaign buying model and activation method (standard direct IO, programmatic guaranteed, sponsorship, etc.).']
  vast_tag_validation_status varchar [note: 'Validation status of VAST tags for video ad creatives, indicating whether the tags conform to IAB VAST specifications and are ready for serving.']

  Note: 'Represents the formal trafficking instruction set issued to an ad server or DSP to activate campaign line items and ads. Captures trafficking order number, parent campaign reference, parent flight reference, ad server platform (CM360, The Trade Desk, etc.), trafficking status (pending, submitted, in-progress, live, completed, rejected), submitted by, submitted date, go-live date, trafficking notes, QA status, QA completed by, QA completed date, number of line items trafficked, number of ads trafficked, VAST tag validation status, pixel firing validation status, and audit timestamps. Represents the operational handoff from planning to execution — a distinct business workflow from the campaign plan itself.'
}

Table campaign.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each campaign status transition record. Primary key for the campaign status history event log.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval record if this status change required formal approval. Links to approval workflow for governance tracking.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campaign that experienced this status transition. Links this history record to the campaign master entity.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flights have flight_status attribute with transitions throughout their lifecycle. status_history is described as tracking status transitions for campaigns, but should also track flight status changes.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line items have multiple status attributes (trafficking_status, delivery_status, pacing_status) with transitions throughout their lifecycle. status_history should track line_item status changes. Addin']
  trafficking_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Trafficking orders have multiple status attributes (trafficking_status, qa_status) with transitions throughout their lifecycle. status_history should track trafficking order status changes. Adding tra']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated the status change. Null for system-initiated transitions. Critical for accountability and audit trails.']
  workflow_step_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the workflow step or stage that triggered this transition. Used for process mining and workflow optimization.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition required formal approval according to campaign governance rules.']
  changed_by_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system that triggered the status change. Populated for automated and system-initiated transitions.']
  changed_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who initiated the status change. Denormalized for reporting convenience and historical preservation.']
  client_visible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition should be visible to the client in external reporting and dashboards.']
  compliance_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required compliance review has been completed for this status transition.']
  compliance_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition requires compliance or legal review according to regulatory requirements.']
  duration_in_previous_status_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'The number of hours the campaign spent in the previous status before this transition. Calculated field for lifecycle analytics.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date when the new status became effective. May differ from transition timestamp for scheduled or backdated changes.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the status change was initiated. Captured for security audit and fraud detection purposes.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The status of the campaign after this transition. Captures the to-state in the status lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|scheduled|active|paused|completed|cancelled|archived — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the status transition. Captures contextual information for future reference.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated notifications were sent to stakeholders about this status change.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when stakeholder notifications were sent for this status transition. Null if no notifications were sent.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status of the campaign immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the from-state in the status lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|scheduled|active|paused|completed|cancelled|archived — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this history record was created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  rollback_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition was later rolled back or reversed. Used for tracking corrections and error recovery.']
  rollback_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this status transition was rolled back. Provides context for error analysis and process improvement.']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this status transition was rolled back. Null if the transition was not rolled back.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'The user session identifier during which the status change occurred. Links to session logs for detailed audit trails.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that recorded this status transition. Critical for data lineage and multi-system reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this status transition record in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to source.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the status change. Used for categorization and reporting of transition patterns.']
  transition_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of why the status transition occurred. Provides context for audit and client reporting purposes.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the campaign status transition occurred. Primary event timestamp for this history record.']
  transition_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism that triggered the status change. Distinguishes between user-initiated, system-triggered, and automated workflow transitions.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string for the session that initiated the status change. Used for security and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all status transitions for a campaign throughout its lifecycle. Captures history record ID, campaign reference, previous status, new status, transition reason, transition type (manual, automated, system), changed by user, changed by system, change timestamp, effective date, approval reference (if status change required approval), notes, and source system. Enables full lifecycle traceability for campaign governance, client reporting, and regulatory compliance. Distinct from the campaign master record — this is the event-sourced history of state changes.'
}

Table campaign.campaign_kpi_target {
  campaign_kpi_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign KPI target record. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campaign when parent_entity_type is \'campaign\'. Null for flight or line_item parent types.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight when parent_entity_type is \'flight\'. Null for campaign or line_item parent types.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the line item when parent_entity_type is \'line_item\'. Null for campaign or flight parent types.']
  positioning_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_positioning. Business justification: Campaign KPI targets measure performance against brand positioning objectives. Awareness lift, sentiment improvement, and NPS targets derive from brand positioning baselines. Campaign success is evalu']
  target_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: KPI targets are frequently set for specific audience segments (e.g., \'achieve 8% conversion rate among in-market auto intenders segment\'). Performance reporting and optimization decisions require meas']
  alert_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage deviation from target that triggers a performance alert. Used for automated monitoring and optimization workflows.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The baseline or minimum acceptable value for this KPI. Used to establish performance thresholds below which the campaign is considered underperforming.']
  benchmark_source varchar [note: 'The source of industry benchmark data used to establish this target (e.g., Nielsen norms, IAB benchmarks, historical campaign data).']
  benchmark_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The industry or historical benchmark value for this KPI type. Used for comparative performance analysis.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'The media channel or platform for which this KPI target applies (e.g., display, video, social, search). Allows for channel-specific targets within multi-channel campaigns.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'The level of confidence in achieving this target based on historical performance, market conditions, and campaign parameters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this KPI target record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for monetary KPI targets (CPA, CPM, CPC, ROAS). Required when target_unit is \'currency\'.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date through which this KPI target remains active. Null indicates the target is active through the end of the parent entity\'s duration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this KPI target becomes active and should be measured. Allows for phased KPI introduction or mid-campaign target changes.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market for which this KPI target applies. Enables market-specific performance targets.']
  is_primary_kpi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary KPI for the parent entity. True if this is the main success metric; false for secondary or supporting KPIs.']
  kpi_type varchar [note: 'The specific KPI metric being targeted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: impressions|clicks|ctr|conversions|cpa|cpm|cpc|roas|vtr|grp|trp|sov|reach|frequency|nps|viewability|completion_rate|engagement_rate — promote to reference product]']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology used to measure and calculate this KPI. Includes attribution model, measurement window, deduplication rules, and any custom calculation logic.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'The system or platform that will provide the actual measurement data for this KPI. Determines the source of truth for performance reporting.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user or system that last modified this KPI target record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this KPI target record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, context, or special instructions related to this KPI target. Captures nuances not covered by structured fields.']
  optimization_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level for optimization efforts when this KPI is underperforming. Guides resource allocation for campaign management.']
  parent_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of parent entity to which this KPI target is attached. Determines the scope of the KPI measurement (campaign-level, flight-level, or line-item-level).']
  reporting_cadence varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this KPI should be measured and reported. Determines the monitoring and optimization cycle for this metric.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the most recent revision to this KPI target. Captures why the target was changed mid-campaign.']
  stretch_target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The aspirational or stretch goal value for this KPI. Represents exceptional performance beyond the standard target.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'The specific audience segment for which this KPI target applies. Allows for segment-specific performance targets within a single campaign.']
  target_period varchar [note: 'The time period over which the target should be achieved. \'Total\' indicates the target is for the entire campaign/flight/line_item duration.']
  target_revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number tracking revisions to this KPI target. Increments each time the target is modified during campaign execution.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI target. Tracks whether the target is in planning, actively being measured, or has been completed or cancelled.']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the target_value. Defines how the target should be interpreted (absolute count, percentage, currency amount, or rating point).']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric target value for the KPI. Interpretation depends on kpi_type and target_unit (e.g., 1000000 impressions, 2.5% CTR, $50 CPA).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user or system that created this KPI target record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific, measurable KPI targets set for a campaign, flight, or line item at the time of planning and as revised during execution. Captures KPI target ID, parent entity type (campaign/flight/line_item), parent entity reference, KPI type (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, CPA, CPM, CPC, ROAS, VTR, GRP, TRP, SOV, reach, frequency, NPS), target value, target unit, target period (total, daily, weekly, monthly), baseline value, stretch target value, measurement methodology, measurement source (Nielsen, Comscore, CM360, GA, custom), reporting cadence, is_primary_kpi flag, and effective date range. Enables structured KPI governance and performance benchmarking across all campaign types.'
}

Table campaign.campaign_brief {
  campaign_brief_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign brief record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (advertiser) who commissioned this campaign brief.']
  author_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.worker. Business justification: Campaign briefs are authored documents requiring clear ownership for version control, approval routing, and accountability in agency workflows. Agency_lead_name exists but is denormalized text; proper']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific brand that this campaign brief is promoting.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign that this brief initiates and governs.']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Campaign briefs specify which client brand the campaign serves—critical for brand guidelines compliance, brand-level budget allocation, creative approval workflows, and ensuring brand strategy alignme']
  competitive_intelligence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.competitive_intelligence. Business justification: Strategic planning dependency: campaign briefs are informed by competitive intelligence studies (market positioning, competitor SOV, creative strategies). Linking brief to the specific competitive_int']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Campaign briefs are created under the scope of a Statement of Work (SOW). Currently sow_reference_number is stored as a string. Normalizing to sow_id FK enables joining to SOW budget, deliverables, mi']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Campaign briefs often specify client-mandated or preferred suppliers (measurement vendors, brand safety tools, verification partners). Required for ensuring compliance with client vendor requirements ']
  agency_lead_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the agency lead responsible for this campaign brief.']
  agency_lead_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the agency account lead or strategist responsible for this campaign brief.']
  approved_by_client_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has formally approved this brief version (True) or not (False).']
  brief_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the campaign brief, used in client communications and internal tracking.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign brief indicating its approval and usage state.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget envelope amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  budget_envelope_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocation or spending cap approved for the campaign as specified in the brief.']
  channel_preferences varchar [note: 'Preferred or mandated media channels and platforms for campaign execution (e.g., digital, TV, OOH, social, CTV).']
  client_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the client formally approved this campaign brief version.']
  client_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the client representative who provided formal approval for this brief.']
  competitive_context varchar [note: 'Analysis of the competitive landscape, including key competitors, their messaging, and market positioning relevant to this campaign.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign brief record was first created in the system.']
  creative_format_requirements varchar [note: 'Specification of required creative formats and deliverables (e.g., video lengths, display ad sizes, social media formats).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic markets or regions where the campaign will be executed (e.g., USA, EMEA, APAC, specific countries or cities).']
  issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign brief was formally issued to the agency team for execution.']
  key_message varchar [note: 'Primary message or value proposition that the campaign must communicate to the target audience.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages in which campaign creative and messaging must be produced and delivered.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign brief record was last updated or modified.']
  mandatory_exclusions varchar [note: 'List of prohibited elements, themes, imagery, or messaging that must not appear in campaign creative due to brand, legal, or ethical constraints.']
  mandatory_inclusions varchar [note: 'List of required elements, messages, disclaimers, logos, or legal copy that must appear in all campaign creative.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Special regulatory, legal, or compliance considerations that must be observed in campaign execution (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, industry-specific advertising restrictions).']
  revision_history_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or pointer to the detailed revision history log for this brief, tracking all changes across versions.']
  success_criteria varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the qualitative and quantitative metrics that will define campaign success (e.g., brand lift, engagement rate, conversion targets).']
  target_audience_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the intended audience for the campaign, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioral characteristics.']
  timeline_end_date date [note: 'Target date by which the campaign must be fully executed and delivered as specified in the brief.']
  timeline_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date by which campaign planning and creative development must commence as specified in the brief.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise, descriptive title of the campaign brief that summarizes the campaign intent.']
  tone_of_voice varchar [note: 'Prescribed communication style and personality that creative assets must embody (e.g., professional, playful, authoritative, empathetic).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the brief document, incremented with each revision (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0).']

  Note: 'Structured creative and strategic brief document that initiates a campaign — the formal client-approved mandate that drives all downstream planning, creative development, and media buying. Captures brief ID, campaign reference, client reference, brand reference, brief title, brief version, brief status (draft, in-review, approved, superseded), campaign objective narrative, target audience description, key message, tone of voice, mandatory inclusions, mandatory exclusions, competitive context, budget envelope, timeline requirements, channel preferences, success criteria narrative, approved by client, client approval date, agency lead, brief issued date, and revision history reference. The SSOT for campaign intent — distinct from the campaign master record which captures execution parameters.'
}

Table campaign.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign approval record. Primary key.']
  approval_assigned_reviewer_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user assigned to review and decide on the approval request.']
  approval_created_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created this approval record.']
  approval_escalated_to_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the higher-authority user to whom the approval was escalated. Populated only when escalation_flag is True.']
  approval_modified_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified this approval record.']
  approval_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initiated the approval request.']
  campaign_brief_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign_brief. Business justification: Campaign briefs require client approval before campaign execution (campaign_brief has approved_by_client_flag, client_approval_date, client_approver_name). approval.parent_entity_type can include camp']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the parent entity (campaign, media plan, IO, etc.) that requires approval.']
  flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: approval has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to various entities including flights. Currently only has campaign_id FK. Flights have flight_status and require approval workflows be']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: approval has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to various entities including line items. Line items have trafficking_status and delivery_status requiring approval workflows. Adding ']
  trafficking_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Trafficking orders require QA and approval workflows before going live (trafficking_order has qa_status, qa_completed_timestamp). approval.parent_entity_type can include trafficking orders. Adding tra']
  amount_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold (in campaign currency) that triggered this approval requirement. Used for budget change and IO execution approvals.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval request: pending (awaiting decision), approved (authorized to proceed), rejected (denied), escalated (sent to higher authority), or withdrawn (request cancelled).']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'The category of approval being requested: campaign launch, budget change, creative approval, IO (Insertion Order) execution, media plan sign-off, or compliance review.']
  assigned_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user assigned to review the approval request.']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'The organizational authority tier required for this approval (e.g., Manager, Director, VP, C-Level, Board).']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory or industry compliance framework governing this approval (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, IAB Standards, FTC Guidelines, Brand Safety Standards). Populated primarily for compliance_review approval types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approval record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approval amount threshold (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  decision_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reviewer made their approval or rejection decision.']
  decision_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or rationale provided by the reviewer explaining their approval or rejection decision.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to supporting documentation attached to the approval request (e.g., revised media plan, updated creative brief, budget justification memo).']
  escalated_to_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the higher-authority user to whom the approval was escalated.']
  escalation_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the approval request was escalated to a higher authority.']
