  office_location varchar [note: 'The physical office or geographic location where this organizational unit is primarily based (e.g., \'New York – Hudson Yards\', \'London – Soho\', \'Singapore – Marina Bay\'). Supports regional headcount and capacity reporting.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the organizational unit\'s mandate, scope of services, and primary business functions within the agency (e.g., \'Responsible for all programmatic media buying and DSP/SSP management for North American clients\').']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO profit center code assigned to this organizational unit. Enables revenue and cost attribution for P&L reporting at the business unit level, supporting QBR financial analysis.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region classification for the organizational unit. Used for regional performance reporting, media planning alignment, and multi-market campaign resource allocation.']
  reorganization_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent organizational restructuring event affecting this unit (e.g., merger with another department, split into sub-units, reclassification of discipline). Supports change management and audit trails.']
  shared_services_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit operates as a shared services center providing support across multiple client accounts or practice groups (e.g., centralized analytics, production services, finance operations).']
  sow_ownership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is designated as the primary owner and delivery accountable party for client Statements of Work (SOWs). Used in account management and contract attribution.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The IANA time zone identifier for the primary operating time zone of this organizational unit (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for scheduling, resource planning, and cross-market campaign coordination.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the organizational unit across agency systems. Used in financial reporting, headcount roll-ups, and resource planning integrations (e.g., Workfront, SAP S/4HANA CO).']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the organizational unit as defined in Workday HCM (e.g., \'Creative Studio – North America\', \'Programmatic Media Buying\', \'Analytics & Audience Insights\').']
  unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. \'active\' indicates the unit is operational; \'dissolved\' indicates it has been disbanded; \'restructuring\' indicates it is undergoing reorganization.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the organizational unit into its structural category within the agency hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: department|practice_group|discipline|cost_center|office|team|division — promote to reference product]']
  workday_org_unit_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier assigned to this organizational unit within Workday HCM organizational management module. Used for system-of-record cross-referencing and integration.']
  workfront_group_reference varchar [note: 'The corresponding group or team identifier in Workfront project management and resource planning. Used to synchronize capacity planning, resource allocation, and WIP tracking between Workday HCM and Workfront.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy units within the agency — including practice groups, departments, disciplines (Creative, Media, Strategy, Analytics, PR, Production), cost centers, office locations, and reporting lines. Provides the structural context for headcount, capacity, and financial roll-ups. Sourced from Workday HCM organizational management.'
}

Table talent.guild_membership {
  guild_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guild membership record. Primary key.']
  renewed_guild_membership_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on guild_membership (renewed_guild_membership_id)']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (worker) who holds this guild membership. Links to the talent_profile or worker entity.']
  annual_dues_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual membership dues amount required for this membership tier.']
  availability_restriction varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any availability restrictions imposed by union rules (e.g., mandatory rest periods, maximum consecutive work days, blackout dates).']
  booking_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is currently eligible for booking based on their guild membership status and compliance (True/False). Critical for production compliance.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding compliance requirements, special conditions, or exceptions related to this guild membership.']
  contract_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Code or reference number of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or master contract that governs this membership (e.g., SAG-AFTRA Commercials Contract 2022).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild membership record was first created in the system.']
  dues_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for membership dues (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP for UK unions).']
  dues_status varchar [note: 'Current status of membership dues payment obligations (e.g., current, overdue, delinquent, exempt for honorary members).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the membership benefits and obligations become active and enforceable.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Specific product or brand categories subject to exclusivity restrictions (e.g., automotive, beverage, financial services). Null if no category-specific restrictions apply.']
  exclusivity_restriction varchar [note: 'Level of exclusivity restrictions imposed by union rules (e.g., none, partial exclusivity for certain product categories, full exclusivity).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the membership expires or requires renewal. Null for lifetime memberships.']
  health_benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this membership tier qualifies the talent for union-sponsored health insurance benefits (True/False).']
  join_date date [note: 'Date when the talent officially joined or was admitted to the union or guild.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code representing the primary jurisdiction of the union or guild (e.g., USA for SAG-AFTRA, CAN for ACTRA, GBR for BECTU).']
  last_dues_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent membership dues payment received by the union.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild membership record was last modified or updated.']
  local_chapter_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the local chapter, branch, or regional office of the union to which this membership is affiliated.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'The externally-issued membership number or identifier assigned by the union or guild (e.g., SAG-AFTRA membership card number).']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guild membership (e.g., active, inactive, suspended due to non-payment, lapsed, pending approval).']
  membership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the membership level or tier within the union (e.g., full member, associate member, honorary member, lifetime member).']
  minimum_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'Reference to the union minimum rate schedule or scale that applies to this membership (e.g., SAG-AFTRA Scale 2023, DGA Minimum Basic Agreement rates).']
  next_dues_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next membership dues payment is due.']
  pension_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this membership tier qualifies the talent for union pension plan contributions (True/False).']
  residual_payment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this membership entitles the talent to residual payments for reuse of their work (True/False).']
  signatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent can only work with union signatory production companies (True) or can work with non-signatory companies (False).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the membership was terminated, withdrawn, or cancelled. Null for active memberships.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the union or guild affiliation (e.g., SAG-AFTRA for Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists, DGA for Directors Guild of America). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG-AFTRA|ACTRA|BECTU|DGA|WGA|IATSE|AEA|AFM|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the union or guild organization (e.g., Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the membership credentials were last verified with the union or guild.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal verification of the membership credentials (e.g., verified with union, pending verification, unverified, expired credentials).']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal user or system that performed the membership verification.']
  work_permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a special work permit or clearance is required from the union for certain types of engagements (True/False).']

  Note: 'Union and guild membership records for talent — including SAG-AFTRA, ACTRA, BECTU, DGA, and other entertainment/advertising industry unions. Captures membership ID, union affiliation, membership status, dues payment status, signatory requirements, residual payment obligations, and exclusivity/availability restrictions imposed by union rules. Critical for production compliance and talent booking eligibility.'
}

Table talent.talent_assignment {
  talent_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for talent_assignment']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to project.initiative identifying the project to which the worker is assigned.']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to talent.worker identifying the workforce member assigned to this initiative.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The cumulative actual hours worked by this worker on this initiative, typically sourced from time tracking systems (Workfront, Monday.com, or timesheets). Updated continuously as time is logged.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the worker\'s total capacity allocated to this initiative (e.g., 50.00 means half-time). Used for resource utilization tracking and capacity planning. Sum of all concurrent allocations for a worker should not exceed 100% in healthy resource plans.']
  assignment_code varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each worker-to-initiative assignment record. Primary key.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this assignment. Planned = forecasted but not yet started; Active = worker currently engaged; On Hold = temporarily paused; Completed = assignment finished; Cancelled = assignment terminated before completion.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this worker\'s time on this initiative is billable to the client (true) or non-billable/internal (false). Drives revenue recognition and client invoicing. May differ from initiative.is_billable if specific roles are non-billable on an otherwise billable project.']
  billing_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The hourly or daily rate at which this worker\'s time is billed to the client for this specific initiative. May differ from the worker\'s standard pay rate and can vary by project, client, or contract terms. Used for revenue calculation and client invoicing.']
  cost_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The internal cost rate for this worker on this initiative, used for project profitability analysis and margin calculation. Typically derived from the worker\'s pay_rate plus burden (benefits, overhead allocation). May be assignment-specific if special compensation applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this assignment record was created in the source system or data platform.']
  end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when the worker\'s assignment to this initiative ends. May be null for ongoing assignments. Used for resource forecasting and assignment lifecycle management.']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours planned or budgeted for this worker on this initiative. Used for capacity planning, budget estimation, and variance analysis against actual hours.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of this assignment relative to the worker\'s other concurrent assignments. Used for resource conflict resolution and workload balancing when a worker is over-allocated.']
  role varchar [note: 'The specific role or function the worker performs on this initiative (e.g., Creative Director, Account Manager, Designer, Media Planner). This is assignment-specific and may differ from the worker\'s job title—a Senior Designer may serve as Lead Designer on one project and Contributing Designer on another.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique assignment or resource allocation identifier from the originating project management system (Workfront, Monday.com). Used for data lineage and system reconciliation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the worker\'s assignment to this initiative begins. Used for resource planning, availability tracking, and billing period determination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this assignment record was last modified in the source system or data platform.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of agency workforce members to project initiatives. It captures the operational reality of resource allocation, tracking which workers are assigned to which projects, in what capacity, for what duration, and at what rates. Each record links one worker to one initiative with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific assignment relationship—role, dates, hours, allocation percentage, and billing/cost rates.. Existence Justification: In advertising agency operations, workers are routinely assigned to multiple concurrent initiatives (an account director may oversee 5 campaigns simultaneously, a designer may contribute to 3 projects), and initiatives require multiple workers across creative, account, and production teams. The detection reasoning explicitly states that \'the assignment relationship tracks role, dates, hours, rates, and billability—all relationship-specific data\' and notes that the business calls this \'project assignment\' or \'resource assignment.\' This is a textbook operational M:N relationship where the assignment itself is a managed business entity.'
}

Table talent.representation {
  representation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the representation agreement record. Primary key for the association.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier (talent agency or management firm) providing representation']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the talent profile being represented']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The commission percentage charged by the supplier agency for bookings made under this representation agreement, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 for 15%). Varies by supplier-talent combination based on negotiated terms.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier for the legal representation contract document stored in the contract management system or document repository.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the representation agreement record was created in the data platform.']
  end_date date [note: 'The termination or expiry date of the representation agreement. Null indicates an open-ended or currently active agreement. Used for historical tracking and commission cutoff.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the exclusivity terms of the representation agreement. Values: exclusive (sole representation across all markets), non_exclusive (talent can have multiple agencies), category_exclusive (exclusive within specific industry verticals), territory_exclusive (exclusive within defined geographic territories).']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the primary or preferred agency contact for the talent when multiple non-exclusive representation agreements exist. Used to route initial booking inquiries and campaign briefs.']
  representation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the representation agreement. Values: active (currently in force), suspended (temporarily inactive), terminated (ended), pending (signed but not yet effective). Only active agreements are used for commission calculation and booking workflow.']
  start_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the representation agreement. Used to determine active representation periods for talent booking and commission calculation.']
  territory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers defining the geographic scope of the representation agreement (e.g., USA|CAN|MEX for North America). Used to determine which agency to contact for bookings in specific markets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the representation agreement record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual representation relationship between talent profiles and supplier agencies in the advertising ecosystem. It captures the commercial terms, territorial scope, and lifecycle of representation agreements. Each record links one talent_profile to one supplier (talent agency, management firm) with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific representation contract, including commission structures, exclusivity terms, territory restrictions, and representation periods.. Existence Justification: In the advertising industry, talent (actors, influencers, models, directors, creatives) are commonly represented by multiple agencies simultaneously for different geographic territories, media types, or under non-exclusive arrangements. Conversely, each talent agency or management firm (supplier) represents many talent profiles. The representation relationship is a contractual business entity with specific commercial terms (commission rates, exclusivity scope, territory restrictions) that vary by each talent-agency pairing and cannot be stored on either entity alone.'
}

Table talent.payroll_run {
  payroll_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_run']
  correction_of_payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_run (correction_of_payroll_run_id)']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary cost center to which payroll expenses from this run are allocated for management accounting.']
  created_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated or created this payroll run.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (company, subsidiary, or branch) under which this payroll run is executed for tax and compliance purposes.']
  modified_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this payroll run record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or organizational unit for which this payroll run is executed, used for segmentation and reporting.']
  original_payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payroll run that this run reverses or corrects, populated only when is_reversal is true.']
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll calendar that governs the schedule and timing rules for this payroll run.']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically HR manager or payroll administrator) who approved this payroll run for processing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was approved for processing.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch identifier assigned by the payroll processing system for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which payroll amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees, contractors, freelancers, and talent included in this payroll run.']
  employer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total employer-side tax obligations for this payroll run including employer portion of social security, Medicare, unemployment taxes, and other statutory contributions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this payroll run is attributed.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this payroll run is attributed for financial reporting and tax compliance purposes.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring schedule frequency for this payroll run: weekly, biweekly (every two weeks), semimonthly (twice per month), monthly, quarterly, or annual.']
  general_ledger_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which payroll expenses and liabilities from this run are posted to the general ledger for financial accounting.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross compensation amount for all employees and talent included in this payroll run before any deductions.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payroll run is a reversal or correction of a previous payroll run.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run record was last modified or updated.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net compensation amount disbursed to employees and talent after all deductions, representing the actual payment amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this payroll run, including special circumstances, adjustments, or processing instructions.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which payments from this payroll run are scheduled to be disbursed to employees, contractors, and talent.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment disbursement method used for this payroll run: direct deposit (ACH), physical check, wire transfer, payroll card, or cash.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run completed processing and payments were initiated.']
  processing_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the payroll processing system or platform used to execute this payroll run (e.g., Workday HCM, ADP, internal system).']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payroll run, typically formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD or similar convention used for external reference and reporting.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run indicating the purpose: regular scheduled payroll, off-cycle payment, bonus distribution, commission payout, adjustment run, or final settlement.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll run indicating its workflow state from draft through completion or cancellation.']
  submitted_to_bank_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when payment instructions were transmitted to the banking institution for disbursement.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary tax jurisdiction or geographic region governing tax withholding and reporting requirements for this payroll run (e.g., state, province, country).']
  total_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all deductions applied in this payroll run including taxes, benefits, garnishments, and other withholdings.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}

Table talent.review_cycle {
  created_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this review cycle record.']
  modified_by_user_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this review cycle record.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the review form template used for this cycle.']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for administering and managing this review cycle (typically HR Business Partner or Talent Manager).']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for completing calibration sessions to normalize ratings across teams.']
  completed_reviews_count varchar [note: 'Number of reviews that have been completed and finalized in this cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the review cycle (e.g., \'RC-2024-Q1\', \'ANN-2024\').']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the review cycle (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Performance Review\', \'2024 Annual Review\').']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review cycle based on frequency and purpose.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the review cycle purpose, scope, and objectives.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the review cycle officially ends and is closed.']
  feedback_delivery_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which managers must deliver final review feedback to employees.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter to which this review cycle belongs, if applicable.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this review cycle belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  includes_compensation_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review cycle includes compensation adjustments (salary, bonus).']
  includes_promotion_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review cycle includes promotion considerations.']
  manager_review_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which managers must complete employee performance reviews.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review cycle record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional administrative notes or comments about the review cycle.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated email/system notifications are enabled for this review cycle.']
  rating_scale_type varchar [note: 'Type of rating scale used for this cycle (e.g., \'5-point scale\', \'exceeds/meets/below\', \'numeric 1-10\').']
  requires_calibration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether calibration sessions are required to normalize ratings across the organization.']
  requires_peer_feedback boolean [note: 'Indicates whether peer feedback collection is required for this review cycle.']
  requires_self_assessment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees are required to complete a self-assessment as part of this cycle.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance period being evaluated (distinct from cycle end date).']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance period being evaluated (distinct from cycle start date).']
  self_review_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which employees must complete their self-assessment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the review cycle officially begins and becomes active.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review cycle.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Classification of which talent segments are included in this review cycle.']
  total_participants_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees/talent included in this review cycle.']
  workday_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review cycle is integrated with Workday Human Capital Management (HCM) system.']
  workfront_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review cycle is integrated with Workfront resource management system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_cycle. Referenced by review_cycle_id.'
}

Table talent.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for job_profile']
  parent_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_profile (parent_job_profile_id)']
  reports_to_profile_id varchar [note: 'Job profile identifier of the direct supervisor role in the organizational hierarchy.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this job profile for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile was approved for use in the organization.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for capacity planning and resource allocation for this job profile.']
  category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the job profile indicating the type of talent resource.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Primary organizational department or division to which this job profile belongs (e.g., Creative, Strategy, Media, Production).']
  description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the job profile including key responsibilities, scope of work, and expected deliverables.']
  education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for this job profile (e.g., Bachelor\'s Degree in Marketing, Portfolio-based, No formal requirement).']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 job category for regulatory reporting and diversity analytics.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile is no longer active. Null for open-ended profiles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile becomes active and available for use in hiring and resource planning.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'FLSA classification indicating whether the job profile is exempt or non-exempt from overtime regulations.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broader grouping of related job profiles for career pathing and compensation planning (e.g., Creative, Account Management, Strategy, Production, Media).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the job profile within the organization indicating seniority and responsibility.']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for this job profile.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was last modified.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether resources in this job profile are eligible for overtime compensation.']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Additional desirable skills that enhance candidate suitability but are not mandatory for the role.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the job profile used in HR systems and resource planning tools.']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the standard rate indicating how the rate is applied (e.g., per hour, per day, per project).']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile can be performed remotely or requires on-site presence.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Core competencies and technical skills required for this job profile, used for talent matching and capacity planning.']
  standard_capacity_per_period decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard available capacity per planning period (e.g., 40 hours per week, 160 hours per month) for resource planning.']
  standard_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard billing or compensation rate for this job profile used in resource planning and project budgeting. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified in rate_currency.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile indicating whether it is available for use in hiring and resource planning.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the job profile as used in job postings, contracts, and organizational charts.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of time requiring business travel for this job profile (0-100).']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Labor union or guild affiliation applicable to this job profile (e.g., SAG-AFTRA, DGA, IATSE), if any.']
  usage_rights_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile requires explicit usage rights management for work produced (e.g., actors, photographers, directors).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this job profile record for tracking changes and maintaining history.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for job_profile. Referenced by job_profile_id.'
}

Table talent.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for requisition']
  approved_by_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executive or manager who provided final approval for this requisition.']
  created_by_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initially created the requisition record.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location for this position.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or business unit that owns this requisition.']
  recruiter_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent acquisition specialist assigned to manage this requisition.']
  replacement_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on requisition (replacement_requisition_id)']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the hiring manager responsible for this requisition.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the requisition in the approval workflow (HR, Finance, Executive).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition received final approval to proceed with recruitment.']
  budget_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the financial budget for this requisition has been formally approved.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was closed (filled, cancelled, or withdrawn).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the salary range (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  education_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the position.']
  headcount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this requisition is for a new role, replacing a departing employee, or expanding capacity.']
  hourly_rate_maximum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the approved hourly rate for contractor or freelancer positions.']
  hourly_rate_minimum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the approved hourly rate for contractor or freelancer positions.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the role, responsibilities, qualifications, and expectations for the position.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional category that groups similar roles together for workforce planning and career pathing.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Seniority or career level of the position within the organizational hierarchy.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The official title of the position being recruited for (e.g., Senior Art Director, Copywriter, Production Manager).']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for the role.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition record was last updated.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was officially opened and made available for candidate sourcing.']
  position_count varchar [note: 'Number of positions to be filled under this single requisition (typically 1, but may be higher for bulk hiring).']
  posting_title varchar [note: 'Public-facing job title used in external job postings and advertisements (may differ from internal job title).']
  posting_visibility varchar [note: 'Defines whether the requisition is advertised internally only, externally, or both.']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of desirable but not mandatory skills or qualifications.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business urgency assigned to this requisition for resource allocation and recruiter prioritization.']
  remote_work_policy varchar [note: 'Defines the remote work arrangement permitted for this position.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of mandatory skills, competencies, or certifications required for the role.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the requisition, typically used in communications and approvals.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition in the recruitment workflow.']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship being recruited for.']
  salary_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the approved salary range for this position in the agency\'s base currency.']
  salary_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the approved salary range for this position in the agency\'s base currency.']
  target_fill_date date [note: 'Desired or planned date by which the position should be filled.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from requisition opening to closure, used for recruiting performance metrics.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for requisition. Referenced by requisition_id.'
}

Table talent.candidate {
  candidate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for candidate']
  hiring_manager_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hiring manager or department head who is evaluating this candidate.']
  job_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job requisition or open position the candidate is being considered for.']
  referred_by_candidate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on candidate (referred_by_candidate_id)']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal recruiter or talent acquisition specialist assigned to this candidate.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date when the candidate submitted their application or was first entered into the system.']
  availability_date date [note: 'The date when the candidate is available to start work if hired.']
  candidate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the candidate based on the type of talent role they are being considered for (employee, freelancer, contractor, influencer, actor, photographer, director, production crew).']
  certifications varchar [note: 'List of professional certifications, licenses, or credentials held by the candidate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the candidate record was first created in the system.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the candidate\'s current employer or most recent employer if currently unemployed.']
  current_job_title varchar [note: 'The candidate\'s current or most recent job title or role.']
  degree_field varchar [note: 'The primary field of study or major for the candidate\'s highest degree.']
  desired_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'The candidate\'s desired or expected salary amount for the position.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for candidate communication and correspondence.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'The first name or given name of the candidate.']
  highest_education_level varchar [note: 'The highest level of education attained by the candidate (high school, associate, bachelor, master, doctorate, professional degree).']
  interview_count varchar [note: 'Total number of interviews conducted with this candidate.']
  languages varchar [note: 'List of languages the candidate speaks and their proficiency levels.']
  last_interview_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent interview conducted with the candidate.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'The last name or family name of the candidate.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'URL to the candidate\'s LinkedIn professional profile.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the candidate is currently located or resides.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the candidate\'s current location (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the candidate is currently located.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes and comments about the candidate from recruiters, interviewers, or hiring managers.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate accepted the job offer.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'Date when a job offer was extended to the candidate.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the candidate.']
  portfolio_url varchar [note: 'URL to the candidate\'s online portfolio showcasing their creative work, projects, or professional samples.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Name of the person or entity who referred the candidate, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or explanation for why the candidate was rejected or not selected for the position.']
  resume_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the candidate\'s resume or curriculum vitae document.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the desired salary (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  screening_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned during initial screening or assessment of the candidate\'s qualifications (0-100 scale).']
  skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or text description of the candidate\'s key skills, competencies, and areas of expertise relevant to advertising and marketing roles.']
  source varchar [note: 'The channel or source through which the candidate was acquired (job board, referral, agency, direct application, social media, career site).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the candidate in the recruitment process (new, screening, interviewing, offer extended, hired, rejected, withdrawn).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the candidate record was last modified or updated.']
  willing_to_relocate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is willing to relocate for the position (True/False).']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'The candidate\'s current work authorization status in the target country (citizen, permanent resident, work visa, requires sponsorship, not authorized).']
  years_of_experience decimal(4,1) [note: 'Total number of years of relevant professional experience the candidate possesses.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for candidate. Referenced by candidate_id.'
}

Table talent.holiday_calendar {
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for holiday_calendar']
  derived_from_holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on holiday_calendar (derived_from_holiday_calendar_id)']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organization or business unit that owns and uses this holiday calendar. Applicable for organizational and production calendars; nullable for national/regional calendars.']
  applies_to_contractors boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to contractors and freelancers. True if contractors observe these holidays; false if they follow different schedules.']
  applies_to_full_time_employees boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to full-time employees. True if FTE staff observe these holidays; false if excluded.']
  applies_to_production_crew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to production crews (directors, photographers, actors, production staff). True if production schedules must respect these holidays; false if production operates independently.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this holiday calendar was formally approved for use in resource planning, payroll, and time tracking systems.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this holiday calendar for use. Typically an HR manager, operations director, or authorized administrator.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the calendar for system integration and reference (e.g., \'US_FED_2024\', \'UK_BANK\', \'PROD_CAL\').']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the holiday calendar (e.g., \'US Federal Holidays 2024\', \'UK Bank Holidays\', \'Agency Production Calendar\').']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holiday calendar indicating its scope and purpose: national (country-wide public holidays), regional (state/province holidays), organizational (company-specific holidays), production (shoot/production blackout dates), project (campaign-specific non-working days).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country jurisdiction for this holiday calendar (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN, FRA). Used for national and regional calendars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this holiday calendar record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and applicability of this holiday calendar, including any special rules or exceptions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The last date on which this holiday calendar is applicable. Nullable for open-ended calendars that remain in effect until superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The first date on which this holiday calendar becomes applicable for resource planning, payroll, and time tracking purposes.']
  external_calendar_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this holiday calendar in the source system (e.g., Workday calendar ID, Workfront calendar GUID). Used for integration and synchronization with upstream systems.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default holiday calendar for the organization or region. True if this calendar should be automatically applied to new employees or projects; false otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this holiday calendar record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this holiday calendar (e.g., \'Includes floating holidays\', \'Production blackout dates for Q4 campaign\').']
  observance_rule varchar [note: 'Defines how holidays that fall on weekends are observed. Values: actual_date (holiday observed on the actual date regardless of day of week), nearest_weekday (shifted to nearest weekday), following_monday (shifted to following Monday if on weekend), preceding_friday (shifted to preceding Friday if on weekend).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional subdivision code for calendars that apply to specific geographic regions within a country (e.g., \'CA\' for California, \'ON\' for Ontario, \'NSW\' for New South Wales). Nullable for national or organizational calendars.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this holiday calendar was sourced or integrated (e.g., \'Workday HCM\', \'Workfront\', \'Manual Entry\', \'Government API\'). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the holiday calendar. Active calendars are in use for resource planning and time tracking; draft calendars are under review; inactive calendars are temporarily disabled; archived calendars are retained for historical reference only.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this holiday calendar (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Tokyo\'). Used to determine exact holiday start/end times for global operations.']
  total_holidays varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct holiday dates defined in this calendar. Used for quick reference and capacity planning calculations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this holiday calendar. Incremented each time the calendar is updated or revised. Supports change tracking and audit history.']
  weekend_pattern varchar [note: 'Defines the standard weekend days for this calendar. Values: sat_sun (Saturday and Sunday, common in Western countries), fri_sat (Friday and Saturday, common in Middle East), sun_only (Sunday only), none (no standard weekend, e.g., shift-based operations).']
  working_days_per_year varchar [note: 'The calculated number of working days in the calendar year after excluding weekends and holidays. Used for resource capacity planning, project scheduling, and workload forecasting.']
  year varchar [note: 'The calendar year (YYYY) for which this holiday calendar is defined. Many organizations maintain separate calendar records per year to accommodate year-specific holiday variations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for holiday_calendar. Referenced by holiday_calendar_id.'
}

Table talent.review_template {
  review_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_template']
  derived_from_review_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_template (derived_from_review_template_id)']
  approval_workflow_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completed reviews using this template require approval from senior management or HR before being finalized and shared with the reviewee.']
  approved_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this review template for active use. Typically an HR leader or senior manager. Null if template is not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review template was approved for active use. Null if template is not yet approved.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reviews using this template must go through a calibration process where managers meet to ensure consistent rating standards across the organization.']
  compensation_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the results of reviews using this template directly influence compensation decisions such as merit increases, bonuses, or promotions.']
  competency_framework_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competency framework that defines the skills and behaviors evaluated in this review template. Links to the competency model used for assessment.']
  created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this review template record. Links to the user or employee who authored the template.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review template record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the review template purpose, scope, and intended use cases. Explains when and how this template should be applied.']
  development_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review process requires creation of a formal development plan or individual development plan (IDP) as an output.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this review template becomes active and available for use in review cycles.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this review template is no longer active. Null for templates with no planned end date.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'The employment classification this template is intended for (full-time employee, contractor, freelancer, temporary worker, intern, or all types).']
  goal_setting_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review template includes a goal-setting component for defining future objectives and development plans.']
  is_default_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default review template for its target role category and employment type. Only one template per category/type combination should be marked as default.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 country code) indicating the language of the review template content (e.g., en, en-US, es, fr-CA).']
  last_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this review template record. Links to the user or employee who made the most recent update.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review template record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or guidance for administrators and managers using this review template. May include best practices, tips, or special considerations.']
  peer_feedback_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review process requires input from peers or colleagues as part of a 360-degree feedback approach.']
  rating_scale_max varchar [note: 'Maximum value of the numeric rating scale (e.g., 5 for a 1-5 scale). Null if rating scale is not numeric.']
  rating_scale_min varchar [note: 'Minimum value of the numeric rating scale (e.g., 1 for a 1-5 scale). Null if rating scale is not numeric.']
  rating_scale_type varchar [note: 'The type of rating scale used in this template (numeric scale like 1-5, letter grades like A-F, descriptive labels like Exceeds/Meets/Below, or no formal rating).']
  review_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected duration in minutes for completing a review using this template. Used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The typical cadence at which this review template is used (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, project-based, or ad-hoc).']
  self_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review process requires the reviewee to complete a self-assessment before the manager review.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review template. Draft templates are under development, active templates are in use, inactive templates are temporarily disabled, archived templates are retained for historical purposes, deprecated templates are replaced by newer versions.']
  target_role_category varchar [note: 'The primary role category this review template is designed for. Allows templates to be tailored to specific talent segments (e.g., creative roles, production crews, account managers, strategists, media planners, technology staff, operations, leadership).']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the review template used in external systems and integrations (e.g., PERF-ANNUAL, FRLNC-90D, DIR-EVAL).']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the review template (e.g., Annual Performance Review, 90-Day Freelancer Check-In, Creative Director Evaluation).']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review template by its primary purpose (performance evaluation, competency assessment, project retrospective, onboarding review, offboarding interview, probation review).']
  upward_feedback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review template includes upward feedback where direct reports can provide feedback on their manager.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this review template has been used to conduct reviews. Tracks template adoption and popularity.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the review template (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1) to track template evolution and changes over time.']
  workday_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review template is integrated with Workday HCM for automated data synchronization, review initiation, and results storage.']
  workfront_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review template is integrated with Workfront resource management for project-based performance data and resource utilization metrics.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_template. Referenced by template_id.'
}

Table talent.location {
  location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for location']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Description of accessibility features available at the location (e.g., wheelchair access, elevators, accessible restrooms).']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the location (street number and name).']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the location (suite, floor, building name).']
  capacity_headcount varchar [note: 'Maximum number of people (employees, contractors, freelancers) that can be accommodated at the location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the location is situated.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which location expenses are allocated.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the location is situated (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the location record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for financial amounts associated with the location (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the location record became active and operational.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the location record is active; null for currently active locations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the location (e.g., general inquiries, reception).']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the location, if applicable.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the location in decimal degrees.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when the lease or occupancy agreement for the location ends or is scheduled to end.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the lease or occupancy agreement for the location began.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Ownership or lease arrangement for the location.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the location (e.g., office code, studio code, facility code).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the location (e.g., \'New York Creative Studio\', \'London Office\').']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location in its lifecycle.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location based on its primary function within the agency.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the location in decimal degrees.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who manages or oversees this location.']
  monthly_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly rental cost for the location in the local currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the location.']
  parent_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent location if this location is part of a hierarchical structure (e.g., a satellite office under a regional headquarters).']
  parking_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parking facilities are available at the location.']
  parking_spaces_count varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces available at the location.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the location address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business region or geographic market segment to which the location belongs (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\').']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the security measures and access controls in place at the location.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the location in square feet.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the location is situated.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the location record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for location. Referenced by location_id.'
}

Table talent.payroll_calendar {
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_calendar']
  prior_payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_calendar (prior_payroll_calendar_id)']
  applies_to_employee_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee types this calendar applies to (e.g., \'FTE,Contractor,Freelancer,Temporary\').']
  approved_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this payroll calendar for operational use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payroll calendar was approved for use by authorized personnel.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the payroll calendar used in payroll systems and HR integrations.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the payroll calendar (e.g., \'US Biweekly 2024\', \'UK Monthly 2024\').']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll calendar based on payment frequency (weekly, biweekly, semimonthly, monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the jurisdiction or country for which this payroll calendar is configured (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payroll calendar record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for payroll transactions processed under this calendar (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  default_pay_date_offset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the pay period end date that payment is typically issued (e.g., 5 days for processing).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the payroll calendar including its purpose, scope, and any special processing rules.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this payroll calendar ceases to be active. Nullable for open-ended calendars.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this payroll calendar becomes active and begins governing payroll processing.']
  first_pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the first pay period in this payroll calendar, used as the anchor for calculating subsequent periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this payroll calendar applies (e.g., 2024).']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the holiday calendar used to adjust pay dates when they fall on non-working days.']
  is_default_calendar boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the default payroll calendar for the organization or region.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payroll calendar is locked from further modifications (typically after payroll processing has begun).']
  last_pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the final pay period in this payroll calendar.']
  locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payroll calendar was locked from further changes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payroll calendar record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the payroll calendar for internal reference and documentation.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Number of pay periods per year for this calendar (e.g., 52 for weekly, 26 for biweekly, 24 for semimonthly, 12 for monthly).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Regional or state/province code within the country for localized payroll calendars (e.g., \'CA\' for California, \'ON\' for Ontario).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll calendar indicating whether it is in use, archived, or under development.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the payroll calendar (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\') used for scheduling and cutoff times.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the payroll calendar to track changes and revisions over time.']
  workday_hcm_calendar_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the corresponding payroll calendar in Workday HCM system for integration and synchronization.']
  workfront_calendar_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the corresponding resource calendar in Workfront resource management system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_calendar. Referenced by payroll_calendar_id.'
}

  dissolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal entity was officially dissolved or ceased operations, if applicable.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or fictitious business name under which the entity operates, if different from legal name.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet identifier used for business credit and vendor management.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees (FTE and contractors) associated with this legal entity.']
  entity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and legal status of the entity in its lifecycle.']
  fiscal_year_end_day varchar [note: 'Day of month (1-31) when the entity\'s fiscal year ends.']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when the entity\'s fiscal year ends for financial reporting purposes.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (e.g., NAICS, SIC) identifying the entity\'s primary business activity.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity record is currently active and in use for business operations.']
  is_publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity\'s shares are traded on a public stock exchange.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity has tax-exempt status (e.g., non-profit, charitable organization).']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the primary jurisdiction where the entity is incorporated.']
  jurisdiction_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional subdivision within the country where the entity is registered.']
  legal_entity_type varchar [note: 'The legal structure classification of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership).']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the entity as it appears on incorporation documents and legal contracts.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier for global entity identification in financial transactions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was last updated.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership held by the parent entity, if applicable (0.00 to 100.00).']
  parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent legal entity if this entity is a subsidiary or branch.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address for official correspondence with the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for contacting the legal entity.']
  primary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the entity\'s primary operating currency.']
  registered_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address on file with the business registry.']
  registered_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered address (suite, floor, building name), if applicable.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered address.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the registered address.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction\'s business registry.']
  stock_exchange_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter ISO Market Identifier Code (MIC) of the primary stock exchange where shares are listed.']
  stock_exchange_symbol varchar [note: 'Ticker symbol under which the entity\'s shares are traded, if publicly traded.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identifier for the legal entity (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU).']
  ultimate_parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the top-level parent entity in the corporate hierarchy.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the legal entity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for legal_entity. Referenced by legal_entity_id.'
}

Table performance.impression_event {
  impression_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each individual ad impression event. Primary key for the impression event log. Serves as the atomic unit for all downstream delivery measurement, billing reconciliation, and performance analytics.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_activation. Business justification: Activations push audience segments to DSPs/platforms where impressions are served. Links impression delivery to the specific activation job, enables match rate analysis, activation performance measure']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser client on whose behalf this impression was served. Enables client-level reporting and cross-campaign analysis.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Core targeting relationship - every impression is served to a targeted audience segment. Essential for segment performance reporting, ROAS analysis, and optimization decisions. Fundamental to campaign']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Ad servers must track which brand profile each impression serves for brand safety compliance, consent management, and brand-level performance reporting. Essential for GARM/brand safety floor enforceme']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent advertising campaign under which this impression was served. Enables campaign-level aggregation and performance rollup.']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Brand-level performance reporting is core to advertising operations. Brand managers and clients require impression delivery metrics aggregated by brand for campaign optimization, media mix analysis, a']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset that was displayed in this impression. Links to creative master data containing asset specifications, format, dimensions, and approval status.']
  holdout_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.holdout_group. Business justification: Holdout groups define control populations that should NOT receive impressions for incrementality testing. Essential for measuring true advertising lift, validating campaign effectiveness, and calculat']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media plan line item or insertion order line that governs this impression delivery. Critical for pacing, budget tracking, and billing reconciliation.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific media placement where this impression was delivered. Links to the media placement master data defining publisher, site, position, and format.']
  programmatic_deal_id varchar [note: 'The identifier for the programmatic guaranteed deal, private marketplace (PMP) deal, or preferred deal under which this impression was transacted. Links to deal master data for supply-path analysis.']
  publisher_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the publisher or media property where the impression was delivered. Links to publisher master data for supply-side analysis and reconciliation.']
  suppression_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.suppression_list. Business justification: Impressions must respect suppression lists (frequency caps, opt-outs, competitive exclusions). Critical for compliance with privacy regulations, honoring user preferences, and preventing over-exposure']
  trafficking_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trafficking instruction that governed the delivery of this impression. Links to ad server trafficking configuration for delivery validation and troubleshooting.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_contract. Business justification: Vendor contracts with publishers/SSPs govern inventory supply terms and rates. Linking impressions to vendor contracts enables rate reconciliation, volume commitment tracking, and supplier performance']
  ad_position varchar [note: 'The position of the ad unit on the page or within the app. Indicates placement prominence and impacts viewability and engagement rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: above_the_fold|below_the_fold|header|footer|sidebar|in_content|interstitial|overlay — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ad_server_source varchar [note: 'The ad serving platform or demand-side platform (DSP) that recorded and delivered this impression. Identifies the operational system of record for this event.']
  ad_size varchar [note: 'The pixel dimensions of the ad creative served (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, 1920x1080). Used for format performance analysis and creative optimization.']
  auction_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the programmatic auction in which this impression was won. Used for RTB (Real-Time Bidding) analysis, bid landscape reconstruction, and supply-path optimization.']
  audience_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audience segment IDs that the user matched at impression time. Links to audience taxonomy from DMP (Data Management Platform) or CDP (Customer Data Platform). Used for audience targeting validation and performance analysis.']
  bid_price_cpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'The winning bid price for this impression expressed in CPM (Cost Per Mille / cost per thousand impressions). Represents the clearing price in the programmatic auction. Used for media cost analysis and ROAS calculation.']
  brand_safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A score from 0.00 to 100.00 indicating the brand safety level of the content context where the impression was served. Higher scores indicate safer, more brand-appropriate environments.']
  browser varchar [note: 'The web browser or app environment in which the impression was displayed (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, in-app). Used for browser-specific performance and compatibility analysis.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The digital channel or device environment where the impression was delivered. Includes desktop web, mobile web, mobile app, Connected TV (CTV), Over-the-Top (OTT), and Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH).']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether user consent was obtained for data collection and ad personalization at impression time. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance. Values: granted (consent obtained), denied (consent refused), not_required (jurisdiction does not require consent), unknown (consent status not captured).']
  content_category varchar [note: 'The IAB content category or taxonomy classification of the page or app content where the impression was served. Used for contextual targeting validation and brand safety analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the media cost and bid price. Enables multi-currency campaign analysis and financial reconciliation.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The category of device on which the impression was rendered. Used for device-level performance analysis and cross-device attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: desktop|smartphone|tablet|smart_tv|gaming_console|wearable|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'The name and version of the source system that generated this impression event record. Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A risk score from 0.00 to 100.00 indicating the likelihood that this impression is fraudulent or invalid. Generated by fraud detection vendors or internal algorithms. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk.']
  frequency_cap_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this user has been exposed to this campaign or creative prior to this impression. Used for frequency capping enforcement and ad fatigue analysis.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'The city or designated market area (DMA) where the impression was delivered. Enables hyper-local targeting validation and metro-level performance analysis.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location where the impression was delivered. Derived from IP geolocation or device GPS. Used for geo-targeting validation and regional performance analysis.']
  geo_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code where the impression was delivered. Supports neighborhood-level targeting and local market analysis.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'The state, province, or region where the impression was delivered. Supports sub-national geographic analysis and local market performance tracking.']
  impression_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the ad impression was served to the end user. Represents the real-world event time of ad delivery. Critical for time-series analysis, dayparting optimization, and billing reconciliation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  impression_type varchar [note: 'The format or modality of the ad impression served. Distinguishes between display banners, video ads, native content, audio spots, and rich media executions.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this impression event record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data freshness monitoring and ETL (Extract Transform Load) pipeline auditing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device that received the impression. Used for geo-targeting validation, fraud detection, and audience verification. May be considered PII under GDPR and CCPA.']
  is_autoplay boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad started playing automatically without user initiation. Applicable to video impressions. Impacts user experience and engagement metrics.']
  is_bot_traffic boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the impression was identified as non-human bot traffic. Used for invalid traffic (IVT) filtering and billing adjustment.']
  is_muted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video or audio ad was muted at the time of impression. Applicable to video and audio impression types. Impacts engagement and completion rates.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the impression delivery location. Used for proximity-based targeting validation and location-based analytics, particularly for DOOH and mobile campaigns.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the impression delivery location. Used for proximity-based targeting validation and location-based analytics, particularly for DOOH and mobile campaigns.']
  media_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'The actual media cost incurred for this single impression. Calculated from the bid price or rate card. Used for billing reconciliation, budget tracking, and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
