  operating_system varchar [note: 'The operating system of the device that rendered the impression (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS). Supports device compatibility analysis and targeting optimization.']
  page_url varchar [note: 'The full URL of the page where the impression was displayed. Supports contextual analysis, brand safety verification, and content alignment validation.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the user session during which this impression was served. Enables session-level analysis and cross-impression journey tracking.']
  site_domain varchar [note: 'The domain name of the website or app where the impression was served. Used for brand safety analysis, contextual targeting validation, and publisher performance tracking.']
  supply_chain_object varchar [note: 'The sellers.json or SupplyChain object captured at impression time, identifying all intermediaries in the programmatic supply chain. Used for supply-path transparency and optimization per IAB ads.txt and sellers.json standards.']
  tcf_consent_string varchar [note: 'The IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) consent string captured at impression time. Encodes user consent choices for data processing purposes. Required for GDPR compliance in programmatic advertising.']
  time_in_view_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total duration in seconds that the impression was visible in the users viewport. Critical metric for engagement analysis and viewability quality assessment.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The full user agent string captured at impression time. Contains detailed device, browser, and operating system information for advanced device fingerprinting and fraud detection.']
  user_identifier varchar [note: 'The pseudonymized or hashed user identifier (cookie ID, device ID, or universal ID) associated with this impression. Used for cross-device tracking, frequency management, and attribution. Subject to GDPR and CCPA privacy regulations.']
  video_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The total duration of the video ad creative in seconds. Applicable only to video impression types. Used for video completion rate analysis and engagement measurement.']
  video_player_size varchar [note: 'The size category of the video player at impression time. Applicable to video impressions. Impacts viewability and user engagement.']
  viewability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the ad creative that was visible in the viewport at the time of measurement. Ranges from 0.00 to 100.00. Used for granular viewability analysis and quality scoring.']
  viewability_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the impression met the MRC (Media Rating Council) viewability standard at the time of delivery. Viewable means the ad was in-view per IAB/MRC guidelines (50% of pixels visible for 1+ seconds for display, 2+ seconds for video).']

  Note: 'Raw delivery event record capturing each individual ad impression served across all channels and placements. Stores the atomic unit of campaign delivery — timestamp, placement, creative, device, geo, and auction-level signals from ad servers (Google Campaign Manager 360) and DSPs (The Trade Desk). Foundation for all downstream delivery measurement and billing reconciliation. Distinct from aggregated metrics: this is the raw event log at the impression grain.'
}

Table performance.click_event {
  click_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each click event record. Primary key for the click event entity.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Clicks occur within targeted segments - critical for segment-level CTR analysis, engagement measurement, and audience quality scoring. Enables advertisers to identify high-performing segments and opti']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Click attribution and engagement analysis require brand context for brand-level CTR reporting, brand engagement dashboards, and multi-brand advertiser performance comparison. Standard in enterprise ad']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign under which this click occurred. Enables campaign-level click aggregation and performance analysis.']
  contract_insertion_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: CPC-based IOs require click-level tracking for budget consumption and invoice reconciliation. Enables automated billing validation against contracted click rates and caps. Essential for performance-ba']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset that was clicked. Identifies the specific ad unit, banner, video, or interactive element that received the user interaction.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent impression event that was served before this click occurred. Links click to the originating ad delivery event.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media placement where the clicked ad was served. Identifies the specific inventory position and publisher context.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the user session during which the click occurred. Enables session-level analysis and multi-touch attribution within a single browsing session.']
  ad_server_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the ad server that recorded and tracked this click event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Sizmek, Flashtalking). Used for data reconciliation and discrepancy analysis.']
  attribution_window_hours varchar [note: 'The number of hours after the click during which a conversion can be attributed to this click event. Typically set at campaign or advertiser level (e.g., 24, 72, 168 hours).']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this click event is billable under the cost-per-click (CPC) pricing model. Non-billable clicks include invalid traffic, duplicate clicks, and clicks outside contracted parameters.']
  click_fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the invalid traffic detection system, typically ranging from 0 (legitimate) to 100 (high fraud risk). Used for sophisticated IVT filtering and billing dispute resolution.']
  click_interaction_type varchar [note: 'The type of user interaction that triggered the click event. Distinguishes between standard clicks, video overlay clicks, expandable ad interactions, and mobile gestures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_click|call_to_action|video_overlay|expandable_panel|swipe|tap|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  click_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time elapsed in milliseconds between the impression render and the click event. Used to detect rapid-fire bot clicks and analyze user engagement timing patterns.']
  click_position_x varchar [note: 'Horizontal pixel coordinate of the click within the ad unit. Used for heatmap analysis and creative engagement optimization.']
  click_position_y varchar [note: 'Vertical pixel coordinate of the click within the ad unit. Used for heatmap analysis and creative engagement optimization.']
  click_redirect_url varchar [note: 'The destination URL to which the user was redirected after clicking the ad. May include tracking parameters, campaign identifiers, and attribution tokens.']
  click_source_platform varchar [note: 'The platform or channel through which the click was delivered. Distinguishes between web, mobile app, connected TV (CTV), digital out-of-home (DOOH), and other channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_web|mobile_app|ctv|dooh|email|social|search|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the user clicked the ad unit, captured in UTC. Primary business event timestamp for click-through rate (CTR) calculation and time-series analysis.']
  click_validation_status varchar [note: 'Validation status indicating whether the click is considered legitimate traffic or flagged as invalid per TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) and MRC (Media Rating Council) invalid traffic (IVT) detection standards. Determines billability for cost-per-click (CPC) campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: valid|invalid_bot|invalid_duplicate|invalid_fraud|invalid_datacenter|suspicious|pending_validation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_attributed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this click event was later attributed to a conversion event within the attribution window. Used for click-to-conversion analysis and return on ad spend (ROAS) calculation.']
  cpc_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'The contracted cost per click rate for this event in the campaign currency. Used to calculate click-level revenue and cost-per-acquisition (CPA) metrics.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the CPC rate and billing amounts. Supports multi-currency campaign reconciliation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or platform that generated this click event record (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Mediaocean Prisma). Used for data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Hashed or anonymized device identifier derived from browser characteristics, IP address, user agent, and other signals. Used for fraud detection, frequency capping, and cross-session attribution without relying on cookies.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device on which the click occurred. Supports device-level performance segmentation and cross-device attribution analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: desktop|mobile|tablet|ctv|ott|wearable|console|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_city varchar [note: 'City or metropolitan area where the click occurred, derived from IP geolocation. Supports hyper-local campaign performance analysis.']
  geographic_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the geographic location of the click based on IP geolocation. Supports geo-targeting validation and regional performance analysis.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the click occurred, derived from IP geolocation. Enables sub-national geographic performance segmentation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the click originated. Used for geographic targeting validation, fraud detection, and datacenter traffic filtering. May be anonymized or hashed per GDPR and CCPA requirements.']
  ivt_detection_method varchar [note: 'The method or layer at which invalid traffic detection was applied: general IVT (GIVT) for automated bot detection, sophisticated IVT (SIVT) for advanced fraud analysis, or manual review.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this click event record was first ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data pipeline monitoring and audit trail purposes.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this click event record was last modified or enriched (e.g., validation status updated, conversion attribution applied). Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the page or application context where the ad was displayed and clicked. Used for contextual analysis and publisher verification.']
  tracking_pixel_fired_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the click tracking pixel successfully fired and recorded the event. Used for data quality validation and discrepancy troubleshooting.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The HTTP user agent string reported by the browser or application at the time of the click. Used for device detection, browser identification, and bot filtering.']

  Note: 'Raw click interaction event record capturing each user click on a served ad unit. Stores click timestamp, placement identifier, creative identifier, redirect URL, device fingerprint, and click validation status (invalid traffic / IVT flags per TAG/MRC standards). Source of CPC billing actuals and CTR computation inputs. Distinct from impression_event: represents a user-initiated interaction, not a passive delivery.'
}

Table performance.conversion_event {
  conversion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the conversion event record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advertiser client who owns this conversion event.']
  attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Each conversion event is attributed using a specific attribution model configuration. The conversion_event table currently has attribution_model (STRING) storing the model name/code. Adding attributio']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Conversions attributed to audience segments - fundamental for calculating segment-level ROAS, CPA, and conversion rates. Enables audience valuation, budget allocation decisions, and segment performanc']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Conversion attribution to brand is fundamental for brand ROI measurement, brand-level CPA/ROAS reporting, and brand performance optimization. Critical for multi-brand advertisers tracking which brand ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the campaign that drove this conversion event.']
  click_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: Post-click conversions are attributed to specific click events. The conversion_event table currently has click_timestamp (STRING) but no FK to click_event. Adding click_event_id FK enables direct link']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Conversion attribution to specific brands is essential for brand-level ROI calculation, brand performance scorecards, and budget allocation decisions. Multi-brand advertisers require separate conversi']
  contract_insertion_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: CPA/CPL insertion orders bill on conversion delivery. Direct IO link enables automated billing calculation, pacing against conversion commitments, and contractual performance reporting. Critical for p']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the creative asset that was served and led to this conversion.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: Post-view conversions are attributed to specific impression events. The conversion_event table currently has impression_timestamp (STRING) but no FK to impression_event. Adding impression_event_id FK ']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the line item within the campaign that generated this conversion.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media placement where the ad was displayed.']
  tracking_pixel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.tracking_pixel. Business justification: Conversion events are generated by tracking pixels/conversion tags deployed on advertiser sites. The conversion_event table has conversion_tag_id (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Adding tra']
  attribution_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this conversion was attributed to a click-through or view-through interaction.']
  attribution_window_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after ad interaction (click or impression) during which a conversion can be attributed to that interaction.']
  browser varchar [note: 'The web browser used by the user at the time of conversion (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).']
  conversion_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  conversion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the specific conversion action as defined by the advertiser (e.g., \'Product Purchase\', \'Newsletter Signup\', \'Demo Request\').']
  conversion_page_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the advertiser page where the conversion action was completed.']
  conversion_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of units or items involved in the conversion (e.g., number of products purchased, number of form submissions).']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'The validation status of the conversion event (e.g., confirmed, pending verification, rejected, flagged as fraud).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the conversion action occurred on the advertiser property. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'The category of conversion action that occurred (e.g., purchase, sign-up, form fill, app install, lead generation, download).']
  conversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value associated with this conversion event (e.g., purchase amount, estimated lead value). Used to calculate ROAS (Return on Ad Spend).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this conversion event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or platform that captured and reported this conversion event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Salesforce Marketing Cloud).']
  deduplication_key varchar [note: 'Unique key used to prevent duplicate counting of the same conversion event across multiple tracking sources or attribution models.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device on which the conversion occurred (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV - Connected TV, other).']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that this conversion is fraudulent, as determined by fraud detection systems.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City where the conversion occurred, derived from IP geolocation.']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the conversion occurred (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the conversion occurred (e.g., California, Ontario, Bavaria).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the user at the time of conversion. May be hashed or truncated for privacy compliance.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this conversion event was identified as a duplicate and excluded from reporting (True/False).']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the advertiser page where the user landed after clicking the ad.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'The operating system of the device on which the conversion occurred (e.g., Windows, macOS, iOS, Android).']
  order_reference varchar [note: 'The advertiser\'s internal order or transaction identifier associated with this conversion (e.g., e-commerce order number).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The category of product or service involved in the conversion (e.g., Electronics, Apparel, Financial Services).']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the page from which the user navigated to the conversion page.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this conversion event record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  user_identifier varchar [note: 'Pseudonymized or hashed identifier for the user who completed the conversion action. Used for cross-device and cross-session attribution.']

  Note: 'Conversion attribution event record capturing post-click and post-view conversion actions (purchases, sign-ups, form fills, app installs) tied to a specific campaign, line item, and creative. Stores conversion type, attribution window, attribution model (last-click, data-driven, linear), conversion value, and deduplication key. Source of CPA and ROAS actuals. Linked to conversion tags and tracking pixels deployed on advertiser properties.'
}

Table performance.viewability_measurement {
  viewability_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the viewability measurement record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this viewability measurement.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset that was served and measured for viewability.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual ad impression being measured. Links to ad server impression logs.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media placement being measured for viewability.']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Viewability SLAs are common contractual guarantees in media agreements. Direct link enables automated compliance monitoring, breach detection, penalty calculation, and make-good triggering. Core to co']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Viewability measurements are provided by third-party verification vendors (IAS, MOAT, DoubleVerify). Agencies need FK to supplier for vendor performance scorecards, contract compliance tracking, discr']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'The format type of the ad unit being measured (display, video, native, rich media, interstitial, expandable).']
  audible_on_complete_flag boolean [note: 'For video ads, indicates whether the ad was audible when the video completed. Supports audible and viewable measurement.']
  brand_safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the placement passed brand safety verification at the time of viewability measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this viewability measurement record was first created in the data platform.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A quality score (0-100) assigned by the measurement vendor indicating the reliability and accuracy of the viewability measurement.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device on which the ad was served and measured (desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV, OTT, DOOH).']
  environment varchar [note: 'The digital environment where the ad was served (web browser, in-app, CTV, DOOH).']
  guaranteed_viewability_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractual viewability rate threshold (percentage) guaranteed in the insertion order, if applicable.']
  in_view_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the ad creative pixels that were visible in the viewport at the time of measurement.']
  invalid_traffic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the impression was flagged as invalid traffic (bot, fraud, or non-human traffic) by the measurement vendor.']
  measurability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total impressions that were measurable for viewability, calculated as (measurable_impression_count / total_impression_count) * 100.']
  measurable_impression_count varchar [note: 'The total number of impressions for which viewability could be measured (excludes unmeasurable impressions due to technical limitations).']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the viewability measurement, used for daily aggregation and reporting.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'The technical methodology used to measure viewability (geometric, browser optimization, or reach extension).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the viewability measurement record (measured, unmeasurable, pending, error).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the viewability measurement was captured. Principal business event timestamp for this measurement record.']
  mrc_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this viewability measurement meets MRC accreditation standards and methodology requirements.']
  player_size_height varchar [note: 'The height of the video player or ad container in pixels at the time of measurement.']
  player_size_width varchar [note: 'The width of the video player or ad container in pixels at the time of measurement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or platform that originated this viewability measurement record (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Comscore, The Trade Desk).']
  time_in_view_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total duration in seconds that the ad was in-view (meeting the pixel threshold) during the measurement window.']
  total_impression_count varchar [note: 'The total number of ad impressions served, including both measurable and unmeasurable impressions.']
  unmeasurable_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why viewability could not be measured for this impression (e.g., cross-domain iframe, ad blocker, technical limitation).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this viewability measurement record was last updated or modified.']
  video_quartile_reached varchar [note: 'For video ads, the furthest quartile reached while the ad was viewable (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%). Not applicable for non-video formats.']
  viewability_guaranteed_io_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this impression is part of a viewability-guaranteed insertion order commitment.']
  viewability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of measurable impressions that were viewable, calculated as (viewable_impression_count / measurable_impression_count) * 100.']
  viewability_standard varchar [note: 'The viewability standard applied for this measurement (MRC display: 50%/1s, MRC video: 50%/2s, GroupM, or custom client standard).']
  viewable_impression_count varchar [note: 'The number of impressions that met the MRC viewability standard (50% of pixels in-view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).']

  Note: 'Viewability measurement record per impression or placement-day, capturing MRC-compliant viewability signals: in-view percentage, time-in-view (seconds), viewable impression count, measurable impression count, and viewability rate. Sourced from third-party measurement vendors (Comscore, IAS, DoubleVerify) and ad server signals. Supports viewability-guaranteed IO commitments and brand safety compliance reporting per MRC and IAB standards.'
}

Table performance.tracking_pixel {
  tracking_pixel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tracking pixel or conversion tag record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser client whose property or campaign this pixel is deployed on.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed per brand for measurement and must respect brand-specific consent requirements, data governance policies, and brand safety standards. Essential for GDPR/CCPA compliance wh']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign this pixel is associated with for measurement purposes.']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed for brand-specific conversion measurement. Brand managers need to audit which pixels are active for their brands, manage brand-specific conversion definitions, and ensure ']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed within specific creative assets for conversion tracking. Trafficking and analytics teams need this link to validate pixel deployment, troubleshoot firing issues, and recon']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific media placement or ad unit this pixel is tracking.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define measurement and tracking requirements as contractual deliverables. Linking pixels to SOWs enables scope validation, ensures deployed tracking matches contracted specifications, and support']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Tracking pixels are often provided by third-party tag management or analytics vendors (Google Tag Manager, Tealium, Adobe). Required for troubleshooting pixel fires, SLA management, vendor consolidati']
  browser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser in which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'Privacy consent category under which this pixel operates, determining when it may fire based on user consent preferences.']
  consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether user consent is required before the pixel can fire, per privacy regulations.']
  conversion_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion value.']
  conversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with a conversion event tracked by this pixel, used for ROAS calculation.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this tracking pixel configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tracking pixel record was first created in the system.']
  custom_parameters varchar [note: 'Additional key-value pairs or query string parameters passed with the pixel fire for custom tracking dimensions.']
  deduplication_key varchar [note: 'Unique identifier used to prevent duplicate counting of the same conversion event across multiple pixels or platforms.']
  deployment_environment varchar [note: 'The digital environment or platform type where the pixel is implemented.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the tracking pixel in the measurement infrastructure.']
  deployment_url varchar [note: 'The advertiser web page, app screen, or landing page URL where this pixel is deployed.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the device on which the pixel fired, derived from user agent analysis.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Technical error code returned when a pixel fire fails, used for troubleshooting and data quality monitoring.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the error that occurred during pixel fire, providing diagnostic detail.']
  fire_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the pixel fire attempt, indicating whether the tracking event was successfully recorded.']
  fire_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the pixel fire event occurred, representing the principal business event for this tracking record.']
  firing_rule varchar [note: 'Business logic or condition that determines when the pixel should fire (e.g., page load, button click, form submission, time on page).']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.']
  implementation_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to deploy and fire the tracking pixel on the target property.']
  ip_address_hash varchar [note: 'One-way cryptographic hash of the user IP address, stored for fraud detection and geographic analysis while maintaining GDPR compliance.']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after an ad interaction during which a conversion can be attributed to that interaction.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this tracking pixel configuration.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tracking pixel record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, implementation instructions, or troubleshooting notes related to this pixel.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device on which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.']
  pixel_code varchar [note: 'Unique external code or identifier for the pixel used in trafficking and reporting systems.']
  pixel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the tracking pixel for identification and management purposes.']
  pixel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tracking pixel based on its measurement purpose and firing trigger.']
  pixel_url varchar [note: 'Full URL endpoint that the pixel fires to when triggered, including protocol and domain.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the page that referred the user to the page where the pixel fired, used for traffic source analysis.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user agent string captured at the time of pixel fire, used for device and browser classification.']

  Note: 'Master record and complete operational event log for each tracking pixel or conversion tag deployed on advertiser web properties, app environments, or landing pages. Header section stores pixel definition: name, type (impression tracker, click tracker, conversion tag, retargeting pixel, audience pixel), implementation method (JavaScript, image pixel, server-to-server), firing rules, consent flags, and deployment status. Detail section stores the complete fire event history: individual fire timestamps, firing URL, user agent, IP hash (GDPR-compliant), referrer URL, custom parameters passed, fire status (success/blocked/timeout), and error details. Single source of truth for all pixel infrastructure configuration AND raw fire event data. Serves as the unified measurement infrastructure registry, pixel health monitoring source, and raw fire event stream for conversion deduplication and data quality validation. Supports GDPR/CCPA consent compliance workflows and tag governance audits.'
}

Table performance.delivery_pacing {
  delivery_pacing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery pacing record. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign for which delivery pacing is being tracked.']
  contract_insertion_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: Pacing analysis compares actual delivery against IO flight dates, budgets, and impression commitments. Direct IO link enables automated pacing alerts, budget consumption forecasting, and delivery guar']
  io_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: Pacing monitors delivery against contracted IO line commitments. Required for under-delivery alerts, budget reallocation decisions, vendor delivery performance tracking, and triggering makegood clause']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific campaign line item being paced. Line items represent individual media buys or placements within a campaign.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific media placement being tracked for delivery pacing.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'The actual number of impressions delivered during this pacing period as reported by the ad server or demand-side platform (DSP).']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual spend amount incurred during this pacing period as reported by the billing system or DSP.']
  alert_severity varchar [note: 'The severity level of the pacing alert if triggered. None means no alert, low/medium/high/critical indicate escalating levels of deviation requiring intervention.']
  bid_win_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'For programmatic campaigns, the percentage of bid requests that resulted in won auctions during this pacing period. Low win rates may indicate bid prices are too low.']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks during this pacing period, calculated as (clicks / impressions) * 100. Key performance indicator for engagement.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'The total number of conversions attributed to this line item or placement during the pacing period, based on conversion tracking pixels and attribution models.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pacing record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Part of audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spend amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this pacing data was sourced. Typically Google Campaign Manager 360 for direct ad serving or The Trade Desk for programmatic campaigns.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The delivery pacing strategy configured for this line item. Standard spreads delivery evenly, accelerated delivers as fast as possible, even maintains consistent daily delivery, ASAP prioritizes immediate delivery, frontloaded concentrates delivery early in the flight.']
  effective_cpa decimal(10,4) [note: 'The effective cost per conversion for this pacing period, calculated as actual_spend_amount / conversion_count. Key metric for performance-based campaigns.']
  effective_cpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'The effective cost per thousand impressions for this pacing period, calculated as (actual_spend_amount / actual_impressions) * 1000. Used to evaluate media efficiency.']
  frequency_cap_reached boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the frequency cap has been reached for this line item during the pacing period, which may impact delivery velocity.']
  impression_pacing_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of planned impressions that have been delivered, calculated as (actual_impressions / planned_impressions) * 100. Values above 100 indicate over-delivery.']
  inventory_availability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A score (0-100) representing the availability of ad inventory matching the targeting criteria during this pacing period. Low scores may explain under-delivery.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pacing record represents the current active measurement period. False indicates historical or superseded records.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact timestamp when this pacing measurement was captured from the source system. Critical for understanding data freshness and real-time vs near-real-time reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from campaign managers or automated systems explaining pacing anomalies, corrective actions taken, or contextual information about delivery performance.']
  pacing_alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an automated pacing alert was triggered for this period due to significant variance from plan, requiring campaign manager attention.']
  pacing_granularity varchar [note: 'The time granularity at which pacing is measured and reported. Hourly for real-time optimization, daily for standard monitoring, weekly for longer flights, or flight for entire campaign duration.']
  pacing_period_end timestamp [note: 'The end timestamp of the pacing measurement period. Defines the conclusion of the window for which delivery progress is being measured.']
  pacing_period_start timestamp [note: 'The start timestamp of the pacing measurement period. Defines the beginning of the window for which delivery progress is being measured.']
  pacing_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery pacing status indicating whether the line item or placement is delivering as planned. On-pace means within acceptable variance, under-delivering means behind target, over-delivering means ahead of target, paused means delivery is stopped, completed means flight has ended, at-risk means significant deviation detected.']
  pacing_variance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance threshold percentage used to determine pacing status. If actual pacing percentage deviates from 100% by more than this threshold, status changes from on-pace to under/over-delivering.']
  planned_impressions varchar [note: 'The target number of impressions planned to be delivered during this pacing period based on the media plan and flight schedule.']
  planned_spend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target spend amount planned for this pacing period based on the media budget allocation and flight schedule.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Platform-assigned quality score (typically 0-10 scale) reflecting ad relevance, landing page experience, and expected CTR. Higher scores improve auction competitiveness.']
  recommended_bid_adjustment decimal(5,2) [note: 'System-generated recommendation for bid adjustment to correct pacing issues. Positive values suggest increasing bids to accelerate delivery, negative values suggest decreasing bids to slow delivery.']
  recommended_budget_adjustment decimal(18,4) [note: 'System-generated recommendation for budget adjustment to correct pacing issues. Positive values suggest increasing budget allocation, negative values suggest reducing budget allocation.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this pacing record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  spend_pacing_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of planned spend that has been consumed, calculated as (actual_spend_amount / planned_spend_amount) * 100. Values above 100 indicate over-spend.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pacing record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Tracks data refresh cycles and enables incremental processing.']
  viewability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of delivered impressions that met viewability standards (MRC: 50% of pixels in view for 1+ seconds for display, 2+ seconds for video) during this pacing period.']

  Note: 'Real-time and near-real-time delivery pacing record tracking actual vs. planned delivery progress for each campaign line item and placement. Stores pacing period (hourly, daily), planned impression/spend target, actual delivered impressions/spend, pacing percentage, pacing status (on-pace, under-delivering, over-delivering), and recommended bid/budget adjustment signals. Sourced from Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk pacing APIs. Critical for operational campaign management and budget stewardship.'
}

Table performance.ivt_classification {
  ivt_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IVT classification record. Primary key for the IVT classification entity.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Invalid traffic detection and chargeback processing is tracked per brand for brand protection, billing dispute resolution, and brand-level fraud reporting. Multi-brand advertisers require brand-specif']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advertising campaign associated with this IVT event. Used for campaign-level IVT quality reporting.']
  click_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: IVT (Invalid Traffic) classifications apply to individual click events when event_type = \'click\'. The ivt_classification table currently has event_id (STRING) which is a generic polymorphic reference.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: IVT (Invalid Traffic) classifications apply to individual impression events when event_type = \'impression\'. The ivt_classification table currently has event_id (STRING) which is a generic polymorphic ']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media plan line item associated with this IVT event. Used for line-level IVT reporting and chargeback allocation.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media placement associated with this IVT event. Used for aggregating IVT metrics by placement.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: IVT detection performed by specialized fraud vendors (White Ops/HUMAN, DoubleVerify, IAS). Essential for vendor performance evaluation, chargeback dispute resolution, MRC accreditation tracking, and c']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_contract. Business justification: Vendor contracts often include IVT guarantees and chargeback terms. Linking IVT classifications to vendor contracts enables automated chargeback calculation, supplier quality scoring, and contract com']
  appeal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the rationale for appeal or dispute resolution outcome. Used for audit trails and process improvement.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Status of any appeal filed by the media vendor disputing the IVT classification. Tracks dispute resolution process.']
  behavioral_anomaly_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Score indicating deviation from normal user behavior patterns (e.g., click speed, navigation patterns, session duration). Higher scores indicate higher SIVT likelihood.']
  bot_signature_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user agent or behavior matched a known bot signature from IAB/ABC International Spiders and Bots List.']
  chargeback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IVT event qualifies for vendor chargeback per contract terms. Depends on IVT category, detection timing, and service level agreement.']
  chargeback_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the chargeback request for this IVT event. Tracks the chargeback lifecycle from identification through payment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|submitted|approved|rejected|paid|disputed|not_eligible — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  classification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IVT classification was performed. May differ from event timestamp for post-campaign analysis or re-classification.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score of the IVT classification, ranging from 0.0000 (no confidence) to 1.0000 (absolute certainty). Used for threshold-based filtering and chargeback negotiations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IVT classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invalid spend amount. Required for multi-currency campaign reconciliation.']
  data_center_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event originated from a known data center IP range. Data center traffic is classified as GIVT per MRC standards.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to detect invalid traffic. Informs confidence level and appeals process for disputed IVT classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rule_based|machine_learning|behavioral_analysis|device_fingerprinting|ip_reputation|user_agent_analysis|hybrid — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique device fingerprint hash used to identify repeat offenders and hijacked devices. Considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  event_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the impression or click event being classified for invalid traffic. Links to the raw delivery event log.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the impression or click event occurred. Used to correlate IVT classification with the original delivery event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of ad delivery event being classified for invalid traffic. Determines which IVT detection rules apply.']
  geographic_anomaly_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event originated from a geographic location inconsistent with campaign targeting or user profile. Common SIVT signal.']
  invalid_click_count varchar [note: 'Number of clicks classified as invalid for this event record. Used for click fraud analysis and CPC chargeback calculations.']
  invalid_impression_count varchar [note: 'Number of impressions classified as invalid for this event record. Typically 1 for event-level records; may be aggregated for batch classifications.']
  invalid_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of media spend attributed to this invalid traffic event. Used for financial reconciliation and vendor chargebacks.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address associated with the event. Used for data center detection, proxy identification, and geographic anomaly analysis. May be considered PII under GDPR.']
  ivt_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of invalid traffic classification per MRC standards. GIVT = General Invalid Traffic (routine filtration); SIVT = Sophisticated Invalid Traffic (advanced analysis required); Valid = legitimate traffic.']
  ivt_detection_source varchar [note: 'Source system or vendor that performed the IVT classification. TAG certified vendors carry higher audit credibility for MRC compliance.']
  ivt_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the event was classified as invalid traffic. True indicates IVT detected; False indicates valid traffic.']
  ivt_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of invalid traffic detected. Provides granular classification for fraud analysis, vendor disputes, and MRC compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bot_traffic|data_center_traffic|hijacked_device|adware|click_farm|cookie_stuffing|domain_spoofing|hidden_ads|incentivized_traffic|malware|proxy_traffic|automated_browsing|none — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mrc_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IVT detection and classification process followed MRC accredited standards. Required for audited campaign reporting.']
  tag_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IVT detection was performed by a TAG Certified Against Fraud vendor. TAG certification provides industry-recognized fraud prevention assurance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IVT classification record was last modified. Captures re-classifications, appeal outcomes, and chargeback status changes.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser user agent string from the event. Used for bot signature matching and device/browser anomaly detection.']

  Note: 'Invalid Traffic (IVT) classification record for impression and click events, capturing general IVT (GIVT) and sophisticated IVT (SIVT) flags per TAG and MRC standards. Stores IVT type (bot traffic, data center traffic, hijacked device, adware), IVT detection source (TAG certified vendor, DSP internal, ad server), IVT volume, and financial impact (invalid spend). Used for media quality assurance, vendor chargebacks, and MRC compliance reporting.'
}

Table performance.brand_safety_signal {
  brand_safety_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand safety signal record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific advertiser brand safety policy or ruleset that was applied to evaluate this signal.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Brand safety signals must be evaluated against the specific brand\'s safety standards, GARM floor settings, and brand guideline compliance rules. Different brands within same advertiser have different ']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the ad impression associated with this brand safety signal, typically provided by the ad server or DSP.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media placement where this brand safety signal was captured.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Brand safety signals provided by verification vendors (IAS, DoubleVerify, MOAT). Required for vendor scorecard tracking, contract compliance validation, signal quality assessment, and GARM framework a']
  adjacency_risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall adjacency risk level summarizing the brand safety assessment (None = no risk, Low/Medium/High = increasing risk levels, Critical = severe brand risk).']
  adult_content_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether adult or sexually explicit content was detected on the page (True = adult content detected, False = no adult content).']
  blocking_action varchar [note: 'Action taken based on the brand safety evaluation (Allowed = impression served, Blocked = impression prevented, Flagged = marked for review, Reviewed = manual review completed).']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the impression was blocked or flagged, including specific policy violations or risk factors identified.']
  brand_safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical brand safety score assigned by the vendor, typically ranging from 0-100 where higher scores indicate safer content for brand adjacency.']
  content_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the web page or content where the ad was served and evaluated for brand safety.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand safety signal record was first created in the system.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device on which the impression was served and evaluated (Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, Connected TV, Over-the-Top, Other).']
  environment_type varchar [note: 'Media environment where the impression was served (Web = website, App = mobile application, Video = video player, Audio = audio stream, DOOH = Digital Out-of-Home).']
  garm_brand_safety_floor varchar [note: 'GARM Brand Safety Floor classification indicating the minimum acceptable brand safety standard (Floor = universally acceptable, Low/Medium/High Risk = increasing levels of brand risk).']
  garm_suitability_tier varchar [note: 'GARM Brand Suitability tier classification (Tier 1 = most restrictive/brand-safe, Tier 4 = least restrictive/broader content acceptance).']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code where the impression was served (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  hate_speech_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether hate speech, discriminatory language, or extremist content was detected (True = hate speech detected, False = no hate speech).']
  iab_content_category varchar [note: 'Primary content category classification using IAB Content Taxonomy (e.g., IAB1 Arts & Entertainment, IAB3 Business, IAB7 Health & Fitness).']
  iab_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory classification within the IAB Content Taxonomy (e.g., IAB1-1 Books & Literature, IAB3-1 Advertising).']
  illegal_content_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether illegal activities, drugs, or other unlawful content was detected (True = illegal content detected, False = no illegal content).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code of the content where the ad was served (e.g., EN for English, ES for Spanish, FR for French).']
  misinformation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether misinformation, disinformation, or fake news content was detected (True = misinformation detected, False = no misinformation).']
  piracy_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether copyright infringement or pirated content was detected (True = piracy detected, False = no piracy).']
  pre_bid_signal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this signal was captured during pre-bid filtering (True) or post-impression verification (False).']
  signal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the brand safety signal was captured and evaluated, representing the real-world event time of the brand safety check.']
  spam_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether spam, malware, or fraudulent content was detected (True = spam detected, False = no spam).']
  terrorism_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether terrorism-related content or extremist propaganda was detected (True = terrorism content detected, False = no terrorism content).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand safety signal record was last modified or updated.']
  vendor_signal_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the brand safety vendor to this specific signal record, used for reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  viewability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the ad that was viewable according to MRC standards, expressed as a value between 0 and 100.']
  viewability_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the impression met MRC viewability standards (Viewable = met standard, Non-Viewable = did not meet standard, Unmeasurable = could not be measured).']
  violence_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether violent, graphic, or disturbing content was detected (True = violence detected, False = no violence).']

  Note: 'Brand safety and suitability signal record for each placement or impression, capturing content category classification (IAB Content Taxonomy), brand safety tier (GARM Brand Safety Floor, Suitability tiers 1-4), adjacency risk flags (hate speech, adult content, misinformation), and blocking action taken. Sourced from brand safety vendors (IAS, DoubleVerify, Oracle Moat) and DSP pre-bid filtering signals. Supports advertiser brand safety commitments and GARM compliance.'
}

Table performance.measurement_discrepancy {
  measurement_discrepancy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the measurement discrepancy record. Primary key for the measurement discrepancy entity.']
  buy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media buy associated with this discrepancy. Links to the broader media purchase agreement.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media placement affected by this discrepancy. Links to the specific ad placement where the measurement variance occurred.']
  vendor_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_contact. Business justification: Discrepancy resolution requires tracking which vendor contact is handling investigation. Essential for SLA adherence tracking, escalation management, response time measurement, and maintaining audit t']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_contract. Business justification: Measurement discrepancies trigger financial reconciliation per vendor contract terms (MRC thresholds, dispute resolution). Direct link enables automated breach detection, financial adjustment calculat']
  affected_date_end date [note: 'End date of the delivery data range affected by this discrepancy. Defines the conclusion of the time window where measurement variance or data quality issues occurred.']
  affected_date_start date [note: 'Start date of the delivery data range affected by this discrepancy. Defines the beginning of the time window where measurement variance or data quality issues occurred.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to detailed audit logs, supporting documentation, email threads, or system logs related to the discrepancy investigation. Supports compliance audits and forensic analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement discrepancy record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation time.']
  data_quality_check_status varchar [note: 'Result status of the automated data quality validation. Passed indicates data met quality criteria. Failed indicates data did not meet criteria and requires remediation. Warning indicates potential issue requiring review. Skipped indicates check was not executed.']
  data_quality_check_type varchar [note: 'For data quality check discrepancies, specifies the type of automated validation that failed. Completeness checks for missing required fields. Consistency checks for logical contradictions. Deduplication checks for duplicate events. Latency checks for delayed data arrival. Schema validation checks for structural conformance. Referential integrity checks for orphaned records.']
  data_quality_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the data quality issue. Critical indicates data unusable for billing or reporting. High indicates significant impact on accuracy. Medium indicates moderate impact requiring attention. Low indicates minor issue with minimal business impact.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discrepancy was first detected by automated monitoring systems or manual review. Represents the business event time of discovery.']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Absolute difference between primary and secondary counts. Calculated as primary_count minus secondary_count. Positive values indicate over-delivery by secondary source; negative values indicate under-delivery.']
  discrepancy_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage variance between primary and secondary counts. Calculated as (discrepancy_count / primary_count) * 100. Used to assess materiality against MRC 10 percent threshold.']
  discrepancy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discrepancy investigation and resolution workflow. Open indicates newly detected. Under investigation indicates active analysis. Resolved indicates root cause identified and remediation applied. Closed indicates final disposition complete. Escalated indicates elevated to senior management or vendor. Pending vendor indicates awaiting external party response.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discrepancy. Count variance represents differences between ad server and vendor reported metrics. Data quality check represents automated validation failures. Latency issue represents delayed data delivery. Schema validation represents structural data problems. Deduplication represents duplicate event detection. Completeness check represents missing data detection.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discrepancy impact in the media buy currency. Calculated based on the discrepancy count multiplied by the contracted CPM, CPC, or CPA rate. Represents the dollar value at stake requiring financial resolution.']
  financial_resolution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial remedy applied to compensate for the measurement discrepancy. Credit represents monetary refund from vendor. Makegood represents additional ad delivery at no cost. Write-off represents agency absorbing the variance. No adjustment represents variance accepted within tolerance. Pending represents resolution not yet determined.']
  investigation_assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team assigned to investigate and resolve the discrepancy. Typically a media operations analyst, data quality engineer, or account manager.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when formal investigation of the discrepancy began. Marks the transition from detection to active analysis.']
  metric_type varchar [note: 'The performance metric type where the discrepancy occurred. Impressions represents ad delivery count. Clicks represents user interaction count. Conversions represents completed actions. Viewable impressions represents MRC-compliant viewability count. Video completions represents full video view count. Engagements represents rich media interaction count.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this measurement discrepancy record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to status, resolution, or investigation findings.']
  mrc_threshold_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the discrepancy percentage exceeds the MRC 10 percent materiality threshold. True indicates breach requiring formal investigation and potential financial adjustment. False indicates variance within acceptable tolerance.']
  primary_count varchar [note: 'The metric count reported by the primary source system (ad server of record). Represents the baseline measurement for discrepancy calculation.']
  remediation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action applied to resolve the discrepancy or data quality issue. Documents data reprocessing, manual adjustments, system configuration changes, vendor notifications, or process improvements implemented.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discrepancy was resolved and remediation action completed. Marks the conclusion of the investigation and correction workflow.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the identified or suspected root cause of the discrepancy. Tag latency represents delayed pixel firing. Filtering methodology represents different data cleansing rules. Timezone mismatch represents reporting period alignment issues. IVT filtering delta represents invalid traffic detection differences. Ad blocking represents client-side blocking. Viewability criteria represents MRC viewability standard interpretation differences. Attribution window represents conversion lookback period differences. Sampling methodology represents statistical sampling variance. Technical error represents system malfunction. Unknown represents unresolved cause. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tag_latency|filtering_methodology|timezone_mismatch|ivt_filtering_delta|ad_blocking|viewability_criteria|attribution_window|sampling_methodology|technical_error|unknown — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause analysis findings. Documents the investigation process, evidence reviewed, technical factors identified, and conclusion reached regarding why the discrepancy occurred.']
  secondary_count varchar [note: 'The metric count reported by the secondary source system (vendor or publisher). Represents the comparison measurement for discrepancy calculation.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the discrepancy represents a breach of contractual service level agreement for data quality, delivery accuracy, or reporting timeliness. True indicates SLA breach requiring escalation. False indicates within SLA tolerance.']
  source_system_primary varchar [note: 'Name of the primary measurement source system reporting the baseline count. Typically the ad server of record such as Google Campaign Manager 360 or The Trade Desk.']
  source_system_secondary varchar [note: 'Name of the secondary measurement source system reporting the comparison count. Typically the media vendor, publisher ad server, or third-party verification vendor such as Nielsen or Comscore.']
  vendor_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the media vendor or publisher was formally notified of the discrepancy. True indicates notification sent. False indicates internal-only issue or notification not yet sent.']
  vendor_response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing the vendor or publisher explanation, justification, or proposed resolution for the discrepancy. Documents external party perspective and commitments.']
  vendor_response_received_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the media vendor or publisher provided a formal response to the discrepancy notification. True indicates response received. False indicates no response or response pending.']

  Note: 'Unified data quality and measurement discrepancy management record for all performance data ingested from ad servers, DSPs, and third-party measurement vendors. Encompasses two operational workflows: (1) Automated data quality checks — completeness, consistency, deduplication, latency, and schema validations applied to incoming delivery data, with check type, severity, status (passed/failed/warning), affected source system, affected date range, and remediation action taken; (2) Formal count discrepancies — variances between ad server delivery counts (Google Campaign Manager 360) and media vendor/publisher reported counts, with discrepancy percentage, MRC 10% threshold breach flag, investigation status, root cause classification (tag latency, filtering methodology, timezone mismatch, IVT filtering delta), and financial resolution (credit, makegood, write-off). Single source of truth for all measurement quality issues. Supports media billing reconciliation, vendor accountability, MRC compliance audits, and operational data quality SLAs.'
}

Table performance.attribution_model {
  attribution_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the attribution model configuration. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser for whom this attribution model is configured.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Attribution models provided by third-party platforms (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Neustar, Visual IQ). Needed for licensing management, support escalation, model validation, platform fee alloca']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved this attribution model configuration for activation. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this attribution model configuration was approved for activation. Null if not yet approved.']
  attribution_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the attribution model configuration, including rationale, use cases, and any special rules or exclusions.']
  attribution_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the attribution model configuration. Active models are applied to conversion events; inactive models are retained for historical reference; draft models are under development; archived models are deprecated; testing models are in A/B comparison.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which marketing channels this attribution model includes in the customer journey. May be a comma-separated list of channel codes (e.g., \'display,search,social,video\') or \'all\' for universal application.']
  click_through_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a click during which a conversion can be attributed to that click event. Standard values range from 1 to 90 days.']
  comparison_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this attribution model to an A/B testing or incrementality testing group for model comparison workflows. Null if not part of a comparison test.']
  conversion_event_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which conversion events this attribution model applies to. May be a comma-separated list of conversion event types (e.g., \'purchase,lead_form,signup\') or \'all\' for universal application.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was first created in the system.']
  cross_channel_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attribution model tracks and attributes conversions across multiple marketing channels (display, search, social, video, email, OOH, CTV, etc.).']
  cross_device_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attribution model uses cross-device identity graph to track and attribute conversions across multiple devices (desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV) for the same user.']
  data_driven_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'For data-driven (algorithmic) attribution models, the version identifier of the machine learning algorithm or model used to calculate fractional credit distribution. Null for rule-based models.']
  data_driven_minimum_conversions varchar [note: 'For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of conversions required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.']
  data_driven_minimum_paths varchar [note: 'For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of unique customer journey paths required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.']
  data_driven_training_end_date date [note: 'For data-driven attribution models, the end date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.']
  data_driven_training_start_date date [note: 'For data-driven attribution models, the start date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this attribution model configuration ceases to be active. Null for open-ended models.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this attribution model configuration becomes active and begins applying to conversion events.']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days to look back in the customer journey when attributing conversions. Defines the outer boundary for all touchpoint consideration.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or identifier for the attribution model used in reporting and system integration.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name for the attribution model configuration (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Last-Click Model\', \'Brand Awareness Linear Model\').']
  model_type varchar [note: 'The type of attribution methodology applied: last-click (100% credit to final touchpoint), first-click (100% credit to initial touchpoint), linear (equal credit across all touchpoints), time-decay (exponentially increasing credit toward conversion), position-based (U-shaped: 40% first, 40% last, 20% distributed), or data-driven (algorithmic/machine-learning-based credit distribution).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this attribution model configuration.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was last modified.']
  position_based_first_touch_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the first touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.']
  position_based_last_touch_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the last touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.']
  position_based_middle_touch_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight distributed equally among all middle touchpoints (e.g., 0.2000 for 20% total). Null for non-position-based models.']
  reprocessing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether historical conversion events within the attribution window need to be reprocessed due to changes in this model configuration.']
  reprocessing_start_date date [note: 'The earliest date from which historical conversion events should be reprocessed when model configuration changes. Null if no reprocessing is required.']
  time_decay_half_life_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'For time-decay attribution models, the number of days at which a touchpoint receives half the credit of the most recent touchpoint. Defines the exponential decay rate. Null for non-time-decay models.']
  view_through_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after an impression (view) during which a conversion can be attributed to that impression event, even without a click. Standard values range from 1 to 30 days.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this attribution model configuration.']

  Note: 'Configuration master defining conversion attribution models, rules, and parameters for each advertiser and campaign. Stores model type (last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, position-based, data-driven/algorithmic, custom), attribution windows (view-through duration, click-through duration), cross-device graph enablement, cross-channel scope, lookback window constraints, fractional credit distribution weights, model training metadata (for data-driven models), and activation status with effective date range. Governs how conversion_event records distribute credit across campaign touchpoints. Model changes trigger reprocessing of historical conversions within the affected attribution window. Supports A/B model comparison and incrementality testing workflows.'
}

Table performance.performance_kpi_target {
  performance_kpi_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance KPI target record. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campaign for which this KPI target is defined. Links to the campaign master entity.']
  io_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: KPI targets often set at IO line level per vendor contracts (guaranteed viewability, CTR floors, completion rates). Needed to track contractual performance guarantees, trigger makegood clauses, and ev']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item within a media plan or insertion order for which this KPI target applies. Nullable if target is set at campaign or placement level.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific media placement for which this KPI target is defined. Nullable if target is set at campaign or line item level.']
  alert_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automated alerts should be triggered when performance deviates from target thresholds. True = alerts active, False = monitoring only without alerts.']
  alert_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which performance is evaluated against thresholds and alerts are generated. Real-time for critical KPIs, daily or weekly for less time-sensitive metrics.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved this KPI target for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KPI target was approved for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'The attribution model used for conversion-based KPIs (CPA, ROAS, conversion_rate). Defines how credit is assigned across touchpoints in the customer journey.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KPI target record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  is_contractual_commitment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this KPI target represents a contractual commitment to the client (typically from Insertion Order or Statement of Work). True = contractual obligation, False = internal goal or aspiration.']
  kpi_type varchar [note: 'The type of performance metric being targeted. CPM = Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions), CPC = Cost Per Click, CPA = Cost Per Acquisition, CTR = Click-Through Rate, ROAS = Return on Ad Spend, VTR = View-Through Rate, GRP = Gross Rating Point, TRP = Target Rating Point, SOV = Share of Voice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|CTR|ROAS|VTR|viewability_rate|GRP|TRP|SOV|conversion_rate|engagement_rate|completion_rate|brand_lift — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after ad exposure during which conversions are attributed to the campaign for conversion-based KPIs. Standard windows are 1, 7, 14, 30, or 90 days.']
  lower_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this KPI. Performance below this threshold triggers alerts or corrective actions. Calculated as target_value minus tolerance band or set explicitly.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of how this KPI is measured and calculated, including data sources, attribution models, and calculation logic. Ensures consistency in performance evaluation.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
