  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KPI target record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or special instructions related to this KPI target. May include client expectations, historical context, or measurement caveats.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this KPI target. Critical targets require immediate attention when thresholds are breached; low priority targets are monitored but may not trigger immediate action.']
  target_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary KPI targets (CPM, CPC, CPA, ROAS). Nullable for non-monetary KPIs.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this KPI target record, used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  target_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.']
  target_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.']
  target_source varchar [note: 'The origin or basis for this KPI target. Insertion Order (IO) commitment represents contractual obligation, client brief represents client expectation, internal benchmark represents agency standard, historical average represents past performance baseline.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI target. Draft = under definition, active = in measurement period, suspended = temporarily paused, achieved = target met, missed = target not met, expired = measurement period ended.']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the target value. Currency for cost-based KPIs (CPM, CPC, CPA), percentage for rate-based KPIs (CTR, VTR, viewability_rate), ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP, count for absolute metrics.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric target value for the specified KPI. Interpretation depends on kpi_type (e.g., dollar amount for CPM/CPC/CPA, percentage for CTR/VTR/viewability_rate, ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP).']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage from the target value before triggering alerts or escalations. For example, 10.00 means ±10% from target is acceptable. Used for pacing alerts and performance monitoring.']
  upper_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this KPI (relevant for cost-based metrics where exceeding target is negative). Performance above this threshold triggers alerts. Calculated as target_value plus tolerance band or set explicitly.']
  viewability_standard varchar [note: 'The viewability measurement standard applied for viewability_rate KPI targets. MRC standard = 50% of pixels in view for 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video). GroupM may have stricter requirements.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Operational KPI target record defining the planned performance benchmarks for each campaign, line item, or placement. Stores KPI type (CPM, CPC, CPA, CTR, ROAS, VTR, viewability rate, GRP, SOV), target value, target period, tolerance band (acceptable variance percentage), and source (IO commitment, client brief, internal benchmark). Used for pacing alerts, delivery_pacing comparisons, and client performance reporting. Distinct from finance budgets: this is the performance measurement target, not the financial spend target.'
}

Table performance.video_completion_event {
  video_completion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the video completion event record. Primary key for this event log entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advertising campaign this video completion event belongs to.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the video creative asset that was played. Links to the specific video ad unit served.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this completion event to the parent video ad impression that was served. References the initial ad serving event.']
  media_placement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media placement where this video ad was served. Links to the specific inventory position purchased.']
  publisher_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the publisher or content owner on whose property the video ad was served. Links to the supply-side partner.']
  ad_server varchar [note: 'Name of the ad server platform that served the video ad and captured this completion event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Innovid, Sizmek). Used for data quality and discrepancy analysis.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Web browser or app environment where the video ad was served (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, in-app). Null for CTV (Connected TV) and OTT (Over-the-Top) environments.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the video that was viewed, calculated as (time_to_completion_seconds / video_duration_seconds) * 100. Supports VTR (View-Through Rate) calculation.']
  completion_quartile varchar [note: 'The quartile milestone reached in video playback: start (0%), first_quartile (25%), midpoint (50%), third_quartile (75%), or complete (100%). Used to calculate VTR (View-Through Rate) and engagement depth.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Type of network connection used during video playback: wifi, cellular (3G/4G/5G), wired (ethernet), or unknown. Impacts video quality and completion rates.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'IAB Content Taxonomy category of the content adjacent to the video ad (e.g., Sports, News, Entertainment). Used for contextual targeting and brand safety verification.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device on which the video ad was viewed: desktop, mobile (smartphone), tablet, CTV (Connected TV), console (gaming console), or other. Critical for cross-device campaign analysis.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the video completion event was captured by the ad server or video player, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City where the video ad was viewed. Used for hyper-local campaign performance analysis and geo-targeting verification.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the video ad was viewed (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for geo-targeting verification and regional performance analysis.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the video ad was viewed (e.g., California, Ontario, Bavaria). Used for sub-national geo-targeting analysis.']
  ip_address_hashed varchar [note: 'Hashed IP address of the device that viewed the video ad. Used for fraud detection and geo-verification while maintaining privacy compliance.']
  is_autoplay boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad started playing automatically without user initiation (True) or required user action to start (False). Impacts viewability and engagement quality.']
  is_fullscreen boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video was playing in fullscreen mode at the time of the completion event (True) or in standard embedded player (False).']
  is_skipped boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the user actively skipped the video ad before completion (True) or allowed it to play through the recorded quartile (False).']
  is_sound_on boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad played with sound enabled (True) or muted (False). Critical metric for video engagement quality and IO (Insertion Order) compliance.']
  is_viewable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad met MRC (Media Rating Council) viewability standards at the time of the completion event: 50% of pixels in-view for 2+ consecutive seconds. Used for viewable VTR (View-Through Rate) calculation.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device where the video ad was viewed (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, tvOS, Roku OS). Used for device-level performance segmentation.']
  player_height_pixels varchar [note: 'Height of the video player in pixels at the time of the completion event. Used for precise viewability and player size analysis.']
  player_size varchar [note: 'Size classification of the video player at the time of the event: small (<400x300), medium (400x300 to 640x480), large (>640x480 but not fullscreen), or fullscreen. Impacts viewability measurement.']
  player_width_pixels varchar [note: 'Width of the video player in pixels at the time of the completion event. Used for precise viewability and player size analysis.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the user browsing or viewing session during which this video completion event occurred. Used for session-level engagement analysis.']
  site_domain varchar [note: 'Domain name of the website or app where the video ad was served (e.g., youtube.com, espn.com). Used for brand safety and contextual analysis.']
  skip_offset_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of seconds into the video when the skip button became available to the user. Null if video was non-skippable.']
  time_to_completion_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual elapsed time in seconds from video start to the recorded completion quartile. May differ from video_duration_seconds if user paused, rewound, or fast-forwarded.']
  user_id_hashed varchar [note: 'Hashed or pseudonymized identifier for the user who viewed the video ad. Used for frequency capping and cross-device attribution while maintaining privacy compliance. Must comply with GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act).']
  vast_version varchar [note: 'Version of the VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) specification used to serve this video ad (e.g., 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2). Indicates ad serving protocol capabilities and tracking fidelity.']
  video_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the video creative in seconds. Used to calculate completion percentage and time-based engagement metrics.']
  video_format varchar [note: 'Format type of the video ad: linear (pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll that interrupts content), non_linear (overlay ads that run concurrently with content), or companion (static/rich media ads displayed alongside video).']
  video_position varchar [note: 'Position of the video ad within the content stream: pre_roll (before content), mid_roll (during content), or post_roll (after content). Applies to linear video formats only.']
  viewable_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the video player that was in the viewable area of the browser viewport at the time of the completion event. Used for granular viewability analysis beyond binary MRC threshold.']
  vpaid_version varchar [note: 'Version of the VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition) specification used for interactive video ads (e.g., 1.0, 2.0). Null for standard VAST-only video ads.']

  Note: 'Video ad completion event record capturing quartile-level video engagement signals (25%, 50%, 75%, 100% completion) per video ad impression. Stores video ad unit identifier, completion quartile reached, autoplay flag, sound-on flag, skip action (skipped, completed), player size, and VTR (View-Through Rate) computation inputs. Sourced from VAST/VPAID event signals from video ad servers. Supports VTR-based IO commitments and video campaign performance measurement.'
}

Table performance.rtb_bid_event {
  rtb_bid_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RTB bid event record. Primary key for the bid event entity.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the advertiser client on whose behalf this bid was submitted.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising campaign associated with this bid event.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the creative asset that would be served if this bid wins the auction.']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: When an RTB bid wins the auction, an impression is served and recorded as an impression_event. Adding impression_event_id FK (nullable, populated only when win_indicator = true) links the bid event to']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the media plan line item or insertion order line associated with this bid event.']
  programmatic_deal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the programmatic deal if this bid participated in a private marketplace (PMP) or programmatic guaranteed (PG) deal. Null for open auction bids.']
  publisher_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the publisher or content owner whose inventory is being auctioned. May be SSP-specific or a standardized publisher ID.']
  app_bundle varchar [note: 'The bundle or package name of the mobile application where the ad will be served (e.g., com.cnn.mobile.android). Null for web inventory.']
  app_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the mobile application (e.g., CNN Mobile App). Null for web inventory.']
  auction_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the programmatic auction instance. Multiple bid events may share the same auction ID when multiple DSPs participate.']
  audience_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audience segment IDs that matched this bid request. Represents the targeting criteria applied by the DSP (e.g., demographic, behavioral, contextual segments).']
  bid_price_cpm decimal(18,6) [note: 'The bid price submitted by the DSP expressed in CPM (cost per thousand impressions). Represents the maximum amount the advertiser is willing to pay for this impression.']
  bid_request_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the bid request received from the SSP or ad exchange. Links this bid event to the originating auction request.']
  bid_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Time taken by the DSP to respond to the bid request, measured in milliseconds. Critical for auction participation and timeout analysis.']
  bid_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the bid event indicating whether the bid won the auction, lost, was not submitted, timed out, or encountered an error.']
  bid_strategy varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the bidding strategy applied by the DSP (e.g., maximize conversions, target CPA, target ROAS, fixed CPM).']
  bid_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the bid was submitted to the SSP or ad exchange. Represents the real-world event time of the bid participation.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Web browser used by the user (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge). Null for in-app inventory.']
  clearing_price_cpm decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual price paid for the impression if the bid won the auction, expressed in CPM. May be lower than bid price in second-price auctions. Null if bid lost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bid event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for bid and clearing prices (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Type of programmatic deal: open auction (no deal), PMP (private marketplace), programmatic guaranteed, or preferred deal.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device where the ad impression will be served: desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV (Connected TV), or set-top box.']
  domain varchar [note: 'The domain of the website where the ad impression will be served (e.g., cnn.com, espn.com). Used for brand safety and supply path transparency.']
  dsp_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the DSP platform that submitted the bid (e.g., The Trade Desk, DV360, Amazon DSP).']
  floor_price_cpm decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum bid price set by the publisher or SSP for this impression, expressed in CPM. Bids below this floor are automatically rejected.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name representing the user\'s geographic location (e.g., New York, London, Toronto).']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the geographic location of the user (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  geo_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code representing the user\'s geographic location. May be truncated for privacy (e.g., 90210, M5H 2N2).']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code representing the user\'s geographic location (e.g., CA for California, ON for Ontario).']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Type of digital inventory being auctioned: web (desktop browser), mobile web, mobile app, CTV (Connected TV), or DOOH (Digital Out-of-Home).']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, tvOS).']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Ad format type for the placement: banner (display), video, native, or audio.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the bid was rejected or lost the auction. Includes floor price not met, brand safety violation, frequency cap exceeded, budget exhausted, geo/device restrictions, inventory quality issues, creative approval pending, or domain/app/category blocks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: floor_price|brand_safety|frequency_cap|budget_cap|geo_restriction|device_restriction|inventory_quality|creative_approval|domain_block|app_block|category_block|none — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ssp_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SSP or ad exchange that sent the bid request and conducted the auction (e.g., Google Ad Manager, Magnite, PubMatic, OpenX).']
  supply_path_cost decimal(18,6) [note: 'Total cost incurred through the supply path (SSP fees, exchange fees, reseller margins) expressed as a percentage or absolute amount. Used for supply path optimization (SPO) analysis.']
  user_id_hashed varchar [note: 'Hashed or anonymized user identifier passed by the SSP for frequency capping and audience targeting. Complies with privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).']
  win_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bid won the auction (True) or lost (False). Derived from bid_status for simplified win rate analysis.']

  Note: 'Real-Time Bidding (RTB) auction participation event record capturing programmatic bid activity from DSP platforms (The Trade Desk, DV360). Stores bid request identifier, auction identifier, bid price (CPM), clearing price, win/loss status, bid rejection reason (floor price, brand safety, frequency cap), supply path details (SSP, publisher, domain, app bundle), audience segment matched, deal identifier (PMP/PG/open auction), and bid strategy parameters applied. Provides the raw programmatic buying event stream for win rate analysis, spend efficiency optimization, supply path transparency (SPO), and auction-level cost reconciliation against impression_event delivery records.'
}

Table performance.pixel_deployment {
  pixel_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the pixel_deployment association']
  tech_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the tech partner platform through which this pixel is deployed']
  tracking_pixel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the tracking pixel being deployed through this tech partner platform']
  api_version varchar [note: 'Version of the tech partner API used for this pixel deployment. Critical for compatibility tracking and upgrade planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pixel deployment record was created in the system.']
  custom_parameters varchar [note: 'Platform-specific custom parameters or configuration settings for this pixel deployment. Varies by tech partner platform capabilities.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date when this pixel was deployed through this specific tech partner platform. Tracks when the integration was established.']
  deployment_environment varchar [note: 'Environment context for this pixel-platform deployment. Tracks whether this is a production integration or test deployment.']
  error_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'Calculated error rate (0.00000 to 1.00000) for pixel fires through this tech partner platform over the monitoring window. Key vendor performance metric.']
  fire_status varchar [note: 'Most recent fire status for this pixel through this tech partner platform. Used for real-time health monitoring.']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the pixel deployment through this tech partner platform. Used for health monitoring and troubleshooting.']
  last_fire_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful pixel fire through this tech partner platform. Used to detect stale or broken integrations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this pixel deployment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the deployment relationship between tracking_pixel and tech_partner. It captures the operational deployment of tracking pixels through specific technology partner platforms (tag managers, DMPs, analytics platforms, ad servers). Each record links one tracking pixel to one tech partner platform with deployment-specific attributes including integration status, API version, deployment environment, custom parameters, and error monitoring metrics. This is the operational record of pixel-platform integration health and deployment configuration.. Existence Justification: In advertising operations, tracking pixels are deployed through multiple technology partner platforms (tag managers like Google Tag Manager, DMPs, CDPs, ad servers, analytics platforms), and each tech partner platform manages the deployment of multiple pixels across their client base. Agencies actively manage these pixel-platform deployments as operational integration records, monitoring deployment health, API compatibility, error rates, and troubleshooting integration issues per pixel-platform combination.'
}

Table performance.model_platform_configuration {
  model_platform_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this attribution model platform configuration record. Primary key.']
  attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the attribution model configuration being deployed on this platform']
  tech_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the technology platform partner hosting this attribution model']
  api_integration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the API integration between the attribution model and this specific tech partner platform. Values: active, inactive, degraded, maintenance, failed, pending. Used for operational monitoring and alerting.']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this model-platform configuration. Values: active (in production use), inactive (disabled), testing (pilot/QA), deprecated (scheduled for removal). Governs whether this configuration is used for live attribution processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this model-platform configuration record was first created in the system.']
  data_feed_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which conversion and touchpoint data is synchronized between the tech partner platform and the attribution model processing engine. Values: real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, on-demand. Impacts attribution latency and reporting freshness.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful data synchronization between the tech partner platform and the attribution model. Used for monitoring integration health and data freshness.']
  license_tier varchar [note: 'Licensing tier or service level for this specific model-platform combination. Values: basic, standard, premium, enterprise. Determines feature availability, data volume limits, and support entitlements.']
  model_deployment_date date [note: 'Date when this attribution model was first deployed and activated on this specific tech partner platform. Used for tracking model lifecycle and historical analysis.']
  platform_version varchar [note: 'Version of the technology platform on which this attribution model is deployed (e.g., Google Analytics 4 v2.1, Adobe Analytics 2.8). Critical for compatibility tracking and troubleshooting.']
  support_sla_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier for technical support and issue resolution for this model-platform configuration. Values: bronze, silver, gold, platinum. Defines response times and escalation procedures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this model-platform configuration record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the deployment configuration between attribution_model and tech_partner. It captures platform-specific implementation details, API integration health, licensing arrangements, and operational parameters that exist only when a specific attribution model is deployed on a specific technology platform. Each record links one attribution model to one tech partner platform with deployment metadata, integration status, and service-level tracking.. Existence Justification: In advertising operations, attribution models are deployed across multiple technology platforms (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Neustar, The Trade Desk) simultaneously to support multi-platform measurement strategies. Each tech partner platform can host multiple attribution models for different advertisers or campaign strategies. The business actively manages platform-specific configurations including API integration health, data feed frequencies, licensing tiers, and support SLAs for each model-platform combination.'
}

Table performance.session {
  session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for session']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advertising campaign associated with this session.']
  originating_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on session (originating_session_id)']
  placement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ad placement where the session originated.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Name of the browser used during the session.']
  browser_version varchar [note: 'Version of the browser used during the session.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which the session was initiated.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of ad clicks recorded during the session.']
  conversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of conversions attributed to the session.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions attributed to the session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for conversion value.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used during the session.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used during the session.']
  exit_page_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the last page viewed before the session ended.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City where the session originated.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the session location.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state where the session originated.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of ad impressions served during the session.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device during the session.']
  is_bot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the session was identified as bot traffic.']
  is_viewable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad impressions in the session met viewability standards.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the first page viewed in the session.']
  medium varchar [note: 'The medium through which the session was acquired (e.g., cpc, organic, email).']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device used during the session.']
  page_views varchar [note: 'Total number of pages viewed during the session.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the referring page that led to this session.']
  screen_resolution varchar [note: 'Screen resolution of the device during the session (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the session measured in seconds.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the session ended.']
  session_key varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the session, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the session started.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the session.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the session based on traffic source type.']
  source varchar [note: 'The origin or source of the session traffic (e.g., google, facebook, direct).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was last updated in the system.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser user agent string captured during the session.']
  user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user associated with this session.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table performance.auction {
  auction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for auction']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser whose creative won the auction and will be served.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign associated with the winning bid in this auction.']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for a private marketplace (PMP) deal or programmatic guaranteed deal. Null for open auction inventory.']
  fallback_auction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on auction (fallback_auction_id)']
  impression_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the ad impression that resulted from this auction. Links auction to downstream delivery and performance events.']
  line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item within the campaign that participated in or won this auction.']
  placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ad placement or inventory slot that was auctioned. Represents the specific location where the ad will be displayed.']
  publisher_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the publisher or content owner providing the ad inventory being auctioned.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Creative format of the ad unit being auctioned. Determines technical specifications and user experience.']
  ad_size varchar [note: 'Dimensions of the ad unit in pixels (e.g., 300x250, 728x90). Standard IAB ad unit sizes or custom dimensions.']
  auction_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the auction was resolved and a winning bid was selected or the auction was closed without a winner.']
  auction_external_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the auction, typically provided by the ad exchange or supply-side platform (SSP). Used for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.']
  auction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the auction event. Tracks progression from initiation through bid collection to final resolution or failure.']
  auction_timeout_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for the auction to complete before timing out. Typical values range from 100-500ms.']
  auction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the auction event was initiated by the ad exchange or SSP. Represents the real-world business event time for auction start.']
  auction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the auction mechanism used for ad inventory pricing. First-price auctions award to highest bidder at their bid price; second-price auctions award at second-highest bid price plus increment.']
  bid_count varchar [note: 'Total number of bids received during the auction from all participating demand-side platforms (DSPs) and bidders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this auction record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this auction (winning bid, floor price).']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the end-user device (IDFA, AAID, or hashed identifier). Used for frequency capping and attribution. May be pseudonymized or hashed.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the device on which the ad impression will be displayed. Critical for targeting and performance analysis.']
  exchange_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the ad exchange or supply-side platform (SSP) that conducted the auction.']
  floor_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable bid price set by the publisher or exchange for this auction. Bids below this threshold are rejected.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Pre-bid fraud risk score (0-100) indicating likelihood of invalid traffic (IVT). Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk. Provided by fraud detection vendors.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name derived from the user\'s location. Used for hyper-local targeting and performance analysis.']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from the user\'s IP address or device location. Used for geographic targeting and reporting.']
  geo_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code derived from the user\'s location. Used for precise geographic targeting and demographic inference.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region within the country derived from the user\'s location. Used for sub-national geographic targeting.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the end-user device at the time of the auction. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Used for geo-targeting and fraud detection. May be truncated for privacy compliance.']
  site_category varchar [note: 'IAB content category classification of the publisher site or app. Used for contextual targeting and brand safety.']
  site_domain varchar [note: 'Domain name of the website or app where the ad will be displayed. Used for brand safety and contextual targeting.']
  supply_chain varchar [note: 'Serialized ads.txt or sellers.json supply chain transparency data. Identifies all intermediaries in the ad transaction chain for fraud prevention.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this auction record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user agent string identifying the software environment of the end user. Used for device detection and compatibility.']
  viewability_prediction decimal(5,2) [note: 'Pre-bid predicted probability (0-100%) that the impression will meet viewability standards (MRC: 50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).']
  winning_bid_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'The monetary value of the winning bid in the auction. Represents the clearing price paid by the advertiser for the impression.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for auction. Referenced by auction_id.'
}

Table brand.brand_profile {
  brand_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the brand profile record. Serves as the primary key and system-of-record anchor for all brand-level entities within the brand domain.']
  approved_media_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Brand profiles maintain approved media supplier lists for brand safety compliance and media buying operations. Critical for vendor approval workflows and ensuring brand-safe media placements across ca']
  active_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing the markets where the brand is currently active and running campaigns. Supports geo-targeting in The Trade Desk, media planning in Mediaocean Prisma, and multi-market reporting.']
  agency_onboard_date date [note: 'Date on which the brand was formally onboarded to the agency and the brand profile record was activated in the system of record. Marks the start of the agency-client brand management relationship.']
  asa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand\'s advertising materials and practices are compliant with Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) regulations. Required for UK and international market campaign approvals.']
  brand_archetype varchar [note: 'Jungian brand archetype classification (e.g., Hero, Caregiver, Explorer, Sage, Outlaw, Creator, Ruler, Magician, Innocent, Everyman, Lover, Jester) that defines the brand\'s personality framework. Anchors tone of voice, creative direction, and brand strategy positioning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hero|Caregiver|Explorer|Sage|Outlaw|Creator|Ruler|Magician|Innocent|Everyman|Lover|Jester — promote to reference product]']
  brand_awareness_baseline_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Baseline unaided brand awareness percentage among the target audience at the time of agency engagement or last formal brand health study. Used as the benchmark for measuring awareness lift from campaign activity. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the brand across agency systems, including Mediaocean Prisma and Google Campaign Manager 360. Used as the business key for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.']
  brand_guidelines_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the master brand guidelines document stored in Workfront DAM. Serves as the authoritative source for all brand identity standards including logo usage, color palette, typography, and tone of voice rules.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The primary commercial name of the brand as it appears in market-facing communications, campaign briefs, and agency deliverables. Serves as the human-readable identity label for the brand profile.']
  brand_safety_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand\'s content adjacency sensitivity level for programmatic media buying. Determines the brand safety targeting parameters applied in The Trade Desk and Google Campaign Manager 360 to prevent ad placement near inappropriate content.']
  brand_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the brand profile record within the agency\'s system of record. Controls whether the brand is eligible for new campaign assignments, media planning, and creative production activities.']
  brand_tier varchar [note: 'Market positioning tier of the brand reflecting its price-point and competitive positioning strategy. Informs media planning, creative tone, and channel selection within campaign strategy and planning processes.']
  ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand has opted out of the sale of consumer personal information under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA). Restricts certain data sharing and targeting activities for California audiences.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand profile record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage, compliance reporting, and record lifecycle management.']
  easa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand\'s advertising practices comply with European Advertising Standards Alliance (EASA) cross-border complaint handling and self-regulatory standards. Required for European market campaign activation.']
  ftc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand\'s advertising disclosures, endorsements, and claims comply with Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Advertising Practices Division guidelines. Required for US market campaign activation.']
  gdpr_data_use_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand has provided consent for audience data usage in programmatic targeting and analytics under GDPR Article 6 lawful basis requirements. Governs data activation in The Trade Desk and DMP/CDP integrations.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The primary industry sector classification of the brand per IAB Content Taxonomy (e.g., Automotive, Consumer Packaged Goods, Financial Services, Retail, Technology). Used for competitive conflict detection, audience segmentation, and Nielsen Ad Intel competitive monitoring alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Automotive|Consumer Packaged Goods|Financial Services|Retail|Technology|Healthcare|Entertainment|Travel|Telecommunications|Food & Beverage — promote to reference product]']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which the brand was officially launched in its primary market. Used for brand age calculations, lifecycle stage validation, and historical competitive Share of Voice (SOV) analysis via Nielsen Ad Intel.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the brand\'s commercial lifecycle. Drives investment strategy, media mix recommendations, and KPI benchmarking. Aligned with brand health monitoring and Nielsen Ad Intel competitive tracking.']
  local_brand_name varchar [note: 'Market-specific or locale-specific variant of the brand name used in regional campaigns, translations, or co-branded executions. Supports multi-market brand management and localization workflows.']
  logo_asset_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the master brand logo file stored in the Workfront Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Provides a canonical pointer to the approved logo for use in campaign trafficking, ad serving via Google Campaign Manager 360, and creative production.']
  market_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand\'s market investment tier indicating the scale and priority of media investment. Tier 1 represents highest-investment markets; Tier 3 represents emerging or test markets. Drives media budget allocation in Mediaocean Prisma.']
  messaging_framework_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the brand\'s approved messaging framework document in Workfront DAM. Defines key messages, proof points, and value propositions used across campaign strategy, PR communications, and creative development.']
  nielsen_brand_code varchar [note: 'External brand identifier assigned by Nielsen Ad Intel for competitive monitoring, audience measurement, and Gross Rating Point (GRP) tracking. Used to reconcile agency brand records with Nielsen\'s competitive intelligence database.']
  nps_baseline_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Baseline Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the brand at the time of agency engagement or last formal brand health assessment. Serves as the reference benchmark for measuring brand health improvement over the engagement period. Range: -100.00 to 100.00.']
  parent_company_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the parent organization that owns or controls the brand. Used for competitive conflict checks, client hierarchy management, and consolidated reporting across brand portfolios.']
  primary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for the brand\'s primary color as defined in the brand guidelines. Used by Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) for asset production and Workfront DAM for brand compliance checks.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the brand\'s primary or home market. Used as the default market context for brand health benchmarking, SOV tracking via Nielsen Ad Intel, and regulatory compliance flag evaluation.']
  primary_typeface varchar [note: 'Name of the primary typeface (font family) specified in the brand\'s typography standards. Governs headline and body copy font usage across all creative executions produced in Adobe Creative Cloud.']
  secondary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for the brand\'s secondary color as defined in the brand guidelines. Supports multi-color palette management in Adobe Creative Cloud and Workfront DAM for creative production workflows.']
  secondary_typeface varchar [note: 'Name of the secondary or supporting typeface specified in the brand\'s typography standards. Used for subheadings, captions, and supporting copy in creative executions.']
  sentiment_baseline varchar [note: 'Baseline brand sentiment classification derived from social listening, PR monitoring, and consumer research at the time of agency engagement. Anchors sentiment tracking and PR communications strategy.']
  sov_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether competitive Share of Voice (SOV) tracking is active for this brand via Nielsen Ad Intel. When enabled, competitive monitoring data is ingested and linked to brand health metrics.']
  tag_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand holds Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) certification for brand safety, anti-fraud, and malware protection in digital advertising. Influences publisher allowlist/blocklist configuration in The Trade Desk and Google Campaign Manager 360.']
  tone_of_voice varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the brand\'s communication style and personality expression across all channels. Governs copywriting, creative briefs, and PR communications to ensure brand consistency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authoritative|playful|inspirational|empathetic|bold|sophisticated|conversational — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand profile record was most recently modified. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for a client brand managed by the agency. Captures brand identity essentials including brand name, parent company, industry vertical, brand tier, logo references, brand color palette codes, typography standards, tone of voice classification, brand archetype, launch date, brand lifecycle stage (emerging, established, mature, declining), active markets and geographies, local brand name variants, regulatory compliance flags (ASA, FTC, EASA), and market tier classifications. Serves as the SSOT for brand identity within the brand domain — distinct from the client master in the client domain. Anchors all brand-level entities including guidelines, positioning, assets, health metrics, and PR activities.'
}

Table brand.guideline {
  guideline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the brand guideline record. Primary key for the brand_guideline data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  approved_production_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Brand guidelines specify approved production vendors (design agencies, production houses) for asset creation. Essential for vendor compliance in creative production and ensuring brand consistency acro']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Brand guidelines govern the visual identity and messaging architecture for a specific brand profile. While client_brand_id tracks client ownership, brand_profile_id links to the operational brand enti']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client brand entity that this guideline governs. Links the guideline to its owning brand within the client portfolio.']
  previous_version_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding version of this guideline. Enables version chain traversal and supports messaging framework evolution auditing.']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent profile of the brand strategist who owns and is accountable for this guideline document.']
  applicable_markets varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes identifying the geographic markets to which this guideline applies (e.g., \'USA|GBR|DEU\'). Supports multi-market brand governance and regional variant management.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the guideline document within the Workfront proofing and approval workflow. Draft indicates work-in-progress; In Review indicates active UAT/stakeholder review; Approved indicates ratified and in force; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Retired indicates no longer applicable.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the guideline received final client or internal approval, as recorded in the Workfront proofing workflow.']
  approved_typefaces varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of approved font family names for use in brand communications (e.g., \'Helvetica Neue|Georgia|Arial\'). Populated for visual_identity guideline types. Sourced from Adobe Creative Cloud font libraries.']
  audience_message_map varchar [note: 'Structured JSON-serialised string capturing audience segment-to-message mappings (e.g., segment name, primary message, secondary message, call-to-action). Populated for messaging_framework guideline types to support audience-targeted communications.']
  brand_pillars varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of the brand\'s strategic pillars (e.g., \'Innovation|Trust|Sustainability\'). Populated for messaging_framework guideline types. Confidential as it represents proprietary brand positioning strategy.']
  brand_promise varchar [note: 'The core brand promise statement articulating the fundamental value the brand commits to delivering to its audience. Populated for messaging_framework guideline types. Confidential as it represents proprietary brand strategy.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the key changes introduced in this version relative to the previous version. Supports change communication to creative, media, and PR teams.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of media channels to which this guideline applies (e.g., \'digital|ooh|ctv|print|social\'). Aligns with IAB channel taxonomy and supports channel-specific creative governance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|ooh|ctv|print|social|radio|dooh|ott|email|search — promote to reference product]']
  co_brand_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the partner brand involved in a co-branding arrangement governed by this guideline. Populated only for co_branding guideline types. Supports competitive conflict checks and partner brand governance.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this guideline document governing its distribution and access permissions. Public guidelines may be shared externally; Restricted guidelines contain unreleased brand strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'ISO 8601 timestamp recording when this brand guideline record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  dam_uri varchar [note: 'Fully-qualified URI pointing to the master guideline document stored in the Adobe Creative Cloud Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Serves as the authoritative source-of-record reference for creative teams.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this version of the guideline becomes binding for all creative production, media planning, and communications activities.']
  elevator_pitch varchar [note: 'Concise 30–60 second verbal description of the brand\'s value proposition for use in sales, PR, and client-facing communications. Populated for messaging_framework guideline types.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date after which this version of the guideline is no longer valid and must be superseded or retired. Nullable for open-ended guidelines with no planned expiry.']
  forbidden_language varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of words, phrases, or claims that are explicitly prohibited in all brand communications under this guideline. Supports compliance with ASA, FTC, and client brand safety requirements.']
  guideline_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the guideline document, used in Workfront project references and Adobe Creative Cloud DAM asset tagging. Format: BG- followed by 4–12 uppercase alphanumeric characters.']
  guideline_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the guideline document by its primary subject matter. Visual Identity covers logos, colour palettes, typography; Tone of Voice covers language style; Messaging Framework covers brand promise and pillars; Digital covers web/social/app standards; Out-of-Home (OOH) covers outdoor/transit formats; Co-Branding covers partner brand usage rules.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guideline applies globally across all markets (True) or is restricted to the markets specified in applicable_markets (False). Supports multi-market brand governance hierarchies.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this guideline is mandatory (True) for all creative and communications outputs, or advisory/recommended (False). Supports brand compliance enforcement workflows.']
  key_proof_points varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of substantiated claims or evidence statements that support the brand promise and pillars. Used by copywriters and PR teams to ensure message consistency. Populated for messaging_framework guideline types.']
  language_variants varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of BCP-47 language tags identifying the language versions covered by this guideline (e.g., \'en-US|fr-FR|de-DE\'). Supports localisation of tone-of-voice and messaging framework guidelines.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'ISO 8601 timestamp recording when this brand guideline record was most recently updated. Supports change detection, audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  logo_usage_rules varchar [note: 'Free-text description of approved and prohibited logo usage rules including minimum size, clear space, colour variants, and placement restrictions. Populated for visual_identity and co_branding guideline types.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date for the next mandatory review of this guideline. Derived operationally from effective_date plus review_cycle_days but stored explicitly to support governance workflow triggers.']
  primary_colour_palette varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of approved primary brand colours expressed as HEX codes (e.g., \'#FF0000|#FFFFFF|#000000\'). Populated for visual_identity guideline types. Sourced from Adobe Creative Cloud brand libraries.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled review frequency for this guideline expressed in calendar days (e.g., 365 for annual review). Drives automated review reminders and governance calendar planning.']
  secondary_colour_palette varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of approved secondary/accent brand colours expressed as HEX codes. Populated for visual_identity guideline types. Supports extended creative palette governance.']
  tagline varchar [note: 'The primary approved tagline for the brand as defined in this guideline version. Confidential as it may represent unreleased creative strategy prior to campaign launch.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the brand guideline document (e.g., \'Acme Corp Global Visual Identity Standards v3.2\'). Used as the primary display label in DAM and Workfront.']
  tone_descriptors varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of adjectives describing the brand\'s approved tone of voice (e.g., \'Authoritative|Warm|Witty|Direct\'). Populated for tone_of_voice and messaging_framework guideline types.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for this iteration of the guideline (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\', \'3.1.2\'). Supports versioned evolution of messaging frameworks alongside visual identity updates.']

  Note: 'Authoritative brand guideline document record governing visual identity, messaging architecture, and usage standards for a brand. Tracks guideline version, effective date, expiry date, approval status, owning brand strategist, document storage URI (DAM reference), applicable markets/regions, language variants, and guideline type (visual identity, tone of voice, messaging framework, digital, OOH, co-branding). For messaging-type guidelines, captures structured messaging architecture including brand promise, brand pillars, key proof points, elevator pitch, tagline variants, audience-specific message maps, tone descriptors, and forbidden language lists. Integrates with Adobe Creative Cloud DAM and Workfront for proofing workflows. Versioned to support messaging framework evolution alongside visual identity updates.'
}

Table brand.positioning {
  positioning_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the brand positioning record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a strategic positioning document/asset owned and managed by the agency on behalf of a client brand.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser (client organisation) that owns the brand being positioned. Enables cross-brand and cross-client reporting.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Brand positioning statements define the strategic positioning for a specific brand profile. Similar to brand_guideline, client_brand_id tracks ownership while brand_profile_id links to the operational']
  client_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client brand entity for which this positioning strategy is defined. Links the positioning record to the brand master in the client domain.']
  parent_positioning_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the global master brand_positioning_id from which this regional or market-specific positioning is derived. Null if this record is itself the global master or a standalone positioning.']
  primary_superseded_by_positioning_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand_positioning_id of the newer record that replaced this one. Null if this is the current active version. Enables navigation of the positioning version chain.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of this positioning record. \'pending\' = awaiting internal review; \'approved\' = signed off internally; \'rejected\' = returned for revision; \'pending_client\' = awaiting client sign-off.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (internal lead or client stakeholder) who granted final approval for this positioning record. Supports audit trail and accountability tracking.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this positioning record received final approval (internal and/or client). Null if not yet approved. Used for audit trail and compliance with client SOW deliverable timelines.']
  awareness_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the methodology used to measure the brand awareness baseline: \'unaided\' = spontaneous recall without prompting; \'aided\' = recognition when brand name is provided; \'top_of_mind\' = first brand recalled in category.']
  brand_awareness_baseline_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The unaided or aided brand awareness percentage recorded at the time this positioning was established or last reviewed. Used as a benchmark to measure the effectiveness of the positioning strategy over time.']
  brand_essence varchar [note: 'A concise distillation of the brand\'s fundamental nature and core value — typically a short phrase or single sentence that captures the brand\'s soul and guides all brand expression (e.g., \'Empowering human potential\').']
  brand_personality varchar [note: 'Description of the human characteristics and traits attributed to the brand (e.g., \'bold, innovative, approachable\'). Guides tone of voice, creative direction, and communications style across all channels.']
  brand_promise varchar [note: 'The explicit commitment the brand makes to its target audience — the experience or value customers can consistently expect. Distinct from brand essence in that it is outward-facing and customer-centric.']
  brand_sentiment_baseline decimal(5,2) [note: 'The baseline brand sentiment score (e.g., on a -100 to +100 scale) recorded at the time this positioning was established or last reviewed. Tracks consumer perception and emotional affinity toward the brand.']
  competitive_set_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of competitor brand or advertiser identifiers that constitute the defined competitive set for this positioning. Used to scope Share of Voice (SOV) tracking and competitive monitoring via Nielsen Ad Intel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this brand positioning record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance requirements. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this positioning record is considered active and authoritative. Supports temporal tracking of positioning history and enables point-in-time analysis of brand strategy.']
  frame_of_reference varchar [note: 'The competitive category or context within which the brand competes and against which consumers evaluate it (e.g., \'premium athletic footwear\', \'cloud-based HR software\'). Defines the competitive set boundary for the positioning.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic market(s) for which this positioning applies, expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional descriptors (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'GLOBAL\'). Supports multi-market brand strategy management.']
  is_global_master boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this positioning record is the global master version from which regional adaptations are derived. True = global master; False = regional or market-specific adaptation.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'The IETF BCP 47 language code for the language in which this positioning record is authored (e.g., \'en\', \'en-US\', \'fr-FR\'). Supports multi-language brand strategy management for global clients.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which this positioning record was most recently formally reviewed and validated by the brand strategy team and client stakeholders.']
  messaging_framework varchar [note: 'Structured narrative framework defining the hierarchy of brand messages — from core proposition through supporting messages to proof points — used to ensure consistency across campaigns and communications.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review of this positioning record. Calculated based on the review cycle and last review date. Used to trigger review workflows and client QBR preparation.']
  nielsen_brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand identifier assigned by Nielsen Ad Intel for competitive monitoring and SOV tracking. Used to link this positioning record to Nielsen competitive intelligence data and GRP/TRP measurement reports.']
  nps_baseline decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Net Promoter Score (NPS) baseline value recorded at the time this positioning was established or last reviewed. Provides a benchmark for measuring brand health improvement attributable to the positioning strategy.']
  point_of_difference varchar [note: 'The key attribute, benefit, or association that differentiates the brand from competitors within the frame of reference. Represents the core competitive advantage articulated in the positioning strategy.']
  positioning_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the positioning record, used in cross-system references, client deliverables, and brand strategy documents (e.g., BP-ACME2024). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  positioning_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or label for this positioning record (e.g., \'FY2024 Premium Repositioning — ACME Corp\'). Used in reports, presentations, and brand strategy documents. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  positioning_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the positioning record. \'draft\' = in development; \'active\' = approved and in use; \'under_review\' = scheduled review cycle in progress; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version; \'archived\' = retired. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  reason_to_believe varchar [note: 'The substantiation or proof points that support the brand\'s point of difference and make the positioning credible to the target audience. May include product features, certifications, heritage, or third-party endorsements.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which this positioning record is formally reviewed and validated by the brand strategy team and client. Drives workflow scheduling in Workfront and QBR agenda planning.']
  sov_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target Share of Voice (SOV) percentage the brand aims to achieve within its competitive set and frame of reference. Used as a KPI benchmark for media planning and campaign performance evaluation. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.']
  statement varchar [note: 'The formal, approved positioning statement articulating the brand\'s unique place in the market. Typically follows the structure: \'For [target audience], [brand] is the [frame of reference] that [point of difference] because [reason to believe].\' Classified confidential as it represents proprietary brand strategy.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'The date on which this positioning record was superseded by a newer version. Null if the record is currently active. Enables full positioning history tracking and audit trail.']
  target_audience_definition varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary target audience for the brand, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioural characteristics. Used to align media planning, creative development, and campaign strategy.']
  territory varchar [note: 'The primary strategic territory in which the brand is positioned. \'functional\' = product/service performance benefits; \'emotional\' = feelings and personal identity; \'societal\' = broader social, environmental, or cultural purpose. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this brand positioning record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this positioning record for the associated brand. Increments with each approved revision. Enables version history tracking and comparison of positioning evolution over time.']
  workfront_project_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the corresponding brand strategy project in Workfront (Project Management system). Enables traceability between the positioning record and the project deliverable, resource plan, and approval workflow.']

  Note: 'Strategic positioning statement and competitive differentiation record for a brand. Stores the formal positioning statement, target audience definition, frame of reference (competitive category), point of difference, reason to believe, brand essence, competitive set identifiers, positioning territory (functional, emotional, societal), and positioning review cycle. Tracks positioning history with effective and superseded dates. Supports Brand Strategy and Positioning process.'
}

Table brand.brand_asset {
  brand_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each brand asset record in the DAM. Primary key for the brand_asset product.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser or client who owns this brand asset. Supports client-level brand compliance auditing.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent brand under which this asset is managed. Links the asset to its owning brand entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campaign for which this asset was originally created or first deployed. Supports campaign-level asset utilization reporting and creative lineage tracking.']
  dam_asset_id varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this asset in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system — Adobe Creative Cloud or Workfront DAM. Used to cross-reference and retrieve the physical file from the DAM platform.']
  guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_guideline. Business justification: Brand assets must comply with specific brand guidelines. Currently brand_guideline_ref is a STRING reference - converting to FK enables validation that assets meet guideline requirements and allows jo']
  parent_asset_brand_asset_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the master or parent asset from which this variant or localized version was derived. Enables asset hierarchy and lineage tracking.']
  production_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Brand assets track production vendor (design agencies, production houses, photographers) for asset provenance, rights management, and vendor performance evaluation. Critical for usage rights tracking ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset received its current approval status. Used for compliance auditing and to determine the start of the approved usage window.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the brand asset as determined by the brand governance review process. Only assets with \'approved\' status may be deployed in client-facing campaigns.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the brand manager or creative director who granted final approval for this asset. Supports brand compliance audit trails.']
  approved_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of channels for which this asset has been approved for deployment (e.g., \'digital,print,OOH,broadcast,social\'). Enforces brand compliance by restricting asset use to authorized channels only.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the brand asset including its intended use, visual characteristics, and any special handling instructions for creative teams.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the brand asset as catalogued in the DAM (e.g., \'Primary Logo RGB\', \'Brand Typeface Regular\', \'Hero Photography Q1 2024\').']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand asset by its creative or identity function. Drives usage rights enforcement and channel eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: logo|icon|typeface|brand_video|photography|illustration|template|motion_graphic|brand_guideline|color_palette — promote to reference product]']
  cobranding_partner varchar [note: 'Name of the co-branding partner organization whose brand identity is incorporated in this asset (e.g., for co-branded campaigns or partnership activations). Null if no co-branding applies.']
  color_mode varchar [note: 'Color space or mode of the asset (RGB for digital, CMYK for print, Pantone for brand-matched spot colors). Critical for ensuring correct reproduction across digital and print channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand asset record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  deployment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this asset has been deployed across campaigns, channels, or markets. Supports asset utilization reporting and ROI analysis for brand asset investment.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format or extension of the brand asset (e.g., AI, EPS, SVG, PNG, PDF, MP4). Determines rendering compatibility across channels and platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AI|EPS|SVG|PNG|JPG|PDF|MP4|MOV|OTF|TTF|PSD|INDD|GIF|WEBP — promote to reference product]']
  file_size_kb decimal(12,2) [note: 'File size of the asset in kilobytes. Used to validate compliance with channel-specific file weight limits (e.g., IAB ad unit max file sizes for digital placements).']
  height_px varchar [note: 'Height dimension of the asset in pixels. Used alongside width for aspect ratio validation and channel placement eligibility.']
  is_master_asset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is the master/canonical version of the asset type for the brand (True) or a derivative/localized variant (False). Supports brand governance by identifying the authoritative source asset.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region subtag) indicating the language of any text or copy embedded in the asset (e.g., \'en\', \'en-US\', \'fr-FR\'). Supports multi-market localization management.']
  last_deployed_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset was most recently deployed in a campaign, channel, or market activation. Used for asset utilization reporting and identifying dormant assets.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the brand asset\'s lifecycle from creation through retirement. Governs whether the asset may be deployed in campaigns or channels. Aligns with Workfront proofing workflow stages.']
  market_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of markets or regions where this asset is approved for use (e.g., \'USA,CAN,GBR\'). Supports multi-market brand governance and localization compliance.']
  resolution_dpi varchar [note: 'Resolution of the asset expressed in dots per inch (DPI). Determines print and digital quality suitability (e.g., 72 DPI for web, 300 DPI for print).']
  restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any specific restrictions, caveats, or conditions on the use of this asset (e.g., \'Not for use in alcohol advertising\', \'Minimum size 100px\', \'Do not alter color values\'). Supports brand compliance enforcement.']
  rights_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the usage rights for this asset expire. After this date, the asset must not be deployed without rights renewal. Critical for rights management compliance and avoiding unauthorized use.']
  rights_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market scope in which the usage rights for this asset are valid (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'Global\', \'EMEA\'). Restricts deployment to authorized markets only.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this brand asset record originated (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud, Workfront DAM). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated taxonomy or keyword tags applied to the asset for searchability and categorization within the DAM (e.g., \'hero,summer-2024,product-launch\'). Facilitates asset discovery and reuse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand asset record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  usage_rights_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intellectual property rights governing this asset\'s use (e.g., owned outright, licensed, royalty-free, rights-managed). Determines permissible deployment scope and commercial use restrictions.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the asset following semantic versioning convention (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Tracks iterative revisions and ensures the correct version is deployed across campaigns.']
  width_px varchar [note: 'Width dimension of the asset in pixels. Used for channel eligibility validation and ad serving compatibility checks.']

  Note: 'Individual brand asset record representing a discrete creative or identity asset (logo, icon, typeface file, brand video, photography, illustration, template) managed under a brand, including its deployment and usage history. Captures asset name, asset type, file format, resolution/dimensions, color mode, usage rights, rights expiry date, approved channels (digital, print, OOH, broadcast), market applicability, DAM asset ID (Adobe Creative Cloud / Workfront DAM reference), version number, approval status, and asset lifecycle stage. Also tracks usage events: each approved deployment across campaigns, channels, or markets with usage context, start/end dates, approver, rights confirmation, co-branding partner references, and restriction notes. Enables brand compliance auditing, rights management tracking, and asset utilization reporting. Distinct from campaign creative assets owned by the creative domain.'
}

Table brand.share_of_voice {
  share_of_voice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for share_of_voice']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client brand being tracked for share of voice analysis.']
  competitive_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor brand being compared in the share of voice analysis.']
  competitive_intelligence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.competitive_intelligence. Business justification: Share of voice tracking data feeds competitive intelligence reports. Analysts aggregate SOV measurements across channels and periods to produce competitive intelligence deliverables. Direct operationa']
  positioning_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_positioning. Business justification: Share of voice measurements track competitive performance of brand positioning strategies. Nullable FK links SOV metrics to the positioning being executed. Enables validation that positioning is achie']
  brand_impression_count varchar [note: 'Total number of advertising impressions delivered by the client brand during the measurement period. Used as an alternative numerator for share of voice calculations when impression data is available.']
  brand_sov_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total category voice captured by the client brand during the measurement period. Calculated as brand impressions or spend divided by total category impressions or spend, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  brand_spend_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated advertising spend by the client brand during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Ad Intel competitive monitoring. Used as the numerator for share of voice calculations.']
  competitive_rank varchar [note: 'Rank of the client brand within the product category based on share of voice during the measurement period. Rank 1 indicates the highest share of voice in the category. Supports competitive positioning analysis.']
  competitor_impression_count varchar [note: 'Total number of advertising impressions delivered by the competitor brand during the measurement period. Used for competitive impression share analysis.']
  competitor_sov_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total category voice captured by the competitor brand during the measurement period. Calculated as competitor impressions or spend divided by total category impressions or spend, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  competitor_spend_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated advertising spend by the competitor brand during the measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Ad Intel competitive monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this share of voice tracking record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting and cross-market comparison.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Quality score assigned to the share of voice data by the source system, ranging from 0.00 (low confidence) to 1.00 (high confidence). Reflects data completeness, accuracy, and reliability. Used to filter or weight competitive intelligence insights.']
