  last_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the supply path was last verified for ads.txt, sellers.json, and brand safety compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and verification of this supply path record to ensure continued compliance and authorization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special conditions, or operational guidance related to this supply path.']
  record_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for this supply path transparency record, used for auditing and reconciliation.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply path record indicating whether the path is currently valid and authorized for programmatic transactions.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled reviews of this supply path, based on tier classification and risk assessment.']
  seller_domain varchar [note: 'The seller\'s domain as declared in the SSP\'s sellers.json file, used for cross-verification with ads.txt declarations.']
  seller_name varchar [note: 'The seller name as declared in the SSP\'s sellers.json file, providing human-readable identification of the publisher or intermediary.']
  seller_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the SSP has a direct relationship with the publisher or is acting as a reseller, as declared in ads.txt and sellers.json.']
  sellers_json_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the sellers.json file was last verified for this supply path, ensuring transparency compliance.']
  sellers_json_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SSP\'s sellers.json file has been verified and contains a valid entry for this publisher relationship.']
  spo_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered classification of the supply path based on quality, transparency, and performance metrics, used for programmatic buying prioritization.']
  ssp_domain varchar [note: 'The domain of the SSP or exchange as declared in ads.txt, used for supply path identification and verification.']
  supply_path_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated quality score for this supply path based on transparency, verification status, intermediary count, and brand safety compliance. Scale 0.00 to 100.00.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply path record was last modified, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  viewability_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average viewability rate for impressions served through this supply path, measured according to MRC viewability standards.']

  Note: 'Programmatic supply chain transparency record capturing ads.txt, sellers.json, and supply path optimization (SPO) data for each publisher and SSP — including authorized seller declarations, seller types (direct/reseller), and supply path quality scores.'
}

Table vendor.platform_activation {
  platform_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this platform activation record. Primary key.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the agency project or initiative for which the tech platform is being activated']
  integration_owner_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically technical lead or project manager) responsible for managing this platform integration within the initiative. Supports accountability and operational ownership.']
  tech_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the advertising technology platform partner being activated for this initiative']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the tech platform was activated and made operational for this initiative. Marks the start of platform usage for this project.']
  api_credentials_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or vault key for project-specific API credentials used to integrate with this tech platform. Supports secure credential management per initiative.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this platform activation record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the tech platform was deactivated or integration was terminated for this initiative. Null if still active. Supports historical tracking of platform usage across project lifecycle.']
  integration_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of platform integration for this specific initiative (e.g., full programmatic integration, limited pilot, measurement only, data sharing only). Varies by project needs and budget.']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the platform integration for this initiative. Tracks integration health and readiness from a project delivery perspective.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent data synchronization or API call between the initiative\'s systems and this tech platform. Used for monitoring integration health.']
  platform_fee_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the tech partner\'s platform fee allocated to this specific initiative, denominated in the project\'s budget currency. Used for project cost accounting and budget tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this platform activation record.']
  usage_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantitative measure of platform usage for this initiative (e.g., impressions served, data records processed, API calls made). Unit of measure depends on platform type and billing model.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the activation and integration of advertising technology platforms (DSPs, DMPs, ad servers, analytics tools) within specific agency projects or initiatives. It captures project-specific integration scope, activation timeline, allocated platform fees, usage metrics, and integration health status. Each record links one tech partner platform to one initiative with attributes that exist only in the context of this project-specific activation.. Existence Justification: In advertising agency operations, tech platforms (DSPs, DMPs, ad servers, analytics tools) are activated for multiple initiatives based on project needs, and initiatives routinely use multiple tech platforms as part of their integrated tech stack. Platform activations are actively managed by project teams with project-specific integration scope, activation timelines, allocated costs, and usage tracking. This is an operational M:N relationship that the business recognizes and manages as \'platform activation\' or \'tech stack assignment.\''
}

Table vendor.invoice_dispute {
  invoice_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for invoice_dispute']
  escalated_from_invoice_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on invoice_dispute (escalated_from_invoice_dispute_id)']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice being disputed.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor whose invoice is being disputed.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'The actual date when the dispute was resolved and closed.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net adjustment applied to the invoice as a result of the dispute resolution. Positive for credits, negative for additional charges.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount approved for payment after dispute resolution. May differ from disputed amount.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team currently responsible for resolving the dispute.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the dispute involves a compliance or regulatory issue requiring special handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dispute record was first created in the system.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued as part of the dispute resolution, if applicable.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this dispute.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional context for the dispute reason.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the dispute, formatted as DSP-NNNNNNNN.']
  dispute_opened_date date [note: 'The date when the dispute was formally opened and logged in the system.']
  dispute_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the dispute was formally opened, representing the principal business event time.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the invoice is being disputed, provided by the initiating party.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute in the resolution workflow.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute based on the nature of the disagreement.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value being contested in the dispute. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  escalated_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the senior manager or executive to whom the dispute has been escalated.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date when the dispute was escalated to a higher authority level.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The current escalation tier of the dispute. 0 = no escalation, higher numbers indicate progressive escalation to senior management.']
  impact_on_vendor_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this dispute impacts the vendors performance rating or accreditation status.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or department who initiated the dispute.']
  is_recurring_issue boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this dispute is part of a recurring pattern with the same vendor or invoice type.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dispute record was last updated or modified.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute based on urgency and financial impact.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'The method or approach used to resolve the dispute.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the resolution outcome, actions taken, and final agreement.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the dispute was resolved and status changed to resolved or closed.']
  supporting_documentation varchar [note: 'References or links to supporting documents, contracts, purchase orders, or evidence submitted with the dispute.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'The target date by which the dispute should be resolved based on service level agreements (SLAs) or business policies.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for invoice_dispute. Referenced by dispute_id.'
}

Table project.initiative {
  initiative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the agency project or engagement. Primary key.']
  account_director_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account director overseeing client relationship and project governance.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client advertiser account for whom this project is being executed.']
  agency_relationship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Projects are delivered under specific agency relationship agreements (AOR, project-based retainer, pitch work). Tracking which relationship governs the work is essential for billing rules, scope bound']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Master agreements govern multi-project client relationships. Agencies link initiatives to MSAs to apply correct rate cards, enforce compliance obligations, and manage multi-year retainer terms across ']
  attribution_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Strategic initiatives define attribution methodology for performance evaluation. Real business process: campaign planning phase where strategists select attribution model (last-click, linear, data-dri']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Initiatives execute brand-specific work requiring access to brand guidelines, assets, positioning, and compliance rules. Core operational link for project setup and brand governance in advertising del']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated marketing campaign if this project is part of a broader campaign initiative. Nullable for non-campaign projects.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Projects must be assigned to cost centers for financial reporting, budget allocation, and P&L tracking in agency operations. Essential for departmental profitability analysis and overhead allocation.']
  parent_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on initiative (parent_initiative_id)']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Initiatives execute SOW-defined scope. Agencies track SOW-to-project mapping for revenue recognition, deliverable tracking, budget burn analysis, and client invoicing against contractual commitments.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Projects/initiatives frequently engage vendors for media buying, tech platforms, creative production. Tracking primary supplier enables project vendor management, cost allocation, and vendor performan']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred to date for labor, materials, and external expenses. Updated as costs are recorded.']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed duration of the project in calendar days. Populated upon project completion.']
  billing_type varchar [note: 'Commercial model under which the project is billed to the client.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for this project in the agency\'s reporting currency.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the project budget (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the project was formally cancelled. Populated only for cancelled projects.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the project was cancelled (e.g., client budget cuts, scope changes, strategic pivot).']
  client_satisfaction_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Client-reported satisfaction rating for the project, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Captured post-delivery or at milestones.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all project deliverables were completed and accepted by the client.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project record was first created in the system.']
  deliverables_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of key deliverables expected from this project (e.g., creative assets, media plans, campaign reports).']
  delivery_methodology varchar [note: 'Project management methodology applied for planning and execution (e.g., agile sprints, waterfall phases, hybrid approach).']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when project work is scheduled to complete or was completed.']
  health_indicator varchar [note: 'Overall health status of the project based on schedule, budget, scope, and quality metrics. Green indicates on-track, yellow indicates at-risk, red indicates critical issues.']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the project or engagement, used for identification and reporting.']
  initiative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the project indicating its stage in the execution workflow.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this project is billable to the client (true) or internal/non-billable (false).']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this project is subject to confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements requiring restricted access.']
  kickoff_date date [note: 'Date of the formal project kickoff meeting with the client and internal team.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing additional context, special instructions, or important observations about the project.']
  owning_department varchar [note: 'Primary agency department responsible for delivering this project. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative|media|strategy|digital|pr|production|account_services — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Originally estimated duration of the project in calendar days from start to end.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the project for client communication and billing. Typically follows agency naming convention.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project based on the primary service offering or deliverable type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: campaign_production|brand_strategy|media_planning|creative_development|pr_activation|digital_marketing|content_creation|market_research|event_management|retainer_services — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the project based on complexity, dependencies, client volatility, and resource constraints.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the project scope, objectives, deliverables, and boundaries.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique project identifier from the originating system of record (Workfront, Monday.com, or other project management platform).']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the project management system from which this project record was ingested.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when project work officially begins.']

  Note: 'Master record for an agency project or engagement — the authoritative SSOT for project identity, scope, and lifecycle within the agency. Captures project name, type (campaign production, brand strategy, media planning, PR activation, etc.), status (briefing, planning, in-flight, on-hold, completed, cancelled), priority, start and end dates, planned vs. actual duration, client account reference, associated campaign reference, owning department, project manager, delivery methodology (agile, waterfall, hybrid), source system (Workfront/Monday.com) project ID, and overall health indicator. Serves as the top-level container for all work breakdown structures, tasks, milestones, and resource assignments.'
}

Table project.work_package {
  work_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work package within the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS). Primary key.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Work packages deliver brand-specific outputs (creative assets, media plans, PR materials). Tracking brand context at work package level enables brand compliance validation and portfolio reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Work packages require cost center assignment for labor cost allocation and profitability analysis by discipline. Enables accurate project costing and departmental charge-back in agency financial syste']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent project or campaign engagement that this work package belongs to.']
  parent_work_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work package in the hierarchical WBS structure. Null for top-level packages.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Work packages decompose SOW scope. Agencies map WBS elements to SOW scope items for traceability, ensuring all contracted deliverables are planned and no out-of-scope work is executed without authoriz']
  worker_id varchar [note: 'Primary resource (employee or contractor) assigned as owner/lead for this work package. Links to talent/resource management system.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for this work package to date. Used for cost variance analysis and Earned Value Management (EVM).']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours expended on this work package to date. Tracked from time entry systems for variance analysis.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when work on this package was completed. Null if not yet completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when work on this package commenced. Null if not yet started.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the work package deliverable. Tracks client or internal stakeholder sign-off.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this work package. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this work package was formally approved. Null if not yet approved.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work completed for this package (0.00 to 100.00). Used for progress tracking and Earned Value (EV) calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work package record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for planned and actual cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary deliverable produced by this work package (e.g., \'Creative Brief\', \'Media Plan\', \'Campaign Report\', \'Video Asset\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative_brief|media_plan|campaign_report|video_asset|print_ad|digital_banner|social_content|presentation_deck|research_report|strategy_document|press_release — promote to reference product]']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Primary agency discipline or department responsible for executing this work package (e.g., creative, media, strategy, production, PR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative|media|strategy|production|account_management|public_relations|digital|analytics|content|design — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work package is billable to the client (true) or non-billable internal work (false).']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work package is on the project critical path. Delays to critical path items directly impact project completion date.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work package record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, instructions, risks, or issues related to this work package.']
  planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost for this work package including labor, materials, and other direct costs. Baseline for Earned Value Management (EVM).']
  planned_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor hours required to complete this work package. Used for resource capacity planning and baseline cost estimation.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when work on this package is planned to be completed. Used for baseline schedule tracking.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when work on this package is planned to begin. Used for baseline schedule tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this work package for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical alphanumeric code representing the work package position in the WBS (e.g., 1.2.3.1). Enables structured decomposition and rollup reporting.']
  wbs_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this work package in the WBS hierarchy (1 = top level, 2 = second level, etc.). Enables level-based filtering and rollup.']
  work_package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope, deliverables, and objectives of this work package. Provides context for resource planning and execution.']
  work_package_name varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive name of the work package that clearly identifies the deliverable or work scope (e.g., \'Creative Concept Development\', \'Media Buy Execution\').']
  work_package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work package indicating progress and execution state.']
  work_package_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work package within the WBS structure (deliverable-oriented, milestone, phase, task group, or control account).']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) node representing a discrete, manageable unit of work within a project. Captures WBS code, package name, description, parent package reference (enabling hierarchical decomposition), level in the WBS hierarchy, owning discipline or department (creative, media, strategy, production, PR), planned effort hours, actual effort hours, planned cost, actual cost, status, and completion percentage. Enables multi-level decomposition of project scope into deliverable-oriented components aligned to Workfront/Monday.com task groups.'
}

Table project.task {
  task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the task. Primary key.']
  assigned_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual worker or employee assigned to complete this task.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tasks need cost center tracking for time-based costing and departmental charge-back. Critical for labor cost allocation when time entries roll up to tasks for financial posting.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project this task belongs to.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team assigned to this task.']
  parent_task_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on task (parent_task_id)']
  predecessor_task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the task that must be completed or started before this task can proceed, based on the dependency type.']
  work_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work package that contains this task.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date when this task was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date when work on this task began.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval status of the task deliverable (not submitted, pending, approved, rejected, changes requested).']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why this task is blocked or cannot proceed.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this task was marked as complete.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of work completed for this task, expressed as a value between 0 and 100.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this task record was first created in the system.']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'The type of deliverable or output expected from this task (e.g., creative asset, media plan, trafficking sheet, approval document).']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'The type of dependency relationship this task has with its predecessor (finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish, start-to-finish, or none).']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated number of hours required to complete this task.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the hours logged on this task are billable to the client.']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task represents a project milestone.']
  logged_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual number of hours logged or worked on this task.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this task record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or instructions related to this task.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The scheduled date when this task is planned to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled date when work on this task is planned to begin.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this task indicating its relative importance and urgency.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record from which this task was sourced (e.g., Workfront, Monday.com).']
  source_system_task_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this task in the source system (Workfront or Monday.com).']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed in this task.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'The name or title of the task.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task (not started, in progress, blocked, complete, cancelled, on hold).']
  task_type varchar [note: 'The category or type of work this task represents (e.g., creative production, copywriting, media trafficking, client review, quality assurance, approval).']

  Note: 'Atomic unit of work assigned to an individual or team within a work package. Captures task name, description, task type (creative production, copy writing, media trafficking, client review, QA, approval, etc.), assigned worker reference, assigned team, planned start date, planned end date, actual start date, actual end date, estimated hours, logged hours, completion percentage, priority, status (not started, in progress, blocked, complete), dependency type, predecessor task reference, and source system task ID from Workfront or Monday.com. The operational heartbeat of project execution tracking.'
}

Table project.project_milestone {
  project_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project milestone. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee authorized to approve milestone completion and sign off on deliverables.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.milestone. Business justification: Contractual milestones trigger payment and acceptance gates. Project teams must track which project milestones satisfy contract obligations to enable billing, client sign-off, and revenue recognition ']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent project to which this milestone belongs.']
  primary_project_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for ensuring milestone completion and stakeholder coordination.']
  replanned_from_project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_milestone (replanned_from_project_milestone_id)']
  work_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated work package or phase within the project structure.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific, measurable criteria that must be met for the milestone to be considered successfully achieved and approved by stakeholders.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Actual date the milestone was achieved or completed. Null if milestone has not yet been reached.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone completion was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Baseline date captured at project approval or contract signature, used for variance analysis and performance measurement against original commitment.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work completed toward achieving this milestone, expressed as a decimal (0.00 to 100.00). Used for progress tracking and earned value analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system.']
  deliverable_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tangible deliverable or output associated with this milestone (e.g., \'Final creative assets in all formats\', \'Campaign performance dashboard\', \'Client presentation deck\').']
  dependency_count varchar [note: 'Number of predecessor tasks or milestones that must be completed before this milestone can be achieved. Used for dependency analysis and scheduling.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Current forecasted date for milestone completion based on project progress and risk assessment. Updated regularly during project execution.']
  is_billing_trigger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether achievement of this milestone triggers invoice generation or payment milestone per the client contract.']
  is_contractual_gate boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this milestone is a contractually binding checkpoint defined in the SOW, requiring formal client sign-off before proceeding.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this milestone lies on the project critical path. Delays to critical path milestones directly impact overall project completion date.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the milestone deliverable, acceptance criteria, and business significance.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the milestone (e.g., \'Creative Concept Approval\', \'Campaign Launch\', \'Post-Campaign Report Delivery\').']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone by its business function: kickoff (project initiation), creative_review (internal creative approval), client_approval (client sign-off gate), campaign_launch (media go-live), post_campaign_report (campaign performance delivery), billing_trigger (invoice generation event), contractual_gate (SOW-defined checkpoint). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kickoff|creative_review|client_approval|campaign_launch|post_campaign_report|billing_trigger|contractual_gate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, issues, decisions, or stakeholder feedback related to the milestone.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for milestone achievement as defined in the project plan or SOW.']
  project_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone: upcoming (not yet started), in_progress (work underway toward milestone), achieved (successfully completed on or before planned date), missed (completed after planned date or not completed by deadline), deferred (postponed to future date), cancelled (no longer required).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Current risk assessment for on-time milestone achievement: low (on track, no issues), medium (minor concerns, monitoring required), high (significant risk of delay), critical (delay imminent or already occurred).']
  sow_milestone_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code linking this milestone to a contractual milestone defined in the client Statement of Work (SOW). Used for contractual compliance and billing trigger validation.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Calculated variance in days between planned date and actual date. Positive values indicate delay (missed deadline), negative values indicate early completion. Null if milestone not yet achieved.']

  Note: 'Significant scheduled checkpoint or deliverable gate within a project lifecycle. Captures milestone name, description, milestone type (kickoff, creative review, client approval, campaign launch, post-campaign report, billing trigger, contractual gate), planned date, actual date, status (upcoming, achieved, missed, deferred), owner, associated work package, linked SOW contractual milestone reference, and variance in days between planned and actual achievement. Used for client reporting, billing triggers, and executive project health dashboards.'
}

Table project.resource_plan {
  resource_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the resource plan record. Primary key for the resource plan entity.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project for which this resource plan is created. Links to the project master entity.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.position. Business justification: Resource plans specify role requirements for initiatives; linking to position enables headcount planning alignment, budget-to-actual position cost comparison, position utilization forecasting, and wor']
  revised_from_resource_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on resource_plan (revised_from_resource_plan_id)']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level for securing this resource allocation (critical, high, medium, low), used for capacity planning and resource conflict resolution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this resource plan was approved by the project manager or resource manager.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the person who approved this resource plan.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned hours for this resource are billable to the client (True) or non-billable/internal (False).']
  cost_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The internal cost rate applied per hour or per FTE for this role, used for capacity planning and project costing.']
  cost_rate_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this resource plan record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The code representing the department or cost center responsible for this resource allocation.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'The name of the department or cost center responsible for this resource allocation.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The broad functional discipline or department to which the role belongs (creative, strategy, media, account management, production, analytics, technology, operations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative|strategy|media|account|production|analytics|technology|operations — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  location_preference varchar [note: 'The preferred work location for this resource allocation (onsite, remote, hybrid, or any).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the user who last modified this resource plan record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this resource plan record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this resource plan, including special requirements, constraints, or assumptions.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'The specific office or geographic location from which the resource is expected to work (e.g., New York, London, Remote).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the resource plan (draft, submitted for approval, approved, active, superseded by newer version, or cancelled).']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the resource plan, enabling tracking of plan iterations and revisions over time (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, draft, final).']
  planned_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) allocation for this role during the planning period. 1.0 represents one full-time resource; 0.5 represents half-time allocation.']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours planned for this role during the planning period.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning period for which resources are being planned.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning period for which resources are being planned.']
  planning_period_type varchar [note: 'The time granularity used for resource planning (week, sprint, month, quarter, or project phase).']
  role_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code representing the role or discipline required for this resource plan (e.g., ACD for Art Creative Director, CPY for Copywriter, STR for Strategist).']
  role_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the role or discipline required (e.g., Art Director, Copywriter, Media Planner, Account Executive).']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'The seniority or experience level required for the role (junior, mid-level, senior, lead, principal, director).']
  skill_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific skills, competencies, or certifications required for the role (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, Google Ads Certification, Salesforce CRM, Spanish language fluency).']
  total_planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total planned cost for this resource allocation, calculated as planned hours multiplied by cost rate.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the user who created this resource plan record.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking resource demand plan for a project, capturing the planned allocation of agency talent and capacity across the project timeline. Stores project reference, planning period (week, sprint, month), role or discipline required, planned FTE count, planned hours, skill requirements, seniority level, department, cost rate applied, total planned cost, and plan version. Distinct from talent.resource_allocation (which records confirmed individual assignments) — this is the demand-side capacity plan used for staffing decisions and capacity forecasting at the project level.'
}

Table project.project_assignment {
  project_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the assignment record. Primary key.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent project to which this assignment belongs.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.position. Business justification: Assignments staff workers into project roles; linking to position enables position utilization tracking, role-based capacity planning, position cost rate application for billing, and position-level re']
  primary_project_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker or team member assigned to the task. Links to talent workforce data.']
  reassigned_from_project_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_assignment (reassigned_from_project_assignment_id)']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Resource assignments are scoped to SOWs. Agencies track which SOW funds each assignment for cost allocation, billing rate application, and ensuring resource deployment aligns with contractual scope an']
  talent_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.engagement. Business justification: When assignments staff external talent (freelancers, contractors), linking to engagement enables contract compliance tracking, rate validation against agreed terms, usage rights verification, and enga']
  task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific task or work package within the project that this assignment addresses.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual number of hours logged by the worker against the assigned task. Used for utilization tracking and billing.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the worker\'s capacity allocated to this assignment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50% allocation).']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment was created or assigned to the worker.']
  assignment_role varchar [note: 'The role or capacity in which the worker is assigned to the task (e.g., lead, contributor, reviewer, approver, observer, consultant).']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment indicating whether it is active, completed, reassigned, on hold, or cancelled.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hours worked on this assignment are billable to the client (True) or non-billable (False).']
  billing_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly billing rate applied to this assignment for client invoicing purposes. Confidential business data.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment was marked as completed by the worker or project manager.']
  cost_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The internal cost rate per hour for the worker on this assignment, used for profitability analysis. Confidential business data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assignment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for billing and cost rates (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker is scheduled to complete work on the assigned task.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assignment record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or instructions related to the assignment, such as special requirements or context for the worker.']
  overtime_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is eligible for overtime compensation on this assignment (True) or not (False).']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated number of hours allocated for the worker to complete the assigned task.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level of this assignment relative to other assignments for the worker (critical, high, medium, low).']
  reassigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment was reassigned to a different worker, if applicable.']
  reassignment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the assignment was reassigned to a different worker, if applicable.']
  skill_required varchar [note: 'The primary skill or competency required for this assignment (e.g., copywriting, art direction, media planning, data analysis).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker is scheduled to begin work on the assigned task.']
  utilization_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment for workforce utilization reporting (e.g., client billable, internal project, training, administrative, bench).']
  work_location varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is to be performed on-site at the client or agency location, remotely, or in a hybrid arrangement.']

  Note: 'Operational record linking a specific worker or team member to a task or work package within a project. Captures assignee worker reference, task or work package reference, assignment role (lead, contributor, reviewer, approver), planned hours, actual hours logged, assignment start date, assignment end date, allocation percentage, billable flag, billing rate applied, assignment status (active, completed, reassigned), and reassignment reason. Bridges project execution with talent workforce data and enables utilization tracking at the task level.'
}

Table project.dependency {
  dependency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project dependency record.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project containing this dependency relationship.']
  linked_dependency_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dependency (linked_dependency_id)']
  successor_entity_task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific task or milestone that depends on the predecessor. The entity type is determined by successor_entity_type.']
  task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific task or milestone that must complete before the successor can begin. The entity type is determined by predecessor_entity_type.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Rigidity level of the dependency constraint. Hard: must be enforced, cannot be violated. Soft: should be enforced but can be overridden with approval. Preferred: advisory only, used for optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dependency record was first created in the system.']
  dependency_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the dependency nature. Mandatory: legally or contractually required. Discretionary: based on best practices or preferences. External: involves third parties or external entities. Internal: within the organization\'s control.']
  dependency_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the dependency relationship. Active: dependency is enforced. Waived: dependency temporarily ignored by project manager approval. Violated: successor started before predecessor completed. Suspended: dependency enforcement paused. Resolved: dependency satisfied and closed.']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'Logical relationship type between predecessor and successor. Finish-to-start (FS): successor cannot start until predecessor finishes. Start-to-start (SS): successor cannot start until predecessor starts. Finish-to-finish (FF): successor cannot finish until predecessor finishes. Start-to-finish (SF): successor cannot finish until predecessor starts.']
  float_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total float or slack time available in this dependency relationship. Number of days the successor can be delayed without impacting the project completion date.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency is part of the project critical path. True when any delay in this dependency directly impacts the project completion date.']
  is_cross_project boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency spans across multiple projects. True when predecessor and successor belong to different projects, enabling portfolio-level critical path analysis.']
  lag_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of working days to delay the successor after the predecessor relationship is satisfied. Positive values create a waiting period between activities.']
  lead_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of working days the successor can start before the predecessor relationship is satisfied. Allows overlapping or fast-tracking of activities.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the dependency record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dependency record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context, constraints, or special instructions related to this dependency relationship. Free-text field for project team collaboration.']
  predecessor_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of the predecessor entity in the dependency relationship. Indicates whether the predecessor is a task or milestone.']
  risk_impact_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of schedule risk if this dependency is violated or delayed. Critical: project completion at risk. High: major milestone impact. Medium: moderate schedule impact. Low: minor impact. Negligible: no material impact.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this dependency record originated. Typically Workfront, Monday.com, or other project management platform.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this dependency record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  successor_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of the successor entity in the dependency relationship. Indicates whether the successor is a task or milestone.']
  violation_date date [note: 'Date when the dependency was violated, if applicable. Recorded when successor activity started or finished before the predecessor relationship was satisfied.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the project manager or authority who approved the dependency waiver.']
  waiver_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the dependency waiver was formally approved.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for waiving or suspending the dependency. Captures the rationale when project manager overrides the dependency constraint.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the dependency record. Typically the project manager or project coordinator.']

  Note: 'Defines scheduling dependencies between tasks or milestones within and across projects. Captures predecessor entity type (task or milestone), predecessor entity reference, successor entity type, successor entity reference, dependency type (finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish, start-to-finish), lag days, lead days, is_cross_project flag, and dependency status (active, waived, violated). Enables critical path analysis and schedule risk identification across the project portfolio.'
}

Table project.schedule_baseline {
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the schedule baseline record. Primary key.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent project for which this schedule baseline was created.']
  prior_schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on schedule_baseline (prior_schedule_baseline_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this baseline was formally approved and locked for use as the measurement reference.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual (project sponsor, client stakeholder, or program manager) who approved this baseline.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Role or title of the approver (e.g., \'Project Sponsor\', \'Client Director\', \'Program Manager\').']
  baseline_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the baseline scope, assumptions, and context at the time of approval.']
  baseline_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this baseline (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Client Approved Baseline\', \'Post-Scope-Change Baseline\').']
  baseline_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context provided by the project manager or approver at the time of baseline creation.']
  baseline_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when this baseline snapshot was captured from the project management system (Workfront, Monday.com).']
  baseline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the baseline (draft during creation, pending-approval awaiting sign-off, approved and locked, active as current baseline, superseded by newer version, or archived for historical reference).']
  baseline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the baseline indicating its purpose or trigger (original baseline at project start, re-baseline after scope change, client-approved milestone baseline, internal planning baseline, interim checkpoint, or final approved baseline).']
  baseline_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this baseline. Increments with each re-baseline event (e.g., 1 for original, 2 for first re-baseline).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was first created in the project management system.']
  critical_path_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration in days of the critical path as identified in this baseline schedule.']
  is_current_baseline boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the currently active baseline against which project performance is being measured (True) or a historical/superseded baseline (False).']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of milestones included in this baseline schedule.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this baseline record (e.g., status change, notes update).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was last modified in the project management system.']
  planned_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total planned cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Baseline planned end date for the project or phase captured in this baseline.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Baseline planned start date for the project or phase captured in this baseline.']
  re_baseline_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for creating a new baseline (e.g., \'Scope change approved by client\', \'Resource reallocation\', \'Major milestone shift\'). Null for original baseline.']
  re_baseline_trigger_event varchar [note: 'Specific event or change request that triggered the re-baseline (e.g., change request number, client directive reference).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this baseline was extracted (e.g., Workfront, Monday.com, MS Project). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: workfront|monday|ms-project|smartsheet|asana|jira|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_baseline_code varchar [note: 'Native baseline identifier or version key from the source project management system.']
  task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of tasks or work packages included in this baseline schedule.']
  total_float_days varchar [note: 'Total float or slack available in the baseline schedule (difference between latest and earliest finish dates).']
  total_planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned cost (Budget at Completion - BAC) for the project as captured in this baseline. Used for cost variance and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.']
  total_planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Total planned duration of the project in calendar days as captured in this baseline.']
  total_planned_effort_hours decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total planned effort in hours across all tasks and resources as captured in this baseline. Used for Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who created this baseline record in the project management system.']

  Note: 'Immutable snapshot of the approved project schedule at a specific point in time — capturing the baseline against which actual progress is measured. Stores baseline version number, baseline name, baseline type (original, re-baseline, client-approved), approval date, approver, total planned duration, planned start date, planned end date, total planned effort hours, total planned cost, and the reason for re-baselining if applicable. Enables earned value management (EVM) and schedule variance (SV) calculations across the project lifecycle.'
}

Table project.change_request {
  change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the change request. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Scope changes may require contract amendments. Agencies track which master agreement governs change approval authority, amendment thresholds, and client sign-off requirements per contractual change co']
  approver_contact_client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual authorized to approve or reject the change request.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Scope changes often driven by brand strategy shifts, repositioning, or crisis events. Change impact assessment requires brand context for compliance and stakeholder communication.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the campaign associated with this change request, if applicable.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Change requests impact project budgets and require linkage for budget amendment tracking, approval workflows, and financial impact analysis. Critical for scope change management and budget variance co']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this change request applies.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to media.media_plan. Business justification: Scope changes to media plans require formal change control. Links change impact assessment to media budget/flight adjustments, enabling SOW amendment tracking, client approval workflows, and financial']
  primary_change_client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual contact who submitted the change request.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Statement of Work (SOW) that may require amendment due to this change request.']
  superseded_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on change_request (superseded_change_request_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the change request was formally approved or rejected.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or rationale provided by the approver regarding the approval or rejection decision.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the change request was approved or rejected, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved or rejected the change request.']
  billing_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this change request has an impact on client billing (True) or not (False).']
  change_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the requested change, including rationale, context, and business justification.']
  change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change request: submitted, under review, approved, rejected, deferred, or implemented.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change request by the dimension being modified: scope addition, scope reduction, timeline extension, budget increase, resource change, or deliverable modification.']
  client_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal client approval is required for this change request (True) or if internal approval is sufficient (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this change request record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deferral_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the change request was deferred to a later date or phase, if applicable.']
  impact_assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the assessed impact of the change request on scope, schedule, budget, quality, and resources.']
  impact_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the impact cost delta.']
  impact_cost_delta decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the change request, expressed as a positive or negative delta from the original budget.']
  impact_effort_hours_delta decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated change in effort hours required to implement this change request, expressed as a positive or negative delta from the original project plan.']
  impact_timeline_delta_days varchar [note: 'Estimated change in project timeline measured in days, expressed as a positive or negative delta from the original schedule.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved change request was implemented into the project plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the change request: critical, high, medium, or low.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for why the change request was rejected, if applicable.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the change request, typically formatted as CR-NNNNNN.']
  requestor_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual who submitted the change request.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who submitted the change request.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party initiating the change request: client, internal agency team, vendor, or partner.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the review of the change request was completed.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal review of the change request began.']
  scope_creep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this change request represents scope creep (True) or a legitimate change within project governance (False).']
  sow_amendment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the SOW amendment document generated as a result of this approved change request.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the change request was formally submitted.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the change request was submitted, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the nature of the change request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this change request record was last modified in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Formal record of a requested change to project scope, timeline, budget, or deliverables — the agency\'s project change control mechanism. Captures change request number, title, description, change type (scope addition, scope reduction, timeline extension, budget increase, resource change), requestor (client or internal), submission date, impact assessment (effort hours delta, cost delta, timeline delta), priority, approval status (submitted, under review, approved, rejected, deferred), approver, approval date, and linked SOW amendment reference. Critical for managing scope creep and client billing adjustments.'
}

Table project.project_brief {
  project_brief_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project brief record. Primary key.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client advertiser for whom this project is being executed.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Project briefs specify primary target audience segments for campaign planning and creative development. Agencies link briefs to segments to ensure creative deliverables align with audience strategy an']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Briefs specify which brand the project serves, driving creative direction, compliance requirements, and success criteria. Essential for brief intake and scope definition in advertising operations.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated campaign that this project brief supports.']
  client_brief_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating client brief that this project brief translates into an actionable plan.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: project_brief translates client briefs into actionable agency projects but currently has no direct link to the initiative product that represents the project execution. Adding initiative_id creates th']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Project briefs operationalize SOW commitments. Agencies must track which SOW funds each brief to validate scope, budget authority, and deliverable alignment during project kickoff and execution.']
  superseded_project_brief_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_brief (superseded_project_brief_id)']
  target_persona_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Project briefs reference target personas to guide strategic planning and creative direction. Agencies link briefs to personas to ensure all project deliverables (creative, media, messaging) align with']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the project brief for execution.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the project brief was formally approved for execution.']
  brand_safety_notes varchar [note: 'Brand safety considerations, content adjacency guidelines, and risk mitigation measures for media placement.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the project brief indicating its stage in the approval and execution workflow.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget envelope amount.']
  budget_envelope_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocation or envelope amount approved for this project execution.']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Description of the media and marketing channels to be utilized in this project (e.g., digital, TV, OOH, social, CTV).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project was marked as completed.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory, legal, and compliance requirements applicable to this project (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, FTC guidelines, ASA standards).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project brief record was first created in the system.']
  creative_direction_notes varchar [note: 'High-level creative direction, brand guidelines, tone, messaging themes, and creative considerations for the project.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when project execution officially began following brief approval.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the project brief is currently active and in use.']
  key_deliverables varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list of key deliverables, outputs, and work products expected from this project.']
  key_stakeholders varchar [note: 'List of key stakeholders, decision-makers, and approvers involved in this project from both agency and client sides.']
  measurement_framework varchar [note: 'Description of the measurement and analytics framework to be used for tracking project performance and outcomes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project brief record was last modified or updated.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for project execution as defined in the project brief.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for project execution as defined in the project brief.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the project brief indicating urgency and resource allocation priority.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project type indicating the nature of work to be performed.']
  risk_considerations varchar [note: 'Identified risks, dependencies, and mitigation strategies relevant to project execution.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the project scope, including in-scope and out-of-scope activities.']
  strategic_objectives varchar [note: 'High-level strategic goals and business objectives that this project aims to achieve for the client.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project brief was submitted for approval.']
  success_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined success criteria and key performance indicators (KPIs) that will be used to measure project success.']
  target_audience_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of the target audience demographics, psychographics, and behavioral characteristics for this project.']
  timeline_constraints varchar [note: 'Description of any timeline constraints, critical milestones, or deadline dependencies that impact project scheduling.']
  title varchar [note: 'The descriptive title of the project brief that summarizes the project scope.']

  Note: 'Internal project initiation brief that translates a client brief or SOW into an actionable agency project plan. Captures brief title, project type, originating client brief reference, associated campaign reference, strategic objectives, target audience summary, key deliverables list, channel mix, budget envelope, timeline constraints, success criteria, key stakeholders, creative direction notes, and brief status (draft, approved, in-execution). Distinct from client.client_brief (client-submitted) and creative.creative_brief (creative-specific) — this is the project management layer brief governing overall project execution.'
}

Table project.status_update {
  status_update_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project status update record. Primary key.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project this status update pertains to. Links to the project master record in Workfront or Monday.com.']
  previous_status_update_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on status_update (previous_status_update_id)']
  primary_status_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who authored and submitted this status update. Links to the employee or user master record in Workday HCM or identity management system.']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'FK to project.project_milestone']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Status reports are delivered per SOW reporting obligations. Agencies track SOW-level status to fulfill contractual reporting requirements, communicate progress against SOW milestones, and manage clien']
  accomplishments varchar [note: 'Narrative description of key accomplishments, milestones achieved, and deliverables completed during the reporting period.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this status update was approved for distribution. Nullable if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person who authored this status update. Typically the project manager or account lead.']
  budget_burn_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative budget spent or committed to date as of the end of this reporting period. Represents actual cost incurred against the total project budget.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget burn amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Status of the project budget and financial performance. Indicates whether the project is on budget, under budget, over budget, at risk of overrun, or in critical overrun.']
  client_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this status update is intended for external client distribution (True) or internal use only (False).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, notes, or context provided by the author or reviewers. Free-form text field for supplementary information.']
  decisions_required varchar [note: 'Narrative description of pending decisions, approvals, or actions required from stakeholders or leadership to unblock progress or resolve issues.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or semicolon-separated list of email addresses or user identifiers to whom this status update was distributed. Supports client-facing and internal stakeholder communication.']
  executive_summary varchar [note: 'High-level executive summary of the project status, suitable for senior leadership and client executives. Concise overview of health, progress, and key issues.']
  issues_and_blockers varchar [note: 'Narrative description of current issues, blockers, impediments, and challenges affecting project progress. Includes problem statements and impact assessment.']
  next_milestone_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next major milestone or deliverable.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Overall health status of the project using RAG (Red/Amber/Green) or equivalent traffic-light indicator. Red indicates critical issues, Amber indicates caution or risk, Green indicates on track.']
  percentage_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall project completion percentage as of the end of this reporting period. Expressed as a decimal value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  planned_activities varchar [note: 'Narrative description of planned activities, tasks, and milestones scheduled for the next reporting period.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last updated in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and governance.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Type or cadence of the status report: weekly, monthly, quarterly, ad hoc, or milestone-based.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the reporting period covered by this status update (e.g., weekly or monthly cycle end).']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the reporting period covered by this status update (e.g., weekly or monthly cycle start).']
  resource_status varchar [note: 'Status of resource availability and allocation for the project. Indicates whether resources are adequate, constrained, critically short, over-allocated, or under-allocated.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Overall risk level for the project during this reporting period. Indicates the severity of identified risks: low, medium, high, critical, or none.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Status of the project schedule and timeline adherence. Indicates whether the project is on schedule, ahead, behind, at risk of delay, or in critical delay.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this status update was captured or generated (e.g., Workfront, Monday.com, Jira, Asana, Smartsheet, or manual entry).']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this status update was submitted or published. Represents the business event timestamp for the status report creation.']

  Note: 'Periodic project status report record capturing the health and progress of a project at a point in time. Stores reporting period, overall RAG status (Red/Amber/Green), schedule status, budget status, resource status, risk status, accomplishments in the period, planned activities for next period, issues and blockers, decisions required, budget burn to date, percentage complete, author, and distribution list. Supports client-facing weekly/monthly status reporting and internal portfolio review cadences.'
}

Table project.risk_register {
  risk_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk or issue record in the project risk register.']
  brand_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Brand-specific risks (reputation damage, compliance violations, crisis escalation) require brand context for assessment and mitigation. Risk management process in advertising depends on brand linkage.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign affected by this risk or issue, supporting cross-functional impact assessment and campaign performance analysis.']
  escalated_from_risk_register_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_register (escalated_from_risk_register_id)']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project or campaign engagement to which this risk or issue belongs.']
  primary_risk_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual accountable for monitoring the risk or issue and executing the mitigation strategy, typically a project manager, account lead, or functional manager.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Risks are managed at SOW level. Agencies track SOW-specific risks for client reporting, mitigation planning, and assessing impact on contractual deliverables, timelines, and budget commitments.']
  work_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific work package or deliverable most directly affected by this risk or issue, enabling granular impact analysis and resource reallocation.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was successfully mitigated or closed, or the issue was resolved, enabling measurement of resolution cycle time and effectiveness.']
  client_communicated_date date [note: 'Date when the risk or issue was formally communicated to the client, tracking transparency and compliance with contractual reporting obligations.']
  client_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk or issue has been communicated to the client or should be included in client-facing status reports, supporting transparency and relationship management.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk or issue was formally closed, supporting cycle time analysis and resolution effectiveness measurement.']
  contingency_plan varchar [note: 'Fallback plan or alternative course of action to be executed if the risk materializes or the mitigation strategy proves insufficient.']
